b7a3fb3d5531.name: Why can I proactively request to cancel my Google Adsense account and receive the final payment?

acceptedAnswer.text: As long as your Google Adsense account reaches the minimum payout threshold, you can proactively apply to cancel the account, and Google will pay you the final earnings. This is because Google allows users to retrieve accumulated ad revenue when they stop operating, preventing funds from being locked in the account for a long time. The article mentions that the author previously had a final balance of US$88, which was retrieved this way.

b7a3fb3d5531.name: How should I start blogging on Wretch and learn web design?

acceptedAnswer.text: Wretch offers a free blogging platform with customizable styles, making it suitable for beginners. The author learned FrontPage 2003 in school computer classes and used Wretch's JavaScript plugins to start exploring how to find materials, embed images, and solve plugin issues. This hands-on experience helped him become more familiar with basic web design and laid the foundation for writing his first blog.

b7a3fb3d5531.name: Which free forum system is more suitable for beginners to set up their own forum?

acceptedAnswer.text: The author tried multiple free forum systems and ultimately recommends Marlito because it is feature-complete and meets the needs. Marlito offers rich settings such as board creation, member permissions, and a plugin center, making it suitable for users who want to be forum admins and need high flexibility. This allowed the author to manage and maintain his forum long-term.

b7a3fb3d5531.name: Why did the author later give up using the Blogger platform to write technical articles?

acceptedAnswer.text: The author felt that Blogger's basic features could not meet his needs and the user experience was poor, leading him to give up after writing a few articles. This is because Blogger's management and formatting for technical articles were not as expected, lacking flexibility and making it hard to focus on content creation.

b7a3fb3d5531.name: What challenges will you face when setting up your own blog with WordPress?

acceptedAnswer.text: WordPress requires you to apply for a domain and purchase hosting yourself, and you must personally handle all feature settings and adjustments, which consumes a lot of time and effort. As a result, the author couldn't focus on writing content, leading to sporadic updates, and eventually the hosting expired without renewal, causing the site to go offline. This shows that self-hosting requires technical skills and time investment.

b7a3fb3d5531.name: How to shift from playing with blog features to focusing on content creation?

acceptedAnswer.text: The author was initially very interested in blog features, playing with styles and plugins, but gradually focused on the essence of article content, sharing technology and experiences. This shift stemmed from a passion for writing and a desire to share with others, trying to gain motivation from ad revenue, and ultimately returning to mutual learning through teaching and life documentation.

b7a3fb3d5531.name: What topics does the author plan to share when restarting blogging?

acceptedAnswer.text: The author plans to share four main areas: technical (iOS app development, Swift, PHP, MySQL), lifestyle (work, photography, unboxing), experience (starting machine learning from scratch), and stories (skill competition experiences, life observations), hoping to continuously document life through mutual teaching and learning.

e37d66ea1146.name: How to solve memory crash issues when inserting images into UITextView?

acceptedAnswer.text: Directly inserting multiple images into UITextView causes all images to be loaded into memory because NSTextAttachment lacks a reuse mechanism, resulting in lag or crashes. The solution is to use XLYTextKitExtension, an extension based on TextKit, which enables NSTextAttachment to support custom UIViews and reuse, reducing memory load. For example, inserting more than 5 images on an iPhone 6 causes lag, but with XLYTextKitExtension it runs smoothly.

e37d66ea1146.name: How to insert multiple images at the cursor position in UITextView?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, get the UITextView's selectedRange.location as the insertion point, then use NSMutableAttributedString to insert the image's NSTextAttachment at that position. Specifically, copy the current attributedText, insert NSAttributedString(attachment:) at the location, and then write it back to the UITextView. This ensures images are inserted where the cursor is.

e37d66ea1146.name: How to tag images in UITextView's NSTextAttachment for replacement or deletion if upload fails?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can subclass NSTextAttachment and add a custom property like uuid to tag each image. Generate a unique UUID during upload and assign it to the attachment. If upload fails, traverse the attributedText to find the attachment with the matching uuid, then replace it with an error placeholder image or delete it directly. This allows handling failures for specific images.

e37d66ea1146.name: Why is it difficult to handle end-of-line newlines and deleting lines when using UITableView combined with UITextView for a text wrapping editor?

acceptedAnswer.text: UITableView uses one cell per line, each being a UITextView or UIImageView. While this effectively reuses cells and reduces memory, controlling the behavior of pressing Return at the end of a line to create and jump to a new line, or pressing Backspace at the start of a line to jump to the previous line or delete empty lines, is complex and difficult to implement. Due to this complexity and logic challenges, the author abandoned this approach.

e37d66ea1146.name: How to search and replace specific image tags in UITextView's textStorage using regular expressions?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use NSRegularExpression to define a matching pattern, for example "(\[\[image_id=){1}([0-9]+){1}(\]\]){1}", and search textStorage.string. Use a while loop to find all matches one by one to avoid range errors and crashes after replacements. Once found, get the range to perform replacements or other operations.

e37d66ea1146.name: How to insert a custom UIView NSTextAttachment into UITextView in Swift?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use XLYTextKitExtension's XLYTextAttachment, initializing it with a closure that returns a UIView. Set the attachment's id and bounds, then append the attachment to an NSMutableAttributedString, and finally insert it into the UITextView's textStorage. This allows inserting interactive custom UIViews inside UITextView.

e37d66ea1146.name: Why resize images with Kingfisher's resizeTo before uploading?

acceptedAnswer.text: Original image files that are too large cause slow uploads and insertions, consuming a lot of resources. Using Kingfisher's resizeTo to reduce the image to a specified maximum width (e.g., 1500 px) decreases memory usage and network load, improving upload speed and user experience.

cb6eba52a342.name: What is the time limit for processing in an iOS Notification Service Extension? What happens if it exceeds the limit?

acceptedAnswer.text: The processing time limit for a Notification Service Extension is about 30 seconds. If the callback is not called within 30 seconds, the system will stop the extension and display the original push notification content directly. This is to avoid poor user experience caused by push notification delays. For example, if image downloading times out, the system will abandon processing and show the unmodified notification.

cb6eba52a342.name: How to enable image display support in iOS push notifications?

acceptedAnswer.text: To support image push notifications, the backend push payload must include "mutable-content":1. Then, in the Notification Service Extension's didReceive method, download the image, create a UNNotificationAttachment, and attach it to the notification content. This way, the push notification can display images, for example, by showing the downloaded image as an attachment in the notification.

cb6eba52a342.name: Why add "mutable-content":1 in the push payload?

acceptedAnswer.text: Adding "mutable-content":1 tells the system that the push notification will trigger the Notification Service Extension, allowing the app to modify the content or attach images before the notification is displayed. Without this field, the system will not activate the extension, so image downloading or content processing cannot be performed.

cb6eba52a342.name: Can the Notification Service Extension decide whether to display the push notification?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, the Notification Service Extension cannot prevent the push notification from being displayed. Even if you want to cancel the notification, you can only modify its content, but the notification will still appear on the user's device. The system design does not allow the extension to block the notification from showing.

cb6eba52a342.name: How to track if a push notification is displayed within the Notification Service Extension?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the didReceive method, retrieve custom push fields such as push_id, and use URLSession to create an asynchronous POST request to the backend statistics API to report that the notification has been displayed. This allows the backend to record the push notification display status, for example, by sending a display event with the push_id.

cb6eba52a342.name: What is the difference between didReceive and serviceExtensionTimeWillExpire in Notification Service Extension?

acceptedAnswer.text: didReceive is triggered when the push notification is received and is used to process the notification content and call the completion handler after modification. serviceExtensionTimeWillExpire is called when the processing time limit is about to expire; if processing is not finished, you can perform cleanup here and call the completion handler promptly to avoid the system abandoning the modification.

cb6eba52a342.name: How to add a Notification Service Extension target in Xcode?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Xcode, select File → New → Target, choose iOS Notification Service Extension, enter the product name, and complete the creation. Finally, click Activate to enable it. This creates a dedicated extension target for handling push notification content.

9a9aa892f9a9.name: How does the Vision framework help iOS apps automatically detect faces and crop profile pictures?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Vision framework uses VNDetectFaceRectanglesRequest to detect face locations in photos and converts the face coordinates to the ImageView size. Then, based on the center point of the face area, Kingfisher is used to crop a fixed-size image, preventing the face from being cut off. This allows automatic generation of face-centered profile pictures, such as a 300x300 pixel cropped image.

9a9aa892f9a9.name: Why is coordinate system conversion necessary when using Vision to detect faces?

acceptedAnswer.text: Vision detection results are expressed in the coordinate system of the original image size, which differs from the coordinate system displayed in the ImageView. To correctly map the bounding box or crop area to the image, the result coordinates must be scaled and flipped vertically according to the ImageView size. This ensures that the marked face position is accurately displayed on the interface.

9a9aa892f9a9.name: How to solve the issue of blank spaces or distortion when placing images in an ImageView?

acceptedAnswer.text: The article uses Kingfisher to proportionally scale the image according to the screen width first, then sets imageView.contentMode to .redraw and adjusts the ImageView's Constraints height so that the ImageView size matches the image. This avoids image distortion or blank spaces caused by directly using fill or fit modes.

9a9aa892f9a9.name: What types of detection functions does the Vision framework support?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Vision framework supports text detection, face detection, image matching, QR code detection, and object tracking. The article mainly uses face detection to solve the app's profile picture cropping issue by using VNDetectFaceRectanglesRequest to obtain face regions and achieve automatic cropping.

9a9aa892f9a9.name: Why should Vision face recognition be executed on a background thread?

acceptedAnswer.text: Face recognition is a computationally intensive task; running it on the main thread can cause interface lag. The article demonstrates using DispatchQueue.global().async to perform recognition on a background thread, then returning to the main thread to update the UI, ensuring the app interface remains smooth and responsive.

9a9aa892f9a9.name: From which iOS version does the Vision framework support face detection?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Vision framework's face detection feature is supported starting from iOS 11. The code examples in the article check if the version is >= iOS 11 to ensure the execution environment supports Vision-related APIs; otherwise, a not supported message is printed.

9a9aa892f9a9.name: Can the Vision framework mark the entire head including hair when detecting faces?

acceptedAnswer.text: The built-in face detection in the Vision framework can only mark the face area and does not include the entire head or hair. The article mentions that the demo app can mark face positions but cannot mark the entire head area, which is a current limitation of the API.

793bf2cdda0f.name: Does CoreML 2.0 in iOS 12 support training machine learning models by myself?

acceptedAnswer.text: CoreML 2.0 supports custom models, meaning you can train models yourself. You can use third-party tools like TextClassiferPlayground to prepare data and train text classification models, then directly import them into your iOS project. This lowers the entry barrier for machine learning and makes implementation quite easy.

793bf2cdda0f.name: How to prepare data for training text classification with CoreML?

acceptedAnswer.text: Text classification data needs to include input text and corresponding classification labels, usually in JSON or CSV format. The article demonstrates exporting data from MySQL using Phpmyadmin and cleaning it into a JSON array containing only text and labels. This structure makes it easy for training tools to read and perform machine learning.

793bf2cdda0f.name: How to perform word segmentation when training a text classification model with CoreML?

acceptedAnswer.text: Before training, the text content is segmented into words, splitting sentences into terms. For example, "我想知道婚禮需要準備什麼" becomes "我想,知道,婚禮,需要,準備,什麼". Word segmentation helps the model analyze semantics more accurately and improves classification performance. On iOS, NSLinguisticTagger can be used for segmentation.

793bf2cdda0f.name: How to import a trained CoreML model into an iOS project for use?

acceptedAnswer.text: Once the CoreML model file (.mlmodel) is trained, simply drag and drop it into your Xcode project to use it. Then, in Swift, call the prediction method through the model class, input text, and get classification results. The whole process is simple and can be directly integrated into your app.

793bf2cdda0f.name: Why is the prediction accuracy only about 35% for my self-trained CoreML text classification model?

acceptedAnswer.text: Low prediction accuracy may be due to too little training data. The article uses about 100 data points, which is experimental. Insufficient data causes the model to fail to learn enough features. It is recommended to increase the training data volume and improve the quality of classification labels to enhance accuracy.

793bf2cdda0f.name: Can CoreML train models directly on iOS devices?

acceptedAnswer.text: Currently, CoreML does not support training models directly on iOS devices. Model training still needs to be done on MacOS using tools, then the trained model is imported into the iOS app for prediction. This is a limitation of CoreML at this stage.

793bf2cdda0f.name: Are there convenient tools to help me train CoreML text classification models?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apple officially provides GUI training tools only for image classification. For text classification, you need to write your own code or use third-party tools. The article recommends TextClassiferPlayground on GitHub, which offers a GUI interface to easily load JSON data and train text classification models, making the training process more intuitive.

1ca246e27273.name: Can 3D Touch still be used on iPhone 11 and later models?

acceptedAnswer.text: 3D Touch has been removed on iPhone 11 and later models, replaced by Haptic Touch with a different implementation. This is an official design change by Apple, meaning new models do not support the original 3D Touch pressure-sensitive interactions.

1ca246e27273.name: How to implement 3D Touch Preview ViewController preview feature in an iOS app?

acceptedAnswer.text: To implement a Preview ViewController, you need to register the preview on a 3D Touch supported list view (such as UITableView or UICollectionView) and implement UIViewControllerPreviewingDelegate. When the user presses firmly, the preview window pops up and can be swiped up to show a menu. The code should be placed in traitCollectionDidChange to avoid failure.

1ca246e27273.name: Why should the 3D Touch preview registration be placed in traitCollectionDidChange instead of viewDidLoad?

acceptedAnswer.text: Registering the 3D Touch preview in traitCollectionDidChange is because this method is called when the traitCollection updates, ensuring forceTouchCapability is correctly determined. Placing it in viewDidLoad may cause occasional failures or some cells not supporting it, as traitCollection might not be ready yet.

1ca246e27273.name: How to distinguish between 3D Touch preview window and full window states?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can implement a Protocol in the target ViewController declaring an is3DTouchPreview variable, set to true during preview and false for the full window. In viewDidLoad, you can adjust the display based on this value, such as hiding the toolbar during preview and showing it normally in full mode.

1ca246e27273.name: How to add 3D Touch app shortcut launch feature in an iOS app?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, add UIApplicationShortcutItems in info.plist, setting the shortcut type, title, subtitle, and icon. Then implement application(_:performActionFor:completionHandler:) in AppDelegate to perform corresponding actions based on the shortcut identifier. This allows users to long-press the app icon to quickly launch specific features.

1ca246e27273.name: Can the 3D Touch Preview ViewController include an options menu in the preview window?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, override the previewActionItems property in the target ViewController to return an array of UIPreviewAction. Users can swipe up in the preview window to see the menu and tap to execute corresponding actions. If no menu is needed, return an empty array.

1ca246e27273.name: Is the 3D Touch app shortcut launch feature widely used?

acceptedAnswer.text: According to article data, the usage rate of the 3D Touch app shortcut launch feature is low and rarely used. Compared to the Preview ViewController preview feature, the shortcut launch feature has fewer applications, so developers can decide whether to include it based on needs.

a4bc3bce7513.name: Why can't MAC Address be used as UUID to identify devices in iOS?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apple prohibits using MAC Address as UUID due to privacy concerns, to prevent malicious tracking of users. This restriction forces developers to seek alternative solutions, such as using IDFV to identify devices. MAC Address has been banned, meaning it is no longer allowed to be accessed or used on iOS.

a4bc3bce7513.name: How does iOS identifierForVendor (IDFV) determine that apps from the same vendor share the same UUID?

acceptedAnswer.text: IDFV determines whether apps belong to the same vendor based on the prefix of the app's Bundle ID. Apps with the same prefix on the same device share the same UUID. For example, com.518.work and com.518.job will receive the same UUID because they belong to the same vendor.

a4bc3bce7513.name: Why does deleting all apps from the same vendor and reinstalling cause IDFV to generate a new UUID?

acceptedAnswer.text: When all apps from the same vendor are deleted, the system resets the IDFV. The next installation will generate a new UUID. This is because IDFV is a unique identifier generated for the vendor's app collection, and when no apps exist, the identifier becomes invalid.

a4bc3bce7513.name: How does Key-Chain help solve the issue of UUID changes caused by deleting and reinstalling apps affecting IDFV?

acceptedAnswer.text: Key-Chain can be used to permanently store the UUID. Deleting the app does not clear Key-Chain data, so the same UUID can be read after reinstalling. The approach is to first try reading the UUID from Key-Chain; if none exists, use IDFV and then write it into Key-Chain to ensure the UUID does not change due to reinstallation.

a4bc3bce7513.name: How to read and write UUID from Key-Chain in Swift code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use SecItemCopyMatching with kSecClassGenericPassword and an account identifier to read. To write, first delete the existing item then add the new data. For example: read by querying "DEVICE_UUID" with SecItemCopyMatching; write by first calling SecItemDelete, then SecItemAdd with the new data. This ensures the device's unique identifier is stored in the Key-Chain.

a4bc3bce7513.name: What are the alternatives to iOS UUID besides identifierForVendor?

acceptedAnswer.text: Besides IDFV, there are fingerprinting techniques (such as Canvas, User-Agent), using UIPasteboard to share data between apps, and checking other app installations via canOpenURL. However, MAC Address is banned, and IDFV remains the mainstream and officially recommended solution.

a4bc3bce7513.name: Why is the use of iOS UUID questioned for privacy risks?

acceptedAnswer.text: Because UUIDs can be used to link user behavior across multiple apps, inferring user characteristics and whereabouts. For example, if a user installs travel, transportation, and baby care apps simultaneously, their lifestyle habits can be deduced. Such tracking may infringe on privacy, which is why Apple restricts certain UUID usage permissions.

ade9e745a4bf.name: How to send push notifications on iOS 12 without triggering the authorization prompt?

acceptedAnswer.text: iOS 12 introduced the .provisional authorization option in UserNotifications, allowing apps to obtain silent notification permission without showing the authorization prompt. Developers just need to include the .provisional option when calling requestAuthorization, for example, calling requestAuthorization(options: [.badge, .alert, .sound, .provisional]) in the AppDelegate to silently obtain silent notification permission.

ade9e745a4bf.name: How are silent notifications displayed on iOS 12?

acceptedAnswer.text: Silent notifications do not show banners, play sounds, or appear on the lock screen when the device is in the background. They only appear in the Notification Center after unlocking the device and pulling down, where they are automatically grouped into categories. Users can tap to expand and choose whether to upgrade to full notification permission or turn off notifications. This design avoids disturbing users while still exposing messages.

ade9e745a4bf.name: How to determine if the current push notification permission is provisional?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can use UNUserNotificationCenter's getNotificationSettings method to check if settings.authorizationStatus equals .provisional (iOS 12 and above). For example: if settings.authorizationStatus == .provisional means the current notification permission is provisional, and the app can adjust its push notification strategy accordingly.

ade9e745a4bf.name: What is the difference between iOS 12 provisional notification permission and the notDetermined status?

acceptedAnswer.text: notDetermined means the user has not yet been asked for notification permission, while provisional means silent notification permission has been granted but full authorization has not. Both statuses allow the app to later present the official authorization prompt for the user to choose whether to enable full notification permission, enabling developers to gradually increase user acceptance.

ade9e745a4bf.name: How to manually set an installed app to silent notifications?

acceptedAnswer.text: Users can go to Settings > Notifications > select the app, turn off all notification permissions except for "Notification Center" permission, which results in silent notifications. Alternatively, when receiving a notification, users can long press to expand it, tap the "…" in the top right corner, and select "Deliver Quietly." This way, notifications only appear in the Notification Center without banners or sounds.

ade9e745a4bf.name: After obtaining iOS 12 silent notification permission, how to trigger the official authorization prompt again?

acceptedAnswer.text: When calling requestAuthorization, remove the .provisional option and only request [.badge, .alert, .sound]. This will trigger the official authorization prompt. If the user taps "Allow," full notification permission is granted; if "Don't Allow" is tapped, all notification permissions, including the original silent notification permission, are revoked.

ade9e745a4bf.name: What are the benefits of iOS 12 silent notifications for developers and users?

acceptedAnswer.text: For developers, silent notifications prevent disturbing users initially, increasing push notification click rates and encouraging subsequent full authorization. For users, it avoids being forced to choose authorization immediately after installing the app, reducing rejection rates and allowing users to experience notification content before deciding to enable full notifications. This balances the needs of both parties.

fd7f92d52baa.name: How to check push notification permission status from iOS 9 to iOS 12?

acceptedAnswer.text: For iOS 10 and above, use UNUserNotificationCenter.getNotificationSettings to check authorization status. For iOS 9, use UIApplication.currentUserNotificationSettings combined with a custom UserDefaults flag to mark whether the user has been asked. This allows distinguishing between not asked, allowed, and denied states. The example uses Observable<NotificationStatusType?> to store the status and update it in real time.

fd7f92d52baa.name: How to tell if the user has never been asked or has denied push notifications on iOS 9?

acceptedAnswer.text: iOS 9 cannot directly distinguish between never asked and denied because the permission is blank in both cases. The solution is to use a custom UserDefaults flag iOS9NotificationIsDetermined in the didRegisterUserNotificationSettings callback, which triggers whether the user allows or denies, thus determining if the user has been asked.

fd7f92d52baa.name: How to implement push notification permission requests from iOS 9 to iOS 12?

acceptedAnswer.text: For iOS 10 and above, use UNUserNotificationCenter.requestAuthorization to request permission. For iOS 9, use UIApplication.registerUserNotificationSettings and update the status in the didRegisterUserNotificationSettings callback. This approach supports the permission request flow across different iOS versions.

fd7f92d52baa.name: How to prompt users and guide them to the settings page when push notification permission is denied?

acceptedAnswer.text: When the permission status is denied, present a UIAlertController to inform the user and provide a "Go to Settings" button. Use UIApplication.shared.openURL with UIApplicationOpenSettingsURLString + bundleID to open the app's settings page so the user can enable notifications. Note that using Private APIs for redirection is prohibited and will cause app rejection.

fd7f92d52baa.name: How to make the app automatically update push notification permission status when returning from background to foreground?

acceptedAnswer.text: Call the permission check method checkNotificationPermissionStatus inside applicationDidBecomeActive in appDelegate to ensure the app rechecks notification permission status every time it comes back from the background and updates the notificationStatus object. This allows the UI to reflect permission changes immediately.

fd7f92d52baa.name: How does Observable help dynamically monitor push notification permission status?

acceptedAnswer.text: Implement notificationStatus as an Observable so other pages can subscribe to afterChange events and automatically receive notifications when the permission status changes. This allows adding listeners in viewDidLoad to dynamically handle users enabling or disabling notifications, improving user experience.

fd7f92d52baa.name: Why can't UIApplication.shared.openURL("App-Prefs:root=...") be used to jump to the settings page?

acceptedAnswer.text: This type of redirection uses a Private API, and Apple will reject your app for it. The correct approach is to use the public API UIApplicationOpenSettingsURLString + bundleID to open the settings page, ensuring your app will not be rejected during review.

8d863bcd1c55.name: What challenges do backend engineers face when switching to iOS app development?

acceptedAnswer.text: Backend engineers switching to iOS app development encounter challenges such as differences in language habits, a different development environment, and UI design barriers. Swift is a more modern and strongly typed language, which can feel unfamiliar at first. Xcode requires testing on simulators or real devices, unlike PHP which allows quick refreshes. Storyboard lowers the entry barrier, but memory management and page structures like UITableView implementations take time to adapt to. The author experienced two major obstacles and gradually got the hang of it after about a month of adjustment.

8d863bcd1c55.name: Why is it important to seize opportunities in the information technology field?

acceptedAnswer.text: Seizing opportunities in the IT field brings significant growth and chances. The author once participated in a skills competition training, which was tough but taught multiple technologies like PHP, MySQL, and Photoshop, and helped him secure admission to Taiwan Tech University through a competition championship. This experience became a crucial turning point for entering the IT field, proving that actively engaging when opportunities arise can lead to long-term career benefits.

8d863bcd1c55.name: What are the differences between iOS app development and web development?

acceptedAnswer.text: There are major differences between iOS app development and web development. The former requires using Xcode and Swift, and testing via simulators or physical devices, unlike web development which allows instant refreshes in browsers. UI design often uses Storyboard visual tools, while web uses HTML/CSS. Memory management is stricter; apps must avoid leaks and optimize performance. Additionally, app publishing requires review, and bug fixes are not as quick as on the web. The author felt these differences when switching from backend to iOS.

8d863bcd1c55.name: How to overcome the bottleneck period in iOS app development?

acceptedAnswer.text: Overcoming the bottleneck period in iOS app development requires persistence and time to gain experience. The author faced an initial bottleneck of about a month, and during a second one, he broke through by continuous learning and growing from mistakes. If time is limited, it’s recommended to take beginner courses or find a mentor. Diligently studying Swift, the Xcode environment, and UI design while gradually accumulating experience is key to seeing success.

8d863bcd1c55.name: Why is fixing bugs after iOS app release more troublesome?

acceptedAnswer.text: Fixing bugs after an iOS app release is more troublesome because each version must go through Apple's review process, and downgrading to a previous version is not possible. Bug fixes usually take more than a day, and errors directly affect user experience. This differs from web development where fixes can be made instantly, requiring developers to test more carefully before release to avoid publishing versions with major bugs.

8d863bcd1c55.name: Why does the author believe that passion for exploring new things is important?

acceptedAnswer.text: The author believes that passion for exploring new things is key to continuously breaking through oneself. Historical figures like Columbus, the Wright brothers, and Armstrong achieved greatness because of their passion. Even small steps can become giant leaps for humanity. Maintaining passion encourages courage to try and grow. The author himself succeeded in switching to iOS app development and opened a new chapter in his career because of his love for learning.

8d863bcd1c55.name: Why should backend engineers consider the future when deciding to switch to iOS development?

acceptedAnswer.text: Considering the future when making decisions helps avoid missing opportunities due to inertia or cost concerns. Initially, the author wanted to continue backend work because he didn’t have a Mac and iOS development was new to him, but his supervisor encouraged him to be brave and try. After entering iOS development, he learned Swift and app publishing, opening new doors in his career. This shows that a long-term vision allows you to seize important growth opportunities.

f644db1bb8bf.name: How to add a shortcut to the app's notification settings page in iOS 12 settings?

acceptedAnswer.text: Starting from iOS 12, by including the .providesAppNotificationSettings option when requesting push notification permission, a "Set in App" shortcut will appear on the system settings notification page. This allows users to directly jump to the app's internal notification settings page instead of only being able to turn off notifications at the system level.

f644db1bb8bf.name: How to implement the feature that shows the "Set in App" option on the notification settings page?

acceptedAnswer.text: Implementation involves two steps: First, add the .providesAppNotificationSettings option when requesting notification authorization; second, have the AppDelegate conform to UNUserNotificationCenterDelegate and implement the userNotificationCenter(_:openSettingsFor:) method, where you write the code to navigate to the app's internal notification settings page.

f644db1bb8bf.name: Why allow users to jump from system notification settings to the app's internal notification settings?

acceptedAnswer.text: This prevents users from turning off all notifications at the system level, which would stop developers from sending any notifications. Through in-app settings, users can specify which types of notifications they want to receive, improving user experience and notification accuracy—for example, turning off certain column notifications while keeping important system messages.

f644db1bb8bf.name: Under what circumstances does the iOS 12 notification settings page shortcut appear?

acceptedAnswer.text: As long as the app requests notification permission including .providesAppNotificationSettings, and the user either allows or denies notifications, the "Set in App" option will appear on the system settings notification page. This option also appears when using 3D Touch to disable notifications, making it convenient for users to directly jump into the app to adjust settings.

f644db1bb8bf.name: How to implement navigation to the app's internal notification settings page in AppDelegate?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AppDelegate must conform to UNUserNotificationCenterDelegate and implement the userNotificationCenter(_:openSettingsFor:) method. Inside this method, write the code to navigate to the app's internal notification settings page, for example, by presenting the settings page using the rootViewController. This method is the callback triggered when the system calls after the user taps "Set in App."

f644db1bb8bf.name: What is the role of the new .providesAppNotificationSettings option added in iOS 12?

acceptedAnswer.text: The .providesAppNotificationSettings option lets the app inform the system that it has its own notification settings page. The system will then display a "Set in App" shortcut on the "Settings" -> "Notifications" page and in the notification's 3D Touch options, making it easier for users to directly adjust notification preferences within the app.

f644db1bb8bf.name: What are the benefits of demonstrating this feature using the Wedding Day app?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Wedding Day app uses this feature to allow users to individually turn off column notifications while still retaining important system messages, preventing all notifications from being disabled and missing critical information. This enhances user experience and allows developers to finely manage push content.

a2920e33e73e.name: How to choose the right Apple Watch Series 4 case size for your wrist?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apple Watch Series 4 comes in two case sizes: 40mm and 44mm. The choice should be based on your wrist size. A case that's too large may feel uncomfortable and affect heart rate detection accuracy, while a case that's too small may look odd. It's recommended to try them on in-store or use a daily disposable contact lens case (about 44mm) as a reference. Apple Watch Series 3 and 4 bands are interchangeable, with 38mm fitting 40mm and 42mm fitting 44mm.

a2920e33e73e.name: What’s the difference between the aluminum and stainless steel cases of Apple Watch Series 4?

acceptedAnswer.text: The aluminum case paired with a glass surface is lighter and more affordable but the glass is prone to scratches. The stainless steel case with sapphire crystal glass is heavier but more scratch-resistant and can be polished, making it more suitable for business use and pairing with leather or metal bands. If budget allows, the stainless steel version is recommended for its durable sapphire glass and consistent premium feel.

a2920e33e73e.name: Should I buy the cellular version of Apple Watch Series 4?

acceptedAnswer.text: The cellular version lets you make calls and receive messages without your phone while running or out, but requires an additional monthly plan costing 199 TWD, and your phone must be powered on for it to work. If you rarely leave home without your phone and have a limited budget, the WiFi version is more cost-effective. Spotify currently does not support offline playback, so you still need your phone to listen to music during workouts.

a2920e33e73e.name: How to take a screenshot on Apple Watch Series 4?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, enable the "Enable Screenshots" feature in the Watch app on your iPhone. Then, on your Apple Watch, press the Digital Crown and the side button simultaneously. The screen will flash to indicate the screenshot was taken successfully. Screenshots are automatically saved to the Photos app on your iPhone.

a2920e33e73e.name: What is the battery life of Apple Watch Series 4?

acceptedAnswer.text: With normal use, it lasts about 15 hours, with around 65% battery remaining if not used continuously. If you don’t wear it while sleeping, don’t use it overnight, and switch to airplane mode, the battery can maintain about 98%. Overall, the battery life is sufficient for a full day’s use and can barely last two days on a single charge, though the first charge may take longer.

a2920e33e73e.name: How to set a more complex passcode on Apple Watch Series 4?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the Watch app on your iPhone, go to the "My Watch" tab, tap "Passcode," then turn off "Simple Passcode." This allows you to set a 6-digit complex passcode to enhance security.

a2920e33e73e.name: How to use Apple Watch Series 4 to unlock a Mac computer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Make sure your iPhone, Apple Watch, and Mac are signed in with the same Apple ID and have two-factor authentication enabled. In your Mac’s System Preferences, check "Allow Apple Watch to unlock your Mac." If it doesn’t work initially, try restarting your Mac. This lets you unlock your Mac without entering a password when you’re near it.

a2920e33e73e.name: Which map services does Apple Watch Series 4 support for navigation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apple Watch Series 4 only supports Apple Maps natively; Google Maps is not supported at this time. During navigation, Apple Maps on your iPhone syncs information to the watch and provides vibration alerts at turns. Apple Maps has fewer landmarks and less convenient search in Taiwan, so it’s recommended to use Siri Shortcuts with Google Maps for better results.

e85d77b05061.name: Why can't Apple Watch Apps be installed independently and must be attached to an iOS App?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apple Watch Apps must be attached to an iOS App because they are essentially extensions of the iOS App and cannot run independently. This design ensures the watch serves as an auxiliary device to the iPhone. watchOS 5 and later versions follow this architecture. For example, when creating a new watchOS Target, both a WatchKit App and a WatchKit Extension are generated as essential components.

e85d77b05061.name: What are the differences between watchOS App UI layout and iOS UIKit?

acceptedAnswer.text: watchOS UI layout does not use iOS UIKit's Auto Layout and constraints. Instead, it uses Group components to compose layouts, similar to UIStackView but with more parameter settings like Layout, Insets, Spacing, Radius, etc. This makes layout simpler and more powerful, for example, controlling view arrangement by stacking horizontally or vertically, and directly setting corner radius and spacing.

e85d77b05061.name: How to implement a Table with multiple Row styles in an Apple Watch App?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the WKInterfaceTable of an Apple Watch App, you can add multiple Rows and assign different Identifiers to each. In code, use setRowTypes() to specify multiple Row types, then use rowController(at:) to get the corresponding Row and set data as needed. This allows displaying multiple different row styles within the same Table, such as one showing data and another showing a no-data prompt.

e85d77b05061.name: How does an Apple Watch App sync data with an iPhone App?

acceptedAnswer.text: Data synchronization between Apple Watch App and iPhone App requires using the WatchConnectivity framework because App Groups sharing UserDefaults is no longer supported since watchOS 2. Both sides must implement WCSessionDelegate and use methods like sendMessage and transferUserInfo to send data. For example, the phone side calls WatchSessionManager.sharedManager.startSession() at launch to establish the connection.

e85d77b05061.name: How to test watchOS push notifications and implement custom interfaces?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can use the PushNotificationPayload.apns file in the project to simulate push notifications after deploying the Watch App on the simulator. To customize the push interface, check "Has Dynamic Interface" in Interface.storyboard and design the Dynamic Interface. Override the NotificationController's didReceive(_:) method in code to set the title, content, and buttons. Starting from watchOS 5, you can also dynamically add reply buttons.

e85d77b05061.name: How to pass parameters when navigating pages in an Apple Watch App?

acceptedAnswer.text: When navigating pages, you can pass parameters using pushController(withName:context:) by placing data into the context parameter. The receiving page retrieves and unwraps the parameters in awake(withContext:). For example, to navigate from page A to page B with an id:Int, page A calls pushController(withName: "showDetail", context: 100), and page B parses it with guard let in awake(withContext:). This is simpler than using delegates or segues in iOS.

e85d77b05061.name: How to implement menu functionality in an Apple Watch App?

acceptedAnswer.text: Menu functionality can be added by dragging a Menu from the component library, then adding Menu Items and linking IBAction to the code. Users can display the menu items by Force Touching the page. For example, adding delete or reply functions is simple and aligns with Apple Watch interaction design.

6012b7b4f612.name: Why does the tintColor turn gray when presenting UIAlertController in iOS?

acceptedAnswer.text: When presenting a UIAlertController, the system automatically changes the tintAdjustmentMode of the root view controller's view on the current page to Dimmed, causing the tintColor to become a desaturated dull gray. This is a system design mechanism, not a bug.

6012b7b4f612.name: What are the three settings for the tintAdjustmentMode property?

acceptedAnswer.text: tintAdjustmentMode has three enum settings: .Automatic (inherits the parent view's setting), .Normal (default, displays the tintColor normally), and .Dimmed (makes the tintColor appear dull gray). These three modes control the display effect of the tintColor.

6012b7b4f612.name: How to keep UIButton's original tintColor when presenting UIAlertController?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can force the tintAdjustmentMode to .Normal inside the UIButton's tintColorDidChange event to prevent the system from automatically switching it to Dimmed, thus preserving the original tintColor. Example: override func tintColorDidChange() { self.tintAdjustmentMode = .normal }.

6012b7b4f612.name: How does the .Automatic mode of tintAdjustmentMode work?

acceptedAnswer.text: The .Automatic mode makes the view's tintAdjustmentMode follow the setting of its parent view, so the subview automatically inherits the tintColor display mode of the parent view. This helps maintain overall UI consistency.

6012b7b4f612.name: Is the tintColor display issue caused by presenting UIAlertController a system bug?

acceptedAnswer.text: This is not a system bug but an intended design mechanism of iOS. When a UIAlertController is presented, the system sets the tintAdjustmentMode of the underlying view to Dimmed, causing the tintColor to appear gray. This is expected behavior.

6012b7b4f612.name: Should the tintColor gray-out issue caused by tintAdjustmentMode be fixed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not fixing it is generally fine, though visually it might be bothersome. If you want the tintColor to always remain its original color, you can override tintColorDidChange in a UIView subclass and force tintAdjustmentMode to .Normal. This will resolve the issue.

ac557047d206.name: How to implement caller identification using Call Directory Extension on iOS?

acceptedAnswer.text: iOS allows developers to maintain a local phone identification database through Call Directory Extension, enabling labeling of incoming calls, outgoing calls, and call history. Developers need to create an Extension that stores phone numbers and corresponding names in a local JSON file, which the Extension then reads and writes into the system identification database. Users must manually enable this feature, and it requires iOS 10 or later.

ac557047d206.name: Why can Call Directory Extension only use an offline database for phone identification?

acceptedAnswer.text: Call Directory Extension can only use a pre-written local offline database and cannot perform real-time API calls to query incoming call information. This is an iOS limitation on Extensions to prevent excessive system resource usage and privacy risks. For example, Whoscall regularly pushes notifications to remind users to update the local database to maintain identification accuracy.

ac557047d206.name: How to determine if the user's device supports and has enabled the Call Directory Extension feature?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can use the method CXCallDirectoryManager.sharedInstance.getEnabledStatusForExtension(withIdentifier:completionHandler:) to check whether a specified Extension is enabled. The returned status includes enabled, disabled, and unknown (not supported). This helps the app determine if caller identification functionality can be used.

ac557047d206.name: How to convert phone number formats for correct recognition by Call Directory Extension?

acceptedAnswer.text: Phone numbers must be converted into a pure numeric string. For Taiwanese numbers, the leading 0 should be replaced with the country code 886; for example, 0255667788 becomes 886255667788. Landline extensions are appended directly after the number without any symbols; for example, 0255667788,0718 becomes 8862556677880718. Sample code uses regular expressions to replace strings to meet format requirements.

ac557047d206.name: How to maintain and update the identification data for Call Directory Extension?

acceptedAnswer.text: A local JSON file mapping phone numbers to names must be shared between the app and the Extension. After the app updates this JSON, it calls CXCallDirectoryManager.reloadExtension to have the Extension reload the data. The Extension reads the JSON in the beginRequest method and sequentially adds entries to the identification database.

ac557047d206.name: Why does Call Directory Extension need to delete old data before adding new data?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Extension does not support directly overwriting data. If old data is not deleted before adding new entries, the reload will fail. Therefore, during incremental updates, you must first call removeIdentificationEntry to delete old numbers before adding new ones to ensure the database remains clean and loads successfully.

ac557047d206.name: What settings do users need to configure to use the caller identification feature with Call Directory Extension?

acceptedAnswer.text: Users must manually go to Settings -> Phone -> Call Blocking & Identification to enable caller identification permission for the specified app. The app cannot enable this feature automatically, and if not enabled, caller identification will not work. This is part of iOS's security and privacy design.

c5e7e580c341.name: Why can the iOS DeviceCheck API only store 2 bits of data?

acceptedAnswer.text: The DeviceCheck API can only store 2 bits of data because Apple provides limited cloud storage space per device, allowing developers to write only two bits of information. This limits the available state combinations to 4, suitable for marking simple device states such as whether it has been trialed, paid, or blocked. This design protects user privacy while providing basic device identification functionality.

c5e7e580c341.name: How does the iOS DeviceCheck API prevent users from repeatedly trying after resetting their phone?

acceptedAnswer.text: DeviceCheck uses Apple's cloud to mark the device with 2 bits of state information. Even if the user resets the phone or changes the iCloud account, these marks remain and are not cleared. This allows developers to determine whether the device has already used a one-time offer or trial, preventing users from repeatedly obtaining benefits by resetting the device. Tests have proven that the marks persist even after a factory reset.

c5e7e580c341.name: How does the iOS DeviceCheck API obtain a temporary device identification token on the App side?

acceptedAnswer.text: On the iOS App side, you can use the DCDevice.current.generateToken method provided by DeviceCheck to obtain a temporary identification token for the device. This token is base64-encoded data. After the App obtains it, it needs to send it to the backend server, which then communicates with Apple's servers to query or update the device status. This process is straightforward and officially supported on iOS 11 and above.

c5e7e580c341.name: How does the backend use the DeviceCheck API to update or query the device's 2 bits status?

acceptedAnswer.text: The backend first assembles a JWT that complies with Apple's specifications (using the ES256 algorithm, including Team ID, Key ID, timestamp, etc.) and signs it with the privateKey.p8. Then it sends a POST request with an Authorization header to Apple's DeviceCheck API, calling /v1/query_two_bits to query or /v1/update_two_bits to update the 2 bits status. A successful response returns a 200 status code, and querying returns bit0, bit1, and the last modification time.

c5e7e580c341.name: Which iOS versions does the iOS DeviceCheck API support?

acceptedAnswer.text: The DeviceCheck API supports iOS 11 and later versions. This is because the API was announced at WWDC 2017 and officially made available to developers starting with iOS 11. Devices running versions below iOS 11 cannot use this API for device identification and status marking.

c5e7e580c341.name: How to test DeviceCheck functionality in a Swift App without writing backend code?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can use the third-party package CupertinoJWT within a Swift App to directly assemble JWTs and call the DeviceCheck API, simulating the backend's query and update process. The example implements token generation, JWT signing, and sending HTTP requests to Apple's servers purely in Swift. This method is suitable for testing and demonstration but is not recommended for production environments due to the higher risk of private key exposure.

c5e7e580c341.name: What information must the JWT for the iOS DeviceCheck API include to comply with Apple’s specifications?

acceptedAnswer.text: The JWT must include a Header and Payload. The Header specifies the algorithm as ES256 and includes the Key ID; the Payload must include the Team ID (iss), issued-at time (iat), and expiration time (exp), with timestamps formatted as integer Unix Timestamps. After signing this information with the privateKey.p8, it can be used as an Authorization Bearer Token to call Apple's DeviceCheck API.

33afa0ae557d.name: How much faster is the wired version of AirPods 2 compared to the wireless version?

acceptedAnswer.text: The wired version of AirPods 2 charges faster and more steadily. The wireless version charges slower due to limitations of the charging case and charger, and it cannot quickly charge both the iPhone and Apple Watch at the same time. Users have tested that wireless charging accessories cannot effectively boost the watch's current, so they ultimately choose the wired version to ensure charging efficiency.

33afa0ae557d.name: How to quickly switch back to using AirPods 2 on an iPhone?

acceptedAnswer.text: Simply pull up the Control Center on your iPhone and tap the icon at the top right of the audio block to quickly switch back to using AirPods 2. This operation is convenient for quickly selecting audio output when switching between multiple devices, and you can also check the AirPods battery level within the same interface.

33afa0ae557d.name: Is the Hey Siri feature on AirPods 2 convenient to use in practice?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Hey Siri feature on AirPods 2 is very convenient, allowing you to wake Siri hands-free, which is ideal when your hands are busy. Users can directly issue voice commands to adjust volume or control playback, enhancing operational smoothness. For example, saying "Hey Siri, set volume to 75%" quickly adjusts the volume.

33afa0ae557d.name: Do AirPods 2 easily fall out when worn while cycling?

acceptedAnswer.text: When wearing AirPods 2 while cycling, they can be pressed against the ears by the helmet, causing the earbuds to fall out easily. Testing suggests wearing only one earbud for navigation while cycling to avoid the helmet affecting earbud stability during putting on and taking off. This approach is safer and less likely to cause the earbuds to fall.

33afa0ae557d.name: What is the difference in sound quality between AirPods 2 and EarPods?

acceptedAnswer.text: AirPods 2 have much better sound quality than EarPods, offering noticeable surround layers and a wider sound range. Although they lack noise cancellation and are not primarily focused on sound quality, their overall performance is still solid and suitable for daily use. However, they still fall short compared to headphones in the same price range that emphasize sound quality.

33afa0ae557d.name: How to distinguish genuine AirPods 2 from counterfeit versions?

acceptedAnswer.text: Genuine AirPods 2 show battery levels for the left ear, right ear, and charging case in the software, and Bluetooth settings allow adjustment of tap functions for each earbud. The charging case indicator light turns off once connected. Counterfeit versions usually show only one battery level, have limited Bluetooth settings, and the charging case indicator light stays on continuously. It is recommended to purchase from official or major retailers to avoid buying counterfeit products.

33afa0ae557d.name: Which protective case is recommended for AirPods 2 accessories?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Catalyst AirPods waterproof storage case is recommended. It features waterproof and drop-resistant functions, includes a hook, and is convenient to use. You can take out the earbuds and charge them without removing the case. Priced around 1000 TWD, it is suitable for users worried about dropping and damaging their AirPods.

c3150cdc85dd.name: How to set up Mi Home Desk Lamp Pro directly with Apple HomeKit?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mi Home Desk Lamp Pro supports Apple HomeKit. To set it up, open the "Home" app on your iPhone, tap the "+" icon at the top right to add an accessory, and scan the HomeKit QR code on the bottom of the lamp. This will directly add the lamp and allow you to control brightness and color. This is the simplest native integration method without needing extra bridging tools.

c3150cdc85dd.name: How to connect Mi Home devices that don't support Apple HomeKit using HomeBridge?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mi Home devices that do not support HomeKit can be bridged using HomeBridge installed on a Mac computer, simulating them as HomeKit accessories. Install node.js and HomeBridge, then find the corresponding plugin for your device (for example, use homebridge-mijia-camera for Mi Home cameras). After configuring the IP and Token, start HomeBridge, and you can add and control the device in the "Home" app.

c3150cdc85dd.name: How to obtain the Token required by HomeBridge for Mi Home cameras?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, find the camera's IP address using the Mi Home app. Then connect your phone to your Mac and back up your phone data via iTunes. Use iBackupViewer to export the Mi Home app's sqlite file, and use an online tool to decrypt the ZTOKEN inside the sqlite file. Convert this into the Token required by HomeBridge. This is currently the effective method to obtain the Token.

c3150cdc85dd.name: How to add Mi Home LED Smart Desk Lamp to Apple HomeKit using HomeBridge?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Mi Home LED Smart Desk Lamp does not support HomeKit natively. First, bind the device in the Yeelight app and enable LAN control. Then install the homebridge-yeelight-wifi plugin on your Mac, modify the HomeBridge config.json to add the Yeelight platform settings. After starting HomeBridge, the lamp can be controlled for color and brightness in the "Home" app.

c3150cdc85dd.name: How to set up Apple HomeKit automation to control Mi Home devices automatically when arriving or leaving home?

acceptedAnswer.text: HomeKit requires a home hub (such as HomePod, Apple TV, or iPad) for precise automation. If you don't have a hub, you can use third-party apps like myHome Plus combined with your phone's GPS to set trigger zones and link them to HomeKit scenes. This enables automatic execution of actions like turning lights or cameras on or off when entering or leaving home. GPS accuracy is about 100 meters.

c3150cdc85dd.name: How to use Mi Home native app with Shortcuts and IFTTT to achieve automation?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the Mi Home app, enable iOS Shortcuts support through the "Lab Features." Add Mi Home scenes to the Shortcuts app, then use IFTTT to set GPS-based enter or leave triggers that send notifications linked to executing the shortcuts. Tapping the notification will automatically run the Mi Home scene, achieving similar automation functionality with an accuracy of about 100 meters.

c3150cdc85dd.name: How to control Mi Home smart devices using Siri voice commands?

acceptedAnswer.text: HomeKit devices can be controlled directly with Siri voice commands without extra setup. For Mi Home native scenes, you need to add Siri in the Mi Home app's "Smart" tab, record voice commands, and then you can use Siri to trigger the specified scenes, enabling voice control of Mi Home devices.

a66ce3dc8bb9.name: How to install the Catalyst Apple Watch case without affecting its waterproof performance?

acceptedAnswer.text: When installing the Catalyst case, first unscrew the back screws, place the Apple Watch body into the waterproof sleeve, and ensure the sleeve has no wrinkles to avoid compromising waterproofing. Finally, reattach the top case and watch band, and screw back the screws without tightening them too much. This maintains the IP-68 rating with 100-meter waterproof performance.

a66ce3dc8bb9.name: Who is the Muvit Apple Watch case suitable for?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Muvit case is suitable for regular office workers or occasional exercisers because it is lightweight and easy to install or remove, allowing you to change the watch band according to your mood. Although its protection is simpler compared to Catalyst, it passes the US military MIL-STD 810G 3-meter drop test, providing sufficient protection for daily life.

a66ce3dc8bb9.name: Does the Catalyst Apple Watch case affect heart rate detection and charging functions?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Catalyst case does not affect heart rate detection or charging functions. In tests, heart rate data was identical to the bare watch, and charging worked normally with direct attachment and typical speed, ensuring full functionality and convenience during use.

a66ce3dc8bb9.name: Why is just applying a full-coverage screen protector not enough to protect the Apple Watch?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Apple Watch screen is curved, and the edges of full-coverage screen protectors are prone to cracking from rubbing against the watch frame. The author replaced three screen protectors within three months, each costing about 990 TWD. Applying only a screen protector cannot effectively prevent edge chipping; a protective case can reinforce the frame and reduce damage.

a66ce3dc8bb9.name: Which offers stronger protection, Catalyst or Muvit Apple Watch cases?

acceptedAnswer.text: Catalyst features an IP-68 waterproof rating and US military-grade drop protection, providing 360-degree all-around protection, suitable for intense sports or outdoor activities. Muvit passes the US military 3-meter drop test, offering sufficient protection for daily life but does not have waterproof capabilities.

a66ce3dc8bb9.name: Is the Catalyst Apple Watch case bulky or heavy to wear?

acceptedAnswer.text: Although the Catalyst case provides full protection, it is designed to be ultra-thin and lightweight. The strap uses skin-friendly materials, making it feel similar to the original sport band. While it looks more rugged, it is actually comfortable to wear and well-suited for the larger 44mm models.

a66ce3dc8bb9.name: How to quickly install the Muvit Apple Watch case?

acceptedAnswer.text: To install the Muvit case, simply remove the original Apple Watch band first, place the watch body face down into the case, then reattach the band. The process is simple and fast, making it easy to change bands according to different life scenarios.

729d7b6817a4.name: How to fairly group teams for an internal engineering CTF competition while promoting communication?

acceptedAnswer.text: When grouping, distribute engineers evenly according to their expertise and strengths, ensuring representation from Front-End, Back-End, APP, DevOps, and other fields to avoid questions biased toward a single domain. This grouping method is not only fair but also encourages cross-disciplinary communication among team members. The competition time is usually limited to within 90 minutes, allowing teams to brainstorm together after grouping.

729d7b6817a4.name: How to quickly build a simple and stable CTF competition system using PHP and JSON?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use PHP for the backend without any frameworks, storing data directly in JSON files without a database to reduce complexity and security risks. The frontend uses the Semantic UI framework for rapid interface development. The system updates answer status every second via Ajax, and an answer locking mechanism ensures team members must collaborate to solve challenges. This architecture can be completed within a week, suitable for resource-limited side projects.

729d7b6817a4.name: How to design CTF challenges so engineers from different fields can participate and find them interesting?

acceptedAnswer.text: Challenges should not be overly specialized in one technology but combine diverse elements like cybersecurity, coding, and logical reasoning, such as encryption/decryption, bit overflow, and side-channel timing attacks. Challenge titles and descriptions can incorporate movie references or storytelling to increase fun and exploration motivation. This way, engineers with expertise in Front-End, Back-End, APP, and other areas can all find entry points.

729d7b6817a4.name: How to use Gmail hosted mailbox features to create multiple alias email addresses in CTF challenges?

acceptedAnswer.text: Gmail hosted mailboxes allow adding "." or "+" followed by strings to the main account, for example, zhgchgli@gmail.com can use variants like z.hgchgli@gmail.com or zhgchgli+1@gmail.com, and all emails will be delivered to the main inbox. This feature can create multiple identities without registering multiple accounts, suitable for CTF challenges requiring multiple mailbox reception.

729d7b6817a4.name: How to exploit PHP vulnerabilities in CTF challenges to bypass checks and execute specific functions?

acceptedAnswer.text: A common PHP vulnerability involves bypassing checks using GET parameter structures. For example, an answer like ?m.id[]=admin uses array parameters to bypass conditional checks and trigger the setPassedCookie() function inside the else clause. Such vulnerabilities often occur in PHP programs without strict input filtering and are suitable for designing beginner-level security challenges.

729d7b6817a4.name: How does side-channel timing attack work in CTF challenges?

acceptedAnswer.text: Side-channel timing attacks exploit differences in response delays caused by different inputs during system verification. For example, starting with input 0000, if input 2000 causes a one-second delay, it indicates the first digit is 2, and so on, eventually deducing the full verification code like 2256. This attack simulates system timing differences, but in practice, network factors make precise judgment difficult.

729d7b6817a4.name: How to determine if CTF challenge difficulty is suitable for a 90-minute competition?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can observe actual competition results; for instance, the first-place team solves 9 challenges within 90 minutes, while the last-place team solves 7, indicating the difficulty is neither too easy nor too hard. Challenge design should balance base points and time-based points, include a hint purchase mechanism, and balance solving speed with strategy to ensure the competition is challenging and promotes teamwork.

46410aaada00.name: Why can data still be stolen by mitmproxy man-in-the-middle attacks when iOS apps use HTTPS for data transmission?

acceptedAnswer.text: HTTPS encrypts communication by itself, but man-in-the-middle attacks replace the original HTTPS certificate with their own certificate, allowing the attacker to decrypt and monitor the data. mitmproxy intercepts and decrypts API transmission content by installing a custom certificate on the phone. Because the phone trusts this certificate, the data is stolen. For example, after setting up a proxy with mitmproxy, you can see the details of API requests and responses sent by the app.

46410aaada00.name: How to set up a man-in-the-middle attack environment with mitmproxy on Mac and iOS devices?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, install mitmproxy on Mac using brew. After starting it, ensure the phone and Mac are on the same WiFi or network sharing environment. Set the phone's WiFi HTTP proxy to the Mac's IP and port 8080. Then, open http://mitm.it in the phone's Safari to install the mitmproxy custom certificate and enable trust. After completion, the phone's HTTPS traffic will be intercepted by mitmproxy and displayed in the Mac terminal or mitmweb interface.

46410aaada00.name: Why does mitmproxy fail to sniff app API data, and how do apps defend against it?

acceptedAnswer.text: There are two main app defense methods: first, SSL Pinning, which embeds the correct certificate information inside the app and rejects connections if the certificate does not match; second, API data encryption, where the app encodes and encrypts data before sending, and the backend decrypts it, with the same applied to returned data. These methods prevent mitmproxy from directly decrypting data but still carry risks of being cracked, as SSL Pinning can be bypassed and keys may be obtained through reverse engineering.

46410aaada00.name: What common mitmproxy shortcut keys help conveniently view and edit requests?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common mitmproxy keys include: ? to view help, k or up arrow to move up, j or down arrow to move down, enter to enter details, q to go back, b to export response content to a text file, e to edit Request parameters, r to resend Request, f to filter records, and z to clear all records. These keys allow users to quickly browse and modify intercepted traffic.

46410aaada00.name: Does mitmproxy provide a graphical interface for users unfamiliar with command lines?

acceptedAnswer.text: mitmproxy offers the mitmweb command to launch a Web GUI, allowing users to operate and observe intercepted traffic through a graphical interface in a browser. This is user-friendly for those not familiar with the CLI and makes it easy to browse request and response details, filter, and analyze data. On macOS, there is also the free graphical tool Proxyman that can be used alongside.

46410aaada00.name: How to use mitmdump with a Python script to automatically filter and redirect HTTP requests?

acceptedAnswer.text: mitmdump can output intercepted records to a text file using the -w parameter and use the -ns parameter with a Python script to read and manipulate traffic. The script can modify Request headers and parameters or redirect requests based on conditions. For example, the script can detect if the Host is 123.com.tw and change it to 456.com.tw. Run mitmdump with -s script.py to automate processing.

46410aaada00.name: Why do apps still need to implement SSL Pinning or data encryption even when using HTTPS?

acceptedAnswer.text: HTTPS prevents data from being eavesdropped during transmission, but man-in-the-middle attacks can decrypt data using custom certificates. SSL Pinning ensures the app only trusts specific certificates to avoid replacement; data encryption means that even if intercepted, the data remains encrypted. These two layers of protection greatly enhance security, preventing attackers from directly reading API content and avoiding data leaks.

4079036c85c2.name: What venue improvements did iPlayground 2019 have to enhance the participant experience?

acceptedAnswer.text: iPlayground 2019 moved the venue from last year's underground conference hall to the NTU Boya Hall, offering a larger and more modern space to avoid crowding. The classrooms were equipped with desks and power outlets, making it convenient to use personal computers and improving overall comfort and convenience for attendees.

4079036c85c2.name: What memorable features did the iPlayground 2019 agenda have?

acceptedAnswer.text: This year featured well-known speakers from both domestic and international communities, including the popular speaker Wang Wei (Cat God). For the first time, limited-capacity workshops with hands-on teaching were added. The agenda covered popular topics such as network request component design, UICollectionViewCompositionalLayout, SwiftUI Apple Watch development, app security, and compilation speed optimization.

4079036c85c2.name: Why did the app security topic at iPlayground 2019 change people's perceptions?

acceptedAnswer.text: The speaker pointed out that apps are not absolutely secure; if the cost to crack is lower than the cost to protect, they are vulnerable to attacks. Through demonstrations of man-in-the-middle attacks and tool introductions, attendees learned that increasing cracking difficulty is a practical security strategy rather than aiming for complete closure. This changed the common impression that "Apple is very secure."

4079036c85c2.name: How did iPlayground 2019 enhance participant communication and interaction?

acceptedAnswer.text: With a larger venue, there were sponsor booths and an Ask the Speaker area, making it easy for participants to interact with speakers and vendors. Additionally, there were plenty of giveaways like eco-friendly straws, stickers, and raffle activities, which fostered a lively atmosphere and helped attendees meet new friends and expand their networks.

4079036c85c2.name: What were the highlights of the catering and After Party at iPlayground 2019?

acceptedAnswer.text: For two days, exquisite boxed meals and unlimited iced coffee and tea were provided throughout the day. The After Party was changed to an afternoon tea format, offering drinks, shumai, and desserts. Although it lacked the bar-like storytelling atmosphere, it encouraged free interaction and gave participants more opportunities to meet new people.

4079036c85c2.name: What advantages of UICollectionViewCompositionalLayout were shared at iPlayground 2019?

acceptedAnswer.text: UICollectionViewCompositionalLayout allows developers to implement complex layouts more cleanly and maintainably without subclassing UICollectionViewLayout or using the complicated approach of embedding CollectionViews inside CollectionView cells. The project also supports iOS versions prior to 12, expanding compatibility.

4079036c85c2.name: How did iPlayground 2019 help address the issue of slow compilation speeds?

acceptedAnswer.text: Ding Peiyao shared attempts and experiences in optimizing compilation speed, proposing improvements targeting the pain points of frequent waiting during UI tweaks. These practical techniques are worth studying and applying to reduce frustration caused by slow compilation during development.

21119db777dd.name: How to use the built-in Shortcuts app on iOS 13.1 and above to automate control of Mijia smart home devices?

acceptedAnswer.text: On iOS 13.1 and above, you can use the built-in Shortcuts app to set up automation. First, create a smart scene in the Mijia app and add a Siri command. Then, in the Shortcuts app under the "Automation" tab, create a new personal automation, select arriving or leaving a location, set the location, and add the action to run the Mijia Siri command you just created. After setup, you will receive a notification when entering or leaving the designated area; tap it to execute the action.

21119db777dd.name: Why not use the Mijia app's geolocation trigger feature directly to set home or away modes?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Mijia app's geolocation trigger feature often experiences GPS drift issues in Taiwan because it uses the Amap (Gaode) map from mainland China, which has fewer landmarks and less accurate positioning in Taiwan. Additionally, Mijia can only set locations that have landmarks on the map, which limits flexibility. Using iOS Shortcuts automation combined with Mijia Siri commands can avoid these limitations.

21119db777dd.name: Will iOS Shortcuts automation triggering Mijia smart home actions execute automatically?

acceptedAnswer.text: When iOS Shortcuts automation triggers, it sends a notification to the user. You need to tap the notification and unlock the phone to execute the action; it does not run automatically in the background. This is an iOS system security restriction. To achieve fully automatic execution, you need to use a HomePod, Apple TV, or iPad as a home hub.

21119db777dd.name: What is the approximate GPS positioning accuracy when using iOS Shortcuts automation with Mijia smart home?

acceptedAnswer.text: The GPS sensing range has an error margin of about 100 meters, which is the normal range for system positioning accuracy. When setting up automation, consider this error to avoid setting too small a trigger area that could prevent accurate execution of actions.

21119db777dd.name: How does iOS Shortcuts automation with Mijia smart home execute on Apple Watch?

acceptedAnswer.text: When entering or leaving the set location, the Apple Watch receives a notification to run the shortcut. Tapping the notification will execute the Mijia smart home action. Unlike IFTTT, Shortcuts notifications can be executed directly on the watch without needing to take out your phone.

21119db777dd.name: How to avoid conflicts between Mijia smart home Siri commands and iOS built-in commands?

acceptedAnswer.text: When setting up smart scenes and adding Siri commands in the Mijia app, the voice command you enter must not duplicate any iOS built-in commands. Otherwise, Siri may fail to recognize it or prioritize the system command. It is recommended to use unique and uncommon phrases as the Siri shortcut command names.

21119db777dd.name: What are the limitations of using iOS Shortcuts automation to control Mijia smart home without a HomePod or Apple TV?

acceptedAnswer.text: Without a home hub device, iOS Shortcuts automation can only send notifications, requiring manual tapping and unlocking of the phone to execute Mijia actions; it cannot run automatically in the background. Additionally, GPS positioning has about a 100-meter error margin, which may affect trigger accuracy.

bcff7c157941.name: What issues occur when using the Xiaomi AI Speaker as a Bluetooth speaker for watching videos?

acceptedAnswer.text: When using the Xiaomi AI Speaker as a Bluetooth speaker, there is a severe delay of 1 to 2 seconds when watching videos. This is a hardware issue and currently there is no official solution. This delay causes the audio and video to be out of sync, negatively affecting the viewing experience. Mainland China forums have also reported that this problem remains unresolved.

bcff7c157941.name: Why can't the Mi Home Bluetooth Temperature and Humidity Sensor read data when the phone is out of Bluetooth range?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Mi Home Bluetooth Temperature and Humidity Sensor only supports Bluetooth connection, so when the phone is out of Bluetooth range, it cannot directly read data. Unless paired with a Bluetooth gateway or other Mi Home devices that support Bluetooth gateway functionality, data cannot be relayed via WiFi. This is because the sensor does not have built-in WiFi and relies solely on Bluetooth transmission.

bcff7c157941.name: Does the Xiaomi AI Speaker support the Bluetooth gateway function for the Mi Home Bluetooth Temperature and Humidity Sensor?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Xiaomi AI Speaker does not support the Bluetooth gateway function and cannot relay data from the Mi Home Bluetooth Temperature and Humidity Sensor to the internet. In contrast, the Mi Home DC Inverter Fan surprisingly supports this feature, allowing it to relay sensor data via WiFi. This is quite unusual since not all Mi Home devices have Bluetooth gateway capability.
