bcff7c157941.name: What smart control features does the Mi Home DC Inverter Fan have?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Mi Home DC Inverter Fan can be controlled via the Mi Home app to turn on/off, adjust wind speed (from 1 to 100 levels), set oscillation angle (within 120 degrees), and select modes including natural wind. It also supports scheduled timers and can be linked with other Mi Home devices, for example, automatically turning on when the temperature exceeds 30°C. These smart features make usage more convenient and energy-efficient.

bcff7c157941.name: What advantages does the Xiaomi AI Speaker's voice assistant have compared to Siri?

acceptedAnswer.text: Xiaomi AI Speaker's Xiao Ai voice assistant is smarter than Siri, capable of reasoning beyond simple commands; for example, after asking about the weather, it will also remind you to bring an umbrella. It has a rich set of extended skills, such as playing mini-games, telling comedic dialogues, and even chatting in a maid-like tone. The voice recognition is sensitive, with 360-degree audio pickup and sufficient volume, making the overall interaction more thoughtful.

bcff7c157941.name: How do I turn off push notifications for the Mi Home Bluetooth Temperature and Humidity Sensor?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can disable push notifications in the Mi Home app by going to “Me” -> top right corner “Settings” -> “Device Notifications,” then find the Mi Home Bluetooth Temperature and Humidity Sensor and turn off its notifications. This will stop alerts for temperature or humidity abnormalities, helping to avoid frequent reminders caused by normal environmental conditions common in Taiwan.

bcff7c157941.name: Which app is needed to sync and record weight data from the Xiaomi Scale 2?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Xiaomi Scale 2 requires the Xiaomi Health app to sync and record weight data. When weighing, simply open the app to automatically record the measurements. Besides basic weight, it also supports weighing objects and balance testing features. This makes it convenient to track weight changes and enhances the user experience.

b08ef940c196.name: How does iOS Deferred Deep Link allow the installed app to still obtain the original URL parameters?

acceptedAnswer.text: iOS Deferred Deep Link works by first saving the original link information to the clipboard or cookies. After the user installs the app, the app reads the clipboard or the cookies from a background-loaded SFSafariViewController to retrieve the original parameters and navigate to the corresponding screen. This way, even if the user is redirected to the App Store before installing, the source information can still be restored after installation.

b08ef940c196.name: Why does reading the clipboard on iOS 16 and above trigger a prompt window?

acceptedAnswer.text: Starting from iOS 16, to protect user privacy, when an app actively reads the clipboard, a prompt window appears asking for user permission. The user must allow access to read the clipboard content. This restricts the use of the clipboard for passing data in Deferred Deep Link implementations, requiring a more user-friendly design or alternative methods.

b08ef940c196.name: How to implement Deferred Deep Link on iOS 9 since it doesn't support automatic clipboard access?

acceptedAnswer.text: iOS 9 does not support JavaScript automatically writing to the clipboard, so the approach is to share cookies between Safari and the app. The app secretly loads an SFSafariViewController in the background to read web cookies. The webpage uses JavaScript to read the cookies and calls the app via a URL Scheme, achieving the Deferred Deep Link effect.

b08ef940c196.name: What is the difference between Universal Link and URL Scheme in Deep Link handling?

acceptedAnswer.text: URL Scheme can only detect if the app is installed and cannot control the navigation flow, often leading to incorrect page jumps; it is usually used for internal app calls. Universal Link is a web link that redirects to a webpage if the app is not installed, or to the app if installed. It supports passing parameters within the webpage, offering a better and safer user experience.

b08ef940c196.name: What are the advantages and disadvantages of using the clipboard method for Deferred Deep Link?

acceptedAnswer.text: The clipboard method is simple to implement and efficient, supporting iOS 10 and above without browser restrictions. However, on iOS 16 and above, a prompt appears when reading the clipboard, which affects user experience. Additionally, users may dislike apps automatically reading the clipboard, so privacy and user feelings must be considered.

b08ef940c196.name: What are the limitations of reading cookies with SFSafariViewController in the background?

acceptedAnswer.text: When SFSafariViewController is loaded in the background, if its opacity is set too low or it is hidden, it may fail to load properly. Also, on iOS 11 and above, Safari cookies cannot be read directly; they can only be accessed through webpage JavaScript interactions. Moreover, app review does not allow SFSafariViewController to be completely hidden, making implementation more complex.

b08ef940c196.name: Why can't Deferred Deep Link simply use a normal Deep Link combined with redirecting to the App Store?

acceptedAnswer.text: A normal Deep Link can only detect if the app is installed. If not installed, redirecting to the App Store interrupts the source parameters, so after installation, the app cannot retrieve the original data and can only display the homepage. This increases user drop-off and results in a poor experience, so Deferred Deep Link technology is needed to preserve source information.

14cee137c565.name: How to disable the pull-down-to-dismiss gesture of UIViewController after iOS 13?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can disable the pull-down-to-dismiss gesture by setting the UIViewController's isModalInPresentation property to true, or by implementing the UIAdaptivePresentationControllerDelegate method presentationControllerShouldDismiss and returning false. Either approach will prevent the user from dismissing the view controller via the pull-down gesture, helping to avoid accidental dismissals.

14cee137c565.name: How to implement pull-down-to-dismiss interactive gesture for UIViewController using UIPercentDrivenInteractiveTransition?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, create a subclass of UIPercentDrivenInteractiveTransition, bind it to the target UIViewController and the interactive view, and add a UIPanGestureRecognizer. When the gesture begins, set wantsInteractiveStart to true and call dismiss. During gesture changes, calculate the drag percentage and call update. When the gesture ends, determine if the threshold is passed to complete or cancel the animation. This process enables a smooth pull-down-to-dismiss interactive animation.

14cee137c565.name: Why do UIViewControllers presented as cards after iOS 13 not trigger viewWillAppear and viewWillDisappear?

acceptedAnswer.text: Because starting from iOS 13, the default modalPresentationStyle is UIModalPresentationAutomatic (card style), the system does not call the underlying ViewController's viewWillAppear and viewWillDisappear methods. This is because the card style does not fully cover the underlying view, so these lifecycle methods are not triggered. Developers should be aware of this to avoid relying on these methods for logic execution.

14cee137c565.name: How to implement a full-screen right-swipe back gesture with custom animation for UIViewController?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can add a UIPanGestureRecognizer to the view and control the gesture interaction using a custom UIPercentDrivenInteractiveTransition. When the gesture begins, call navigationController.popViewController(animated: true). During the gesture, calculate the swipe percentage and call update. When the gesture ends, decide whether to complete or cancel the animation. For the animation, implement UIViewControllerAnimatedTransitioning to control the screen sliding in from the left and sliding out to the right.

14cee137c565.name: How to ensure the transition animation completes without freezing the screen in UIViewController transitions?

acceptedAnswer.text: You must call transitionContext.completeTransition(!transitionContext.transitionWasCancelled) at the end of the animation to inform the system that the animation has completed. If using UIView.animate, ensure the animation actually runs (e.g., by changing the frame), otherwise the completion block won't be called, causing the UI to freeze. This is a critical step in transition animations; missing it will lead to UI freezing.

14cee137c565.name: Is it better to use UIView.animate or UIViewPropertyAnimator for iOS transition animations?

acceptedAnswer.text: For general non-interactive animations, UIView.animate is sufficient and stable. UIViewPropertyAnimator supports pausing and resuming animations, making it suitable for interactive animations, but in practice it can cause issues like navigation bar flickering. The author recommends using UIView.animate unless you have special requirements, as it is more reliable.

14cee137c565.name: How to avoid gesture conflicts and implement pull-down-to-dismiss in a UIViewController with UIScrollView?

acceptedAnswer.text: Implement the UIPanGestureRecognizer delegate method gestureRecognizer(_:shouldRecognizeSimultaneouslyWith:), check if the other gesture's view is a UIScrollView, and verify if scrollView.contentOffset.y is less than or equal to 0. Only allow simultaneous gesture recognition when the scroll view is scrolled to the top, preventing conflicts with internal scrolling and ensuring the pull-down-to-dismiss gesture can be triggered smoothly.

94a4020edb82.name: What are the functional differences between the Mi Home app in Taiwan and China?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Mi Home app has different functions in Taiwan and China. The China region offers more features, while the Taiwan version supports the KKBOX music service. The China version includes full smart home functions and Xiao Ai training, whereas the Taiwan version lacks Xiao Ai training, limiting smart scene settings.

94a4020edb82.name: Why can the Xiaomi Air Purifier 3 only be added to the Mi Home app set to Taiwan region?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Taiwan version of the Xiaomi Air Purifier 3 cannot be added to the Mi Home app set to the China region because the pairing methods differ. It can only be added when the Mi Home app region is set to Taiwan. This requires users to switch the app region and reset all devices to use the air purifier.

94a4020edb82.name: What issues occur if the Xiao Ai speaker and Mi Home app region settings are different?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Xiao Ai speaker and Mi Home app region settings must be consistent; otherwise, they cannot connect to control smart appliances. If the regions differ, the Xiao Ai speaker cannot control Mi Home smart devices, resulting in failed smart home integration.

94a4020edb82.name: How to fix the issue when Mi Home smart home scenes are not recognized by the Xiao Ai speaker?

acceptedAnswer.text: Using Traditional Chinese characters in scene names may cause the Xiao Ai speaker to not recognize them. Changing the names to Simplified Chinese characters allows proper execution. For example, changing “出門” to “出门” enables the Xiao Ai speaker to correctly execute the scene.

94a4020edb82.name: Will the Xiao Ai speaker lose any functions after switching the Mi Home app region to Taiwan?

acceptedAnswer.text: After switching the Mi Home app region to Taiwan, the Xiao Ai speaker will lose the “Xiao Ai training” feature and cannot set custom voice commands for smart home scenes directly within the app. However, after authorized linking, scenes and devices can still be controlled by the Xiao Ai speaker.

94a4020edb82.name: How to interpret the air quality values of the Xiaomi Air Purifier 3?

acceptedAnswer.text: An air quality value below 75 on the Xiaomi Air Purifier 3 indicates good air quality; values above 75 indicate poor quality, and 150 or higher signifies severe pollution. Indoor air quality usually ranges from 001 to 015, with lower values meaning cleaner air.

d01252331b53.name: What is the greatest sense of achievement after managing Medium for one year?

acceptedAnswer.text: The greatest sense of achievement comes from having readers and feedback on my articles, which motivates me to keep writing. Seeing my content being appreciated turns writing from mere record-keeping into a process of growth and communication. For example, accumulating about 60,000 views and 5,000 claps proves that the effort pays off.

d01252331b53.name: Why don't I set up paywalls on my Medium articles?

acceptedAnswer.text: I believe knowledge should be freely shared, so none of my articles have paywalls. This allows more people to read and learn freely, encouraging knowledge sharing. Of course, if readers like the content, they can support Medium by becoming paid members, helping the platform continue to operate.

d01252331b53.name: How do I differentiate between lifestyle and technical articles on Medium?

acceptedAnswer.text: I created two Publication channels to separate content: ZRealm Life focuses on lifestyle and unboxing, while ZRealm Dev concentrates on work and technical articles. This way, readers can follow different topics based on their interests, making the content more organized and targeted.

d01252331b53.name: What is the biggest challenge encountered during the Medium writing process?

acceptedAnswer.text: The biggest challenge is the fear of misleading readers or misunderstanding myself, so I conduct in-depth research when writing to ensure the content is accurate and valuable. This pressure makes me not just record information but deeply explore issues, improving article quality and personal growth.

d01252331b53.name: How to use a Chrome Extension to post beautiful code on Medium?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can install the Chrome extension Code Medium, which allows you to directly paste well-formatted code using Gist in Medium articles. This is especially convenient for developers, enhancing the professionalism and readability of the articles.

d01252331b53.name: How much traffic and followers have been accumulated after managing Medium for one year?

acceptedAnswer.text: By 2019, I accumulated about 60,000 views and over 200 followers, along with 5,000 claps. These numbers reflect a certain level of attention and support for the articles, motivating me to keep creating.

d01252331b53.name: What changes have occurred in meeting community friends after managing Medium for one year?

acceptedAnswer.text: Before writing articles, I was unfamiliar with the community and had almost no interactions. After managing Medium for a year, I met many friends, making me feel less alone on the development journey. This kind of interaction brings more inspiration and support, boosting my motivation to create.

a8c2d7ed144b.name: How to use pointInside in iOS UIButton to enlarge the button's clickable area?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can subclass UIButton and override the pointInside method, using UIEdgeInsets to expand the bounds area. Set negative touchEdgeInsets values to make the clickable area extend beyond the original button size. For example, setting -10 for top, bottom, left, and right will enlarge the clickable area by 10 points. This makes the button more user-friendly for people with thicker fingers and improves operation accuracy.

a8c2d7ed144b.name: Why is it not good to use a transparent UIButton overlay to enlarge the clickable area?

acceptedAnswer.text: Using a transparent UIButton overlay increases the number of components, making management more complex and prone to errors. When there are many components, controlling event responses becomes troublesome, and layout and maintenance costs are high, which is not suitable for large projects. This method is less straightforward and effective compared to directly modifying the UIButton's clickable area.

a8c2d7ed144b.name: How to enlarge the clickable area using UIButton Extension without subclassing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Since Extensions cannot add stored properties, you can use Objective-C's Associated Object technique to bind a touchEdgeInsets property. Then override the pointInside method to expand the clickable area based on touchEdgeInsets. This way, all UIButtons can directly use this feature without replacing them with custom subclasses.

a8c2d7ed144b.name: How to adjust UIButton's insets in UIKit to make the button look consistent with the design?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can adjust UIButton's imageEdgeInsets, titleEdgeInsets, and contentEdgeInsets to position the icon and title according to the design specifications. This method is suitable when using Storyboard or xib, allowing easy adjustments directly in Interface Builder. However, it requires strict spacing for the icon; otherwise, alignment issues may occur.

a8c2d7ed144b.name: Why do touchEdgeInsets values need to be negative to enlarge the clickable area?

acceptedAnswer.text: Because UIEdgeInsets uses positive values to shrink the area and negative values to expand it outward. Setting touchEdgeInsets to negative values extends the bounds outward, increasing the clickable area size. For example, setting top: -10 means expanding the area 10 points upwards, achieving the effect of enlarging the clickable area.

a8c2d7ed144b.name: How to enlarge the clickable area for UIImageView when using UITapGestureRecognizer?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can override UIImageView's pointInside method, similar to how UIButton's clickable area is enlarged. Adjust the clickable area size so that the gesture recognition range exceeds the original image size, improving tap accuracy and user experience. This is especially effective for image taps using UITapGestureRecognizer.

a8c2d7ed144b.name: Why is it not recommended to replace all UIButtons with a custom MyButton in large projects?

acceptedAnswer.text: Replacing all UIButtons with MyButton increases maintenance burden and may conflict with third-party components or existing code. This approach lacks flexibility and convenience. Extending the original UIButton via Extension allows all buttons to inherently have the enlarged clickable area feature, reducing complexity and conflict risks.

7498e1ff93ce.name: What environment and equipment are needed to start iOS reverse engineering?

acceptedAnswer.text: iOS reverse engineering requires a jailbroken iPhone device (an older model like iPhone 6 is recommended) paired with macOS (such as Catalina 10.15.3). The jailbroken phone must have Cydia and Open SSH installed, and ensure the phone and computer are on the same network. Additionally, Python and related packages (like frida) need to be installed to perform tasks such as dumping the app.

7498e1ff93ce.name: Why can't .ipa files extracted from the App Store be used directly?

acceptedAnswer.text: .ipa files downloaded from the App Store are protected by FairPlay DRM encryption and cannot be used or decompiled directly. You must first use a jailbroken phone and tools (such as frida-ios-dump) to dynamically dump and remove the protection before obtaining a usable original .ipa file.

7498e1ff93ce.name: How to use the Lookin tool to view someone else's iOS app UI layout structure?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, install LookinLoader from Cydia on the jailbroken phone and enable the app you want to inspect. Connect the phone to a Mac via USB, open the target app, then on the Mac's Lookin tool, select that app's screen to view its UI layout structure. You can also modify UI elements in real time and see the changes reflected on the phone.

7498e1ff93ce.name: How to use frida-ios-dump to extract and dump iOS apps from a jailbroken phone?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, install Python, pip, and frida on the Mac, and install Open SSH and the matching version of Frida on the jailbroken phone. Connect the phone via USB, run iproxy to forward port 2222 to 22, SSH into the phone, then use dump.py to list apps. Specify the app name and output path to perform the dump. After success, you will get the decrypted .ipa file.

7498e1ff93ce.name: How to use the class-dump tool to export .h header files from an iOS app?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use the MonkeyDev-modified class-dump tool and run the command `./class-dump -H APP_PATH/APP_NAME.app -o OUTPUT_PATH` to export the .h header files of the Objective-C written app and its frameworks. This method does not work for Swift projects.

7498e1ff93ce.name: How to use Cycript for dynamic injection and debugging in reverse engineering?

acceptedAnswer.text: Install Cycript on the jailbroken phone, SSH into the phone, find the target app's Process ID, and run `cycript -p ProcessID` to inject. You can debug using Objective-C or JavaScript syntax, such as dynamically modifying the UI, which facilitates real-time analysis and control of app behavior.

7498e1ff93ce.name: What are the differences between Reveal and Lookin tools for viewing iOS app UI?

acceptedAnswer.text: Reveal is a paid tool that offers more comprehensive and detailed UI structure information with real-time editing capabilities and can highlight Constraint errors, making it suitable for professional developers. Lookin is free and open-source with simpler features, suitable for everyday development and basic layout inspection, and can be used on jailbroken phones to view others' apps.

d796bf8e661e.name: Why can't AVAssetResourceLoaderDelegate intercept HLS .ts file download requests?

acceptedAnswer.text: AVAssetResourceLoaderDelegate can only intercept resource requests with custom schemes and cannot intercept standard HTTP/HTTPS .ts file requests. AVPlayer handles the download of .ts files in HLS streams directly through the underlying system without going through the Delegate, so request information cannot be obtained in Delegate methods. This limitation is confirmed by official documentation and developer experience.

d796bf8e661e.name: Why does intercepting AVPlayer's .m3u8 and .ts requests with URLProtocol not work on real devices?

acceptedAnswer.text: AVPlayer's HLS requests do not go through iOS's URL Loading System, so URLProtocol cannot intercept its .m3u8 and .ts requests. Although it might work on the simulator, on real devices AVFoundation sends requests directly bypassing URLProtocol, causing interception to fail. This is because AVPlayer uses an internal dedicated network layer that is not controlled by URLProtocol.

d796bf8e661e.name: What are the drawbacks of using a Local HTTP Server as a Reverse Proxy to implement HLS caching?

acceptedAnswer.text: A Local HTTP Server operates stably when running in the foreground, but when the app enters the background without continuous requests, the system suspends its socket services causing disconnections. Even if auto-suspension is disabled, long periods of inactivity will still lead to the system terminating it, affecting cache service stability. The workaround is to continuously send long connections or empty requests, but this increases system load and is not a perfect solution.

d796bf8e661e.name: Why doesn't AVPlayer support playing local .m3u8 files using file:// paths?

acceptedAnswer.text: AVPlayer requires .m3u8 files to be served via HTTP/HTTPS protocols and does not support the file:// protocol. This is because the HLS specification is designed as a network streaming format, and AVPlayer internally performs network requests and processing for .m3u8 files and their segments. Using local file paths directly cannot be properly parsed or played.

d796bf8e661e.name: Does AVFoundation AVPlayer have a built-in HTTP Client Cache mechanism that can be utilized?

acceptedAnswer.text: Although AVFoundation AVPlayer receives HTTP Response Headers such as Cache-Control and eTag, it does not implement an effective HTTP Client Cache mechanism. Tests show that replaying still triggers new requests to the server without using local cache, so HTTP Cache Headers cannot be relied upon to achieve HLS caching functionality.

d796bf8e661e.name: How can AVPlayer be used to implement a play-while-cache feature?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most feasible current solution is to start a Local HTTP Server inside the app as a Reverse Proxy, directing .m3u8 and .ts requests to the local server, which handles caching and responses. AVPlayer only needs to play from the local HTTP address, enabling play-while-cache functionality. However, this approach has stability issues when the app runs in the background and requires additional handling.

d796bf8e661e.name: If not using AVFoundation AVPlayer, what alternative solutions exist for HLS playback and caching?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can implement audio file parsing, caching, encoding, and playback yourself, but this requires deep technical expertise and significant time. Alternatively, third-party open-source players like FreeStreamer can be used, which have implemented some streaming and caching features, saving the difficulty of developing these yourself. Both self-implementation and not using HLS are more hardcore and complex solutions.

99db2a1fbfe5.name: Why use Raspberry Pi with HomeBridge to connect Mi Home appliances to HomeKit?

acceptedAnswer.text: Using Raspberry Pi as the HomeBridge host allows bridging Mi Home appliances that do not support HomeKit within the local network, enabling control via the Apple Home app. HomeBridge runs continuously and converts Mi Home device protocols through plugins, allowing iPhones to directly control these devices. For example, Mi Home smart cameras and air purifiers can be operated within HomeKit through HomeBridge.

99db2a1fbfe5.name: How to obtain and convert the Token of Mi Home appliances for use with HomeBridge?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, back up the Mi Home app device data via iPhone and export digital_mihome.sqlite using iBackupViewer to obtain the ZTOKEN. Then use an online AES decryption site to decrypt the ZTOKEN Hex with a key of 32 zeros. After removing spaces, the result is the usable Token. This Token is the necessary authentication string for HomeBridge plugins to connect to Mi Home devices.

99db2a1fbfe5.name: How to set the HomeBridge service to start automatically on Raspberry Pi boot?

acceptedAnswer.text: Create the /etc/init.d/homebridge script on the Raspberry Pi, including commands for start, stop, and restart, and specify the HomeBridge execution path. Then set permissions with sudo chmod 755 and add it to startup with sudo update-rc.d homebridge defaults. After reboot, HomeBridge will start automatically without manual execution.

99db2a1fbfe5.name: Why must "Show When Run" be enabled when running Mi Home app shortcuts automation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Due to system limitations, Mi Home app shortcut automations must show the screen when running and require the iPhone to be unlocked; they cannot run in the background without unlocking. In contrast, HomeKit's native shortcut service can run in the background without unlocking, providing faster and more stable responses.

99db2a1fbfe5.name: How to set up Mi Home LED smart desk lamp for HomeBridge control?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Mi Home LED smart desk lamp must first be bound to the Yeelight app, and the "LAN Control" feature enabled within the app to allow HomeBridge to control it via the yeelight plugin. Add the yeelight platform settings in HomeBridge's config.json. This step is necessary because the Mi Home app does not provide a LAN control toggle.

99db2a1fbfe5.name: Can HomeKit appliances be controlled remotely from external networks without Apple TV or HomePod?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. To control HomeKit devices remotely from external networks (like 4G), an Apple TV, HomePod, or iPad must act as a home hub and remain on standby. Otherwise, the Home app will show "No Response," and control is only possible within the same home network.

99db2a1fbfe5.name: Why is it recommended to assign a fixed IP to Mi Home appliances on the router?

acceptedAnswer.text: If the IP of Mi Home appliances is not fixed, it may change after reboot or reconnection, causing the IP set in HomeBridge's config.json to be incorrect, leading to connection failures or errors. Therefore, it is recommended to reserve the IP on the router to ensure stable device IPs and avoid frequent configuration changes.

2e4429f410d6.name: How to create a perspective transparent phone wallpaper using iPhone and iMovie?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, use a phone stand to fix the angle and shoot a clean video without the phone in the frame. Then set the phone wallpaper in the frame to a green background and record the operation video. Finally, use iMovie's green screen keying feature to composite the two videos. This creates a fake perspective transparent phone wallpaper effect.

2e4429f410d6.name: Why is it necessary to fix the angle with a phone stand when shooting?

acceptedAnswer.text: Fixing the phone stand ensures the perspective of the two videos is exactly the same, preventing frame shifts that would make the composite look unnatural. Handheld shooting cannot achieve the exact same perspective, which would break the illusion of perspective transparency.

2e4429f410d6.name: Why is the green background key to making perspective transparent wallpaper?

acceptedAnswer.text: The green background acts as a green screen, allowing iMovie to use the green screen keying feature to remove the green parts. This lets you composite the phone operation video with the clean video, creating the visual effect of a transparent phone screen.

2e4429f410d6.name: How to use iMovie's green/blue screen keying feature?

acceptedAnswer.text: In iMovie, first insert the clean video, then add the phone operation video. Click the "..." at the top right of the operation video and select "Green/Blue Screen," then click on the green area to complete the keying and compositing.

2e4429f410d6.name: What should be noted about the length of the two videos shot?

acceptedAnswer.text: The two videos should ideally be the same length to avoid timing differences after compositing. If the phone operation video is longer than the clean video, you can trim it to the same length in iMovie.

2e4429f410d6.name: Besides green, what other colors can be used for keying?

acceptedAnswer.text: Besides green, blue backgrounds can also be used for keying blue. iMovie works best with green and blue keying. Other colors can theoretically be used, but the effect may not be as good as green or blue.

2e4429f410d6.name: Should you hide green icon apps on the phone to avoid keying errors?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is recommended to hide apps with green icons, such as Line or Messages, to avoid accidentally keying out these green icons during the keying process, which would cause visual errors.

1aa2f8445642.name: How does Codable handle the issue of JSON field names not matching Swift property names?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can define a CodingKeys enum inside the struct to map Swift property names to JSON field names, for example case targetObject = "target_object". This way, JSONDecoder uses CodingKeys to match fields, preventing decode failures caused by name mismatches. If the difference is only between snake_case and camelCase, you can also set JSONDecoder's keyDecodingStrategy to .convertFromSnakeCase for automatic conversion.

1aa2f8445642.name: How does Swift Codable handle JSON fields that can have multiple possible types?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can implement Decodable with an enum that has associated values. During decoding, first check the field (e.g., type), then decode the corresponding struct based on the type. This allows a field like targetObject to support both UserEntity and SongEntity, and you can extract the value using switch or if case. This approach makes Codable flexible in handling multiple types.

1aa2f8445642.name: How to use Codable to skip decoding failed items in an array to avoid the entire batch failing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use nestedUnkeyedContainer to iterate through the array, and try decoding each item with try?. If decoding fails, decode an empty EmptyEntity to move the pointer forward. This way, only successfully decoded items are added, skipping erroneous entries and preventing the entire batch from failing. This method is currently a workaround because nestedUnkeyedContainer cannot directly skip elements.

1aa2f8445642.name: How does Swift JSONDecoder support custom parsing for multiple date formats?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can set JSONDecoder's dateDecodingStrategy to .custom and implement your own logic. For example, first try parsing with ISO8601DateFormatter; if that fails, use DateFormatter to parse the "yyyy/MM/dd" format. If both fail, throw an error. This method supports multiple date formats simultaneously, increasing parsing flexibility.

1aa2f8445642.name: Why separate Entity and Model, and have Entity only handle Codable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Separating Entity and Model is for separation of concerns: Entity handles JSON parsing and implements Decodable, while Model handles business logic and internal use. This way, Entity focuses solely on matching API data structures, and Model can freely add internal fields (like isSave) and support Objective-C calls, improving maintainability and flexibility.

1aa2f8445642.name: What types of containers are there in Codable's init(from decoder: Decoder) and how to use them?

acceptedAnswer.text: There are three types of containers: keyedContainer for dictionary structures, accessing fields by CodingKeys; singleValueContainer for decoding single values, suitable for enum associated values; unkeyedContainer for arrays, allowing iteration over elements. These containers let you flexibly extract values according to the data structure during custom decoding, fulfilling complex JSON parsing needs.

1aa2f8445642.name: How to use Swift Codable generics to wrap paginated JSON with a fixed format?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can define a generic struct PageEntity<E: Decodable> containing count, offset, limit, and results:[E]. This way, different data types can use the same generic structure to wrap paginated results, enhancing reusability. When decoding, specify PageEntity<SongEntity>.self to parse paginated song data.

1aa2f8445642.name: What is the difference between Codable's decodeIfPresent and decode?

acceptedAnswer.text: decodeIfPresent only attempts to decode if the JSON contains the field; the field can be Optional, and missing fields won't cause an error. decode requires the field to be present and throws an error if missing. decodeIfPresent is suitable for optional or potentially missing fields to avoid decode failures.

724a7fb9a364.name: Can Google Site be linked to a custom domain? How to set it up?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Site supports linking to a custom domain. The setup process includes verifying domain ownership through Google Webmaster, adding TXT and CNAME records via DNS, then entering and assigning the custom domain in the Google Site settings page, and finally publishing the site. This process requires waiting for DNS propagation, usually less than an hour. For example, with Namecheap, add a TXT Record with host '@' and a CNAME Record with host 'www' pointing to ghs.googlehosted.com.

724a7fb9a364.name: How good is Google Site's SEO performance?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Site's SEO structure is very limited and does not allow customization of head meta tags such as description, tags, or og: tags. This results in no preview information when sharing on social media, only showing the URL and site name, which is unfavorable for improving search rankings and traffic. Currently, there are no official customization features to enhance SEO.

724a7fb9a364.name: Does Google Site automatically adjust the height of embedded code (like Gist)?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Site does not automatically adjust the height of embedded iframe code (such as Gist), which may cause double scrollbars when viewed on mobile devices, making the display unattractive and difficult to read. There are limited solutions; the best approach is to avoid using iframes that require adaptive height or to present the code using other methods.

724a7fb9a364.name: What types of websites is Google Site suitable for?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Site is suitable for creating very lightweight websites, such as school clubs, small event pages, or personal resumes. It has a simple and easy-to-use interface, supports responsive web design (RWD), and basic drag-and-drop components, but it is not suitable for websites requiring complex interactions or advanced SEO optimization.

724a7fb9a364.name: What are the convenient advantages of using Google Site?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Site features an intuitive interface where you can quickly build pages by dragging components. It supports RWD, custom page paths, embedding Google Docs and videos, linking custom domains, and integrating Google Analytics. It has low intrusiveness, with only a small floating label at the bottom left. There are no special traffic limits, and storage is counted against Google Drive space.

724a7fb9a364.name: Can the button component in Google Site be set to anchor links?

acceptedAnswer.text: The button component in Google Site cannot be set to anchor (#) links; it only accepts external links or specific internal pages. To achieve an internal page table of contents or back-to-top functionality, it is recommended to split the content into subpages because the platform currently does not support anchor navigation.

724a7fb9a364.name: Why can't embedded Google Forms be filled out directly on the Google Site page?

acceptedAnswer.text: If the Google Form includes a 'file upload' question, browser security restrictions prevent it from being embedded via iframe for direct filling on the Google Site page. It will only display form information and require clicking to open in a new window to fill out. The solution is to remove the file upload question, allowing the form to be filled out directly on the page.

724a7fb9a364.name: Can Google Site integrate with Google Analytics? How to set it up?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Site supports integration with Google Analytics. First, create an account in GA and obtain the tracking ID. Then, go to the Google Site settings page, click the gear icon to select Analytics, enter the GA tracking ID, and finally publish the site to start collecting visitor data. After setup, you can view traffic analysis in real time.

cb00b1977537.name: How does Codable distinguish between a null field and a missing field in API responses?

acceptedAnswer.text: Codable itself cannot distinguish whether a field is null or missing, because decodeIfPresent treats both cases as nil. The solution is to use a custom OptionalValue enum that checks if the field exists using the Decoder's contains method, then decodes it as .value or .null accordingly. This way, you can differentiate between a field explicitly set to null (meaning clear the field) and a field that is missing (meaning do not update the field).

cb00b1977537.name: Can using double optional (String??) in Swift Codable solve the problem of null versus missing fields?

acceptedAnswer.text: In theory, a double Optional (String??) can distinguish three states, but JSONDecoder actually uses decodeIfPresent, which treats both null and missing fields as nil, so it cannot differentiate. Practical tests show it behaves the same as a single Optional, so double optional does not solve this issue.

cb00b1977537.name: How to handle API fields that use 0/1 to represent Bool in Codable?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can override the struct's init(from:) to first try decoding an Int, then convert 1 to true and anything else to false; if decoding Int fails, then decode Bool. This approach supports both 0/1 and true/false representations from the API. For example, decodeIfPresent(Int.self) is used to assign the Bool property after checking the value.

cb00b1977537.name: Why do I get a keyNotFound error when wrapping Codable fields with Property Wrappers?

acceptedAnswer.text: Currently, Swift Codable has limited support for Property Wrappers. If a field is wrapped, the API response must include that key; otherwise, a keyNotFound error is thrown. This applies even if the field is Optional. This is a known issue by Apple and may be fixed in the future. When using libraries like BetterCodable or CodableWrappers, be aware of this limitation.

cb00b1977537.name: How to customize decodeIfPresent behavior using a KeyedDecodingContainer extension?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can extend KeyedDecodingContainer's decodeIfPresent method to force decoding for specific types (e.g., OptionalValue<T>), preventing decodeIfPresent from returning nil immediately when encountering null or missing fields. This allows you to detect whether a field is null or missing and also enables overriding Bool decoding to support 0/1 values.

cb00b1977537.name: How to perform incremental updates on Core Data Entities after Codable decoding?

acceptedAnswer.text: By wrapping fields with an OptionalValue enum, you can determine during decoding whether a field is null, present, or missing. When updating Core Data, if the field is .value, update the data; if .null, clear the field; if nil, leave it unchanged. This allows incremental updates based on the API response without accidentally deleting or missing updates.

cb00b1977537.name: Why does Codable's strictness limit development flexibility?

acceptedAnswer.text: Codable enforces a fixed structure and strict decoding behavior, making it difficult to distinguish between null and missing fields and limiting custom decoding logic. This often requires rewriting init(from:) or using complex workarounds during development. These constraints make Codable convenient but less flexible for complex APIs or incremental update scenarios.

8a04443024e2.name: Why do so many iOS apps read the clipboard when opened?

acceptedAnswer.text: Many iOS apps read the clipboard upon opening to implement the iOS Deferred Deep Link feature, enhancing user experience. This allows users to automatically jump to the page they were just browsing on the web after installing the app, avoiding the need to search again or return to the webpage. This practice increases purchase conversion rates and helps with marketing analytics.

8a04443024e2.name: Why does iOS 14 show a prompt when the clipboard is accessed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Starting with iOS 14, the system displays a prompt when an app reads the clipboard to inform users when their clipboard is being accessed. This is a privacy protection measure by Apple to prevent apps from secretly reading sensitive information. The prompt helps users be aware of unreasonable access behavior and improves privacy transparency.

8a04443024e2.name: How did iOS 16 change app behavior regarding clipboard access?

acceptedAnswer.text: From iOS 16 onwards, if an app reads the clipboard without a user-initiated paste action, a permission dialog will appear, and the user must allow access. This restricts apps from automatically reading the clipboard in the background, further protecting user privacy and preventing unauthorized data access.

8a04443024e2.name: Are there alternatives to using the clipboard for implementing deferred deep links?

acceptedAnswer.text: An alternative is using the Branch.io service, which matches users by recording IP and device information, but it requires significant backend and database investment and may result in false matches. Since Apple blocks cookie reading after iOS 10, the clipboard has become the last resort.

8a04443024e2.name: Why is TikTok's claim that clipboard access is to prevent spam messages unreasonable?

acceptedAnswer.text: TikTok claims clipboard access is to prevent spam messages, but in reality, checking can be done when sending a message without continuously monitoring the clipboard. This reason seems like an excuse to deflect public opinion, as clipboard content may not be related to ads or sensitive messages, and continuous reading infringes on privacy.

8a04443024e2.name: How can developers avoid being prompted to access the clipboard every time the app opens?

acceptedAnswer.text: Developers can first check if the clipboard content is a URL before deciding to read it, reducing unnecessary reads. Apple also provides the DetectPattern API to help accurately identify clipboard content, making prompts more focused. This reduces the frequency of prompts and enhances user trust.

8a04443024e2.name: Does reading the clipboard risk apps stealing passwords or personal information?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is possible because the clipboard may contain passwords or personal privacy information. If an app reads it and sends it back to a server, it can be abused. Whether it is safe depends on the developer's integrity. Man-in-the-middle sniffing can monitor if the app transmits clipboard data.

41c49a75a743.name: How to use Swift in Xcode to write a Run Script that checks for multilingual file errors?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can download the Localize.swift tool from the freshOS team, place it in your project directory, then add a Run Script in Xcode's Build Phases pointing to Localize.swift. Edit the settings inside, such as the localization file directory and the main language, to automatically check for missing, duplicate, or untranslated multilingual files during build. Using Swift for the script is easier to manage than shell scripts.

41c49a75a743.name: Why compile the Swift Run Script into an executable before adding it to Xcode Build Phases?

acceptedAnswer.text: Compiling into an executable greatly reduces build time because the Swift script doesn't need to be recompiled every time. Tests show it can save about 90% of execution time. The compile command is swiftc -o Localize Localize.swift, and after compiling, change the Build Phases to run the executable path.

41c49a75a743.name: How does the Asset Checker tool written in Swift help me check image resources in an Xcode project?

acceptedAnswer.text: Asset Checker.swift scans the image names used in the project code and compares them with the images in the .xcassets directory. During build, it automatically reports missing images (Build Error) or unused images (Build Warning). It supports parameters to set the project and resource directories, making it easy to automate checking that image resources are complete and without redundancy.

41c49a75a743.name: How to customize regular expressions in Swift Run Script to match complex NSLocalizedString statements?

acceptedAnswer.text: The default NSLocalizedString regex only matches simple keys like "Home.Title" and cannot match formatted parameters or full sentences. You can modify the patterns setting to "NSLocalized(Format)?String\\(\\s*@?\"([^(")]+)\"" to extend matching to any string until the closing quote, avoiding false positives for unused statements. You can debug this specifically using tools like rubular.

41c49a75a743.name: How does Swift Run Script check if Localizable.strings file format is correct?

acceptedAnswer.text: The script can use the shell() function to run the shell command plutil -lint to check the .strings file format. If the format is wrong (e.g., missing semicolon), plutil returns non-OK, and the script prints error messages and shows an Error in the build. This feature needs to be added manually in Localize.swift, with example code around line 135.

41c49a75a743.name: How does a Swift-written Run Script display error or warning messages during build?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the Swift script, use print() to output strings in a specific format. The Error format is "file path:line number: error: error message", and the Warning format is "file path:line number: warning: warning message". Xcode automatically parses these and marks them in the build panel for easy error location.

41c49a75a743.name: How to exclude certain statements from being considered unused in Swift Run Script?

acceptedAnswer.text: Localize.swift has an ignoredFromUnusedKeys array where you can add keys you want to ignore, preventing those statements from being mistakenly marked as unused. This is useful for dynamically concatenated or special-use keys to avoid false positive errors.

eab0e984043.name: Is the blood oxygen feature on Apple Watch Series 6 accurate?

acceptedAnswer.text: The blood oxygen feature on Apple Watch Series 6 is not medically certified and is for reference only. It can quickly measure blood oxygen levels, suitable for daily health monitoring, but it is not recommended for medical diagnosis. For example, although you can see changes in blood oxygen values, professional medical equipment should be relied upon if there are health abnormalities.

eab0e984043.name: What is the difference between the Apple Watch Series 6 GPS and LTE versions?

acceptedAnswer.text: The GPS version requires the phone to be nearby or the watch to be connected to a previously recorded WiFi to communicate and send emergency alerts. The LTE version can operate independently, allowing calls and internet use without the phone. The LTE version is better suited for users who need independent communication for safety, such as during outdoor sports or emergencies.

eab0e984043.name: Is Apple Watch Series 6 suitable for people who buy it just for exercise?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apple Watch Series 6 is geared towards comprehensive needs and enhancing lifestyle experience. Its exercise features are fun and useful for tracking but won't motivate people who dislike exercise to start working out. If purely for exercise, a professional sports watch is recommended. It is suitable for users who want health monitoring and phone extension features.

eab0e984043.name: How is the battery life of Apple Watch Series 6?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apple Watch Series 6 has a battery life of about one day per charge and cannot be used continuously for long periods. Users typically wear it when going out and charge it at home, not wearing it during sleep. This is the current norm for Apple Watch series, and users need to get used to daily charging.

eab0e984043.name: How to choose the Apple Watch Series 6 band size and model?

acceptedAnswer.text: Band sizes are divided into large (44mm and above) and small (40mm and below). Band sizes have not changed since Apple Watch Series 4, and bands from Series 1 to 6 are interchangeable. Men are generally recommended to choose 44mm, while those with slimmer wrists may consider 40mm. When purchasing, also consider the band size after adding a protective case.

eab0e984043.name: What is the difference between the aluminum and stainless steel versions of Apple Watch Series 6?

acceptedAnswer.text: The aluminum version is lighter and less expensive but has a more fragile body and screen, so it is recommended to buy a protective case and screen protector; the stainless steel version is more durable and has a more attractive appearance, suitable as a fashion accessory. The aluminum version has higher demand in the second-hand market and is easier to resell.

eab0e984043.name: What convenient lifestyle features does Apple Watch Series 6 offer to improve focus?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apple Watch uses gentle notification sounds and vibrations to filter phone notifications, avoiding frequent phone checking and distractions. Users can quickly view notifications to decide whether to address them, reducing interruptions and improving focus. For example, only important app notifications are set to appear on the watch to avoid unnecessary disturbances.

eab0e984043.name: How to use Apple Watch Series 6 with the Apple ecosystem?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apple Watch can unlock your Mac, serve as a Bluetooth remote shutter button, help locate your phone remotely, and support Apple Pay payments, eliminating the hassle of taking out your phone. Users can have the store scan a digital receipt via a phone widget first, then quickly pay with the watch, enhancing daily convenience.

c0f99f987d9c.name: What are the advantages of the magnetic clasp on the Apple Watch original stainless steel Milanese loop?

acceptedAnswer.text: The magnetic clasp on the Apple Watch original stainless steel Milanese loop allows for infinite adjustment of the band size. The magnet is strong and not easy to loosen, making the band fit the wrist better and easy to wear. This design enhances wearing comfort and allows for quick adjustments, making it suitable for daily use.

c0f99f987d9c.name: Does the Apple Watch Milanese loop catch hair?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Apple Watch Milanese loop can catch hair, which is one of its drawbacks. Whether original or third-party Milanese loops, users commonly report hair getting caught due to the woven mesh design of the band, so caution is advised during use.

c0f99f987d9c.name: What wrist sizes are suitable for the Apple Watch Milanese loop?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Apple Watch 40 mm Milanese loop fits wrists measuring 130–180 mm, while the 44 mm band fits wrists 150–200 mm. The band length is a single piece and cannot be shortened, so people with smaller wrists are recommended to choose the 40 mm band or try it on before deciding.

c0f99f987d9c.name: Is there a big difference between the Apple Watch original Milanese loop and third-party bands?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main differences between original and third-party Milanese loops lie in details and craftsmanship. The original has finer weaving, is a single molded piece, has a strong magnet that doesn’t loosen easily, and does not have a metallic rust smell. However, it costs about 3,100 TWD, several times more than third-party options. Third-party bands cost about 1,000–2,000 TWD and have quality close to the original. It’s recommended to feel the actual product before deciding.

c0f99f987d9c.name: Is the Apple Watch original stainless steel Milanese loop durable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Apple Watch original stainless steel Milanese loop is durable and corrosion-resistant. After nearly 5 years of use, there is no obvious wear, rust, or unpleasant odor. This band is one of the most durable the author has used, suitable for long-term wear and easy maintenance.

c0f99f987d9c.name: What breathability and comfort features does the Apple Watch Milanese loop have?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Apple Watch Milanese loop is made of stainless steel mesh, which is breathable and quick-drying, preventing unpleasant odors. The mesh structure allows the wrist to breathe, making it suitable for long-term wear. The magnetic clasp can be adjusted freely to improve comfort.

c0f99f987d9c.name: Does the weight of the Apple Watch Milanese loop affect the wearing experience?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Apple Watch Milanese loop is heavier than bands made from other materials, which is one of its drawbacks. The heavier band may feel burdensome to some users, but it also provides better texture and durability, making it suitable for those who prefer a formal style.

c4d7c2ce5a8d.name: What is the difference between Version Number and Build Number in an iOS APP?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Version Number is used for identification by the App Store and users, usually in the format x.y.z, and it can only increment upwards without repetition or decrease. The Build Number is an internal development identifier used to distinguish versions when uploading to the App Store; Build Numbers cannot repeat under the same Version Number. The Version Number is displayed externally, while the Build Number is mainly for internal management.

c4d7c2ce5a8d.name: Why is it recommended to use semantic versioning x.y.z format for iOS APP version numbers?

acceptedAnswer.text: Semantic versioning x.y.z clearly expresses compatibility and the scope of changes between versions. 'x' represents incompatible major changes, 'y' is for backward-compatible feature additions, and 'z' is for backward-compatible bug fixes. This helps avoid version management confusion and aligns with official recommendations. For example: 1.0.0 indicates the initial official release, 2.1.0 indicates added features with compatibility, and 2.1.1 indicates bug fixes.

c4d7c2ce5a8d.name: How to compare two iOS APP version numbers in Swift?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can use the String compare method with the .numeric option to compare version strings, for example, "1.0.0".compare("1.22.0", options: .numeric) returns .orderedAscending, indicating the first is smaller. However, the format must be consistent to avoid misjudgments, such as comparing "1.0.0" with "1" which can be incorrect. It is recommended to keep the version format fixed as x.y.z.

c4d7c2ce5a8d.name: What happens if the iOS APP version number format does not comply with App Store requirements?

acceptedAnswer.text: If the version number format does not meet the requirements, although you can build and run the app, you cannot package and upload it to the App Store. The version number can only contain digits and dots ('.'), and must not exceed 18 characters. Incorrect formatting will cause rejection during app submission, and you must correct the version number format to successfully submit.

c4d7c2ce5a8d.name: Can the Build Number of an iOS APP be reused?

acceptedAnswer.text: Build Numbers cannot be duplicated under the same Version Number because the App Store uses the Build Number to distinguish packaged versions. However, the same Build Number can be used under different Version Numbers. For example, Build 1.0.0 under Version 1.0.0 and Build 1.0.0 under Version 1.1.0 are both allowed.

c4d7c2ce5a8d.name: What does the ~> operator mean in semantic version comparison?

acceptedAnswer.text: ~> is a pessimistic version constraint operator that means greater than or equal to the specified version but less than the next major version. For example, ~> 1.2.1 means the version must be >= 1.2.1 and < 1.3.0, allowing minor fixes and minor version updates but excluding major version jumps. This is used in tools like Cocoapods for specifying version ranges conveniently.

c4d7c2ce5a8d.name: Why can't iOS APP version numbers start with 0.y.z?

acceptedAnswer.text: Version numbers starting with 0.y.z usually indicate an early development stage and have not reached the official release phase. Official guidelines recommend avoiding starting version numbers with 0 for formal releases because it signifies the software is still in development and is not suitable as an official release identifier. Official versions are recommended to start from 1.0.0.

ee47f8f1e2d2.name: How to use AVAssetResourceLoaderDelegate with AVPlayer to cache music or videos while playing?

acceptedAnswer.text: By implementing AVAssetResourceLoaderDelegate with AVPlayer and AVURLAsset, you can intercept resource requests to enable caching while playing. This approach allows the player to download and store data simultaneously during playback, preventing redundant downloads. For example, by intercepting network resource requests through the delegate and writing data to local cache, playback smoothness is improved.

ee47f8f1e2d2.name: What common issues should be noted when implementing caching while playing with AVPlayer?

acceptedAnswer.text: When implementing, ensure that AVAssetResourceLoaderDelegate correctly intercepts and returns data to avoid playback anomalies caused by incorrect cached data. The article author initially made errors due to insufficient research and later revised about 30% of the content to fix these issues. In practice, it's important to handle data synchronization and error management properly to ensure consistency between playback and caching.

ee47f8f1e2d2.name: Why use AVQueuePlayer instead of just AVPlayer for caching while playing?

acceptedAnswer.text: AVQueuePlayer supports playing multiple items in a queue, making it suitable for continuous playback of multiple songs or videos. When combined with caching, it enhances user experience. Compared to a single AVPlayer, AVQueuePlayer offers more flexible queue management, making the caching mechanism more efficient during continuous playback. For example, when playing a music playlist, caching the next track reduces waiting time.

ee47f8f1e2d2.name: Is there a complete example code for AVPlayer caching while playing?

acceptedAnswer.text: The article author later wrote a complete project example, including implementation details of AVPlayer and AVAssetResourceLoaderDelegate, which developers can directly reference. These examples cover intercepting resource requests, data caching, and playback flow, making it easy to get started quickly. You can refer to the link provided by the author to obtain the full source code.

ee47f8f1e2d2.name: How much technical change has there been in AVPlayer caching while playing?

acceptedAnswer.text: When updating the article, about 30% of the content was modified, and approximately 60% new information was added, indicating significant adjustments in technical details and implementation methods. This means the original implementation had errors or shortcomings, and after thorough revision, a more accurate and detailed caching solution was provided.

ee47f8f1e2d2.name: What iOS application scenarios are suitable for AVPlayer caching while playing?

acceptedAnswer.text: This technique is suitable for iOS applications that play streaming music or videos and want to reduce redundant downloads, such as music players or video playback apps. By caching while playing, user experience is improved, network traffic is reduced, and playback latency is minimized. For example, a music app can cache upcoming tracks during playback to avoid stuttering when skipping songs.

6ce488898003.name: How does AVPlayer use AVAssetResourceLoaderDelegate to implement local caching?

acceptedAnswer.text: AVPlayer uses AVAssetResourceLoaderDelegate to let a custom Resource Loader handle resource requests. The process starts by changing the URL scheme to a custom 'cacheable' scheme, so AVURLAsset will use the Resource Loader. The Resource Loader determines whether the request is for file info or data, first returning from local cache if available; if insufficient, it sends a URLSession request to fetch and cache network data. This allows playback and caching simultaneously without affecting the native player mechanism.

6ce488898003.name: Why use HTTP/1.1 Range requests to implement AVPlayer's caching strategy?

acceptedAnswer.text: HTTP/1.1 Range requests support retrieving specific segments of a file, avoiding downloading the entire large file at once. AVPlayer uses Range requests to fetch data while playing, enabling real-time playback and segmented caching. The server responds with 206 Partial Content, so the player only needs the data for the currently playing segment, saving bandwidth and reducing wait time.

6ce488898003.name: Why does AVPlayer issue cancel network requests?

acceptedAnswer.text: AVPlayer cancels the current request from 0 to the total file length after obtaining enough data to avoid unnecessary data usage. When playback continues, it issues new Range requests based on the playback progress. During seek operations, it cancels old requests first and requests the new playback segment. This mechanism prevents the player from downloading unnecessary data.

6ce488898003.name: What are the limitations of using PINCache as local cache?

acceptedAnswer.text: PINCache is suitable for caching small files, such as music files around 10 MB. Since PINCache loads the entire file into memory, large files (like videos several GBs) can cause memory overflow (OOM). For large files, it is recommended to use FileHandle with seek read/write to avoid loading the entire file at once.

6ce488898003.name: How does AVAssetResourceLoaderDelegate decide whether to get data from local cache or network?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Resource Loader first checks the local cache for continuous data within the requested range (cachedPrefix) and returns it to the player if available. If the local data is incomplete, it then issues URLSession network requests for the missing segments. This prioritizes cached data, reduces redundant downloads, and improves playback efficiency.

6ce488898003.name: Why must the URL scheme be changed to a custom 'cacheable' to use Resource Loader?

acceptedAnswer.text: AVPlayer only calls AVAssetResourceLoaderDelegate when the system cannot directly recognize or handle the scheme. The http/https schemes are natively supported by the system and do not use the Resource Loader. Changing the scheme to 'cacheable' makes the system unrecognizable, forcing it to use the Resource Loader, allowing interception and implementation of custom caching logic.

6ce488898003.name: Why doesn't AVQueuePlayer automatically cancel the previous resource's download when switching playback items?

acceptedAnswer.text: AVQueuePlayer queues multiple playback items and does not automatically cancel the previous resource's download request when switching. This is because AVQueuePlayer retains the previous resource's AVURLAsset, so the download continues. You need to manually listen for playback item change notifications and call cancelLoading() on the resource to cancel downloads that are no longer needed.

6ce488898003.name: How to avoid playback issues caused by merging non-continuous data during AVPlayer caching?

acceptedAnswer.text: Initially, only continuous data merging is handled to avoid complexity and playback glitches caused by non-continuous segments. Later, a generic Rangeable container manages cached byte ranges, automatically merging adjacent or overlapping segments and querying missing segments. This makes caching more precise and prevents playback abnormalities.

948ed34efa09.name: How does an iOS app automatically fill in saved website account passwords to speed up the login process?

acceptedAnswer.text: By configuring Associated Domains and uploading the apple-app-site-association file, the app can be linked with the website and use the Password AutoFill feature to automatically fill in saved account passwords. This way, when users enter the login page in the app, a saved password option from the website appears above the keyboard, speeding up login. For example: set webcredentials:google.com and upload the corresponding file.

948ed34efa09.name: What is the difference between Password AutoFill and Shared Web Credentials?

acceptedAnswer.text: Password AutoFill is a keyboard autofill feature supported since iOS 11, while Shared Web Credentials is an API available since iOS 8 that allows directly reading, adding, modifying, and deleting website credentials. Shared Web Credentials offers more complete functionality with programmatic management of credentials and requires the Password AutoFill environment to be set up first. For example: Shared Web Credentials can add credentials using SecAddSharedWebCredential.

948ed34efa09.name: How to use AuthenticationServices to read website account passwords on iOS 14 and later?

acceptedAnswer.text: Starting with iOS 14, SecRequestSharedWebCredential is deprecated. Instead, use the AuthenticationServices framework's ASAuthorizationController with ASAuthorizationPasswordRequest to read account passwords. Through delegate methods, obtain an ASPasswordCredential and fill the account and password fields. For example: call controller.performRequests() in viewDidLoad to trigger the authorization process.

948ed34efa09.name: How to configure the app and website to use the Password AutoFill feature?

acceptedAnswer.text: You need to add Associated Domains in the Signing & Capabilities section of your Xcode project, setting webcredentials:yourwebsite.com, and upload the apple-app-site-association file containing the team ID and bundle ID to the website root directory or /.well-known. This allows recognition between the app and website to enable Password AutoFill. For example: webcredentials:google.com and upload the apple-app-site-association file.

948ed34efa09.name: How to programmatically add or modify iOS Shared Web Credentials account passwords?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use the SecAddSharedWebCredential function, passing the domain, account, and password. If the password is nil, it means deleting that account credential; if the account exists and a password is provided, it modifies the credential; if the account does not exist and a password is provided, it adds a new credential. Each modification triggers a system prompt for user confirmation. For example: SecAddSharedWebCredential("google.com", "user@example.com", "newpass").

948ed34efa09.name: What is the password format generated by the iOS password generator SecCreateSharedWebCredentialPassword()?

acceptedAnswer.text: SecCreateSharedWebCredentialPassword() generates passwords containing uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, separated by hyphens "-", for example, Jpn-4t2-gaF-dYk. This format balances strength and readability, making it easier for users to remember or copy.

948ed34efa09.name: Why don't AuthenticationServices read account passwords show data from third-party password managers?

acceptedAnswer.text: Because third-party password managers like 1Password and LastPass do not integrate their data into the iOS system keychain, AuthenticationServices and SecRequestSharedWebCredential can only read credentials stored in the system keychain. They cannot display or access data from third-party tools. This is a current limitation and direct support for third-party password managers is not available.

12c5026da33d.name: How is the apple-app-site-association file updated after iOS 14?

acceptedAnswer.text: After iOS 14, the apple-app-site-association file is cached and periodically updated by Apple's CDN. The app fetches this file from Apple's CDN upon first installation or update. The official details on the update frequency are not clearly stated, but it is estimated to update within about 48 hours at the latest. Therefore, it is recommended to modify the file a few days before releasing or updating the app.

12c5026da33d.name: How to instantly update apple-app-site-association for debugging during development on iOS 14 and above?

acceptedAnswer.text: During development on iOS 14 and above, you can append ?mode=developer to the applinks URL in the Associated Domains entitlement. The app running on the simulator will fetch the latest apple-app-site-association file from the website every time you Build & Run. For testing on a real device, you need to enable the "Associated Domains Development" option in Settings -> Developer.

12c5026da33d.name: In which directories can the apple-app-site-association file for Universal Links be placed?

acceptedAnswer.text: The apple-app-site-association file can be placed either in the root directory of the website or under the .well-known directory. However, when using mode=developer, the system only requests the file from .well-known/app-site-association. Therefore, it is recommended to place it in the .well-known directory during development to ensure instant updates.

12c5026da33d.name: How to set up a local testing environment for Universal Links?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can install nginx on your Mac, configure the root directory, and start a service on port 8080. Place the apple-app-site-association file inside the .well-known folder under the root directory. Then use ngrok to expose the local service as a public URL. Append ?mode=developer to this URL and add it to the applinks entitlement, making it convenient to test during development.

12c5026da33d.name: How to exclude specific paths in Universal Links' paths or components settings?

acceptedAnswer.text: Before iOS 13, you could exclude specific paths by adding "NOT /help" in paths. From iOS 13 onwards, exclusion is done in components by setting exclude: true, for example: { "/": "/help/website/*", "exclude": true }. This way, URLs matching the condition will not open the app as Universal Links.

12c5026da33d.name: Which URL elements matching are supported in Universal Links settings after iOS 13?

acceptedAnswer.text: After iOS 13, the settings support matching on path ("/"), query ("?"), and anchor ("#") URL elements, which can be combined. Wildcards such as *, ?, and ?* are supported, along with options for case sensitivity and automatic encoding, allowing more precise matching and easier handling of URLs with Chinese characters.

12c5026da33d.name: How does the apple-app-site-association file support multilingual URLs for Universal Links?

acceptedAnswer.text: Using the default character sets introduced in iOS 13 and later, such as $(lang) and $(region), you can write components like "/$(lang)-$(region)/$(food)/home". For example, /zh-TW/home or /en-US/home will both match, making it convenient to support multilingual URLs with Universal Links.

12c5026da33d.name: How to verify the version of apple-app-site-association on Apple CDN?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can send a GET request with the Host header to https://app-site-association.cdn-apple.com/a/v1/yourdomain to check the current apple-app-site-association content on Apple CDN. This helps confirm whether the file has been updated, preventing the app from using an outdated version.

87090f101b9a.name: How to install and start the Laravel Homestead environment?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, use git clone to download Homestead, switch to the release branch, and run init.sh to create the configuration file. Then edit Homestead.yaml to configure the virtual machine parameters and directory mappings. Finally, run vagrant up --provision in ~/Homestead to start the virtual machine. Adding --provision ensures the configuration file is updated, preventing the 'no input file specified' error.

87090f101b9a.name: How to configure phpMyAdmin to connect to MySQL inside Homestead?

acceptedAnswer.text: After downloading phpMyAdmin, place the folder inside ~/Homestead, and modify the config.inc.php configuration file by setting auth_type to 'config', user to 'homestead', and password to 'secret'. This is because Homestead's default MySQL username and password are homestead/secret, allowing phpMyAdmin to connect successfully.

87090f101b9a.name: How to configure folders and sites in Homestead.yaml to allow local access to phpMyAdmin?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the folders section, add a map from ~/Homestead/phpMyAdmin to /home/vagrant/phpMyAdmin. In the sites section, add a map from phpMyAdmin.test to /home/vagrant/phpMyAdmin. After this setup, add '192.168.10.10 phpMyAdmin.test' to your local hosts file to access http://phpMyAdmin.test via the browser.

87090f101b9a.name: Why install Composer on Mac when Homestead already provides a PHP environment?

acceptedAnswer.text: Homestead provides a complete PHP development environment, but some PHP projects may not use Laravel or Homestead. Installing Composer directly on Mac makes it easier to manage PHP dependencies. When installing, note that the hash checksum in Composer's official download command changes with versions, so obtain the latest command from the official website to avoid installation failure.

87090f101b9a.name: What settings should be noted when using VirtualBox with Homestead to ensure it starts properly?

acceptedAnswer.text: After installing VirtualBox, the system will require a reboot and you must go to Mac's 'System Preferences' → 'Security & Privacy' to allow VirtualBox's extension; otherwise, not all services can be enabled. This step is necessary for the virtual machine to function properly, and missing it will cause startup failure.

87090f101b9a.name: How to create a Laravel project in Homestead and set up a local test domain?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, use vagrant ssh to enter Homestead, cd to /home/vagrant/code, and run laravel new project-name to create a new project. Then, add a map in the sites section of Homestead.yaml: myblog.test pointing to the project's public directory. Add '192.168.10.10 myblog.test' to your local hosts file. Finally, run vagrant reload --provision to reload the configuration, and you can access it via the browser.

87090f101b9a.name: Why must --provision be added when running vagrant up?

acceptedAnswer.text: Adding --provision forces Vagrant to re-run the setup scripts, ensuring that configuration files like Homestead.yaml are updated and take effect. Without it, settings won't update, causing domain mapping errors and the browser may show a 'no input file specified' error message.

70a1409b149a.name: Why use Proxyman to perform a Man in the Middle attack to sniff APP API?

acceptedAnswer.text: Proxyman is used to unpack HTTPS traffic, helping you find the API endpoints and parameters the APP calls during check-in. This allows you to simulate these requests with Python to complete automatic check-ins. After installing the Root certificate, Proxyman intercepts mobile traffic and displays the original Request and Response for easy analysis.

70a1409b149a.name: How to simulate the APP's API check-in requests with a Python script?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use Python's requests package to send POST requests, including the necessary headers and data sniffed from Proxyman. Set action=checkIn in the code and include authentication info like Cookie and AuthToken. Receiving a 200 status code means the check-in was successful.

70a1409b149a.name: How to receive Python script execution result notifications via Line Bot?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, create a Messaging API channel in the Line Developers Console and obtain your User ID and Channel access token. The Python script uses HTTP POST to call the Line Messaging API, including the message content and authorization token, to send execution result notifications to yourself.

70a1409b149a.name: How to deploy a Python script to Google Cloud Function and set up HTTP trigger?

acceptedAnswer.text: Create a Cloud Function in the Google Cloud Console, select HTTP trigger, and allow unauthenticated calls. Upload the Python code, specify the main function as the entry point, and add the requests package in requirements.txt. After successful deployment, you will get a trigger URL that you can click to execute the script.

70a1409b149a.name: How to set environment variables for a Python script in Google Cloud Function?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the Cloud Function creation or edit page, you can set environment variables. The Python program reads them using os.environ.get('variable_name'). This avoids hardcoding sensitive data and makes management and updates easier.

70a1409b149a.name: How to use Firebase Cloud Firestore to store and read tokens for the check-in APP?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, create a Firebase project and enable Cloud Firestore. Add collections and documents to store tokens. Download the GCP service account JSON key, then initialize with the firebase-admin package in Python to read and write Firestore. This allows dynamic token management and prevents expiration issues.

70a1409b149a.name: How to schedule automatic execution of Python scripts with Google Cloud Scheduler?

acceptedAnswer.text: Create a job in Cloud Scheduler, enter the trigger URL (the Cloud Function HTTP endpoint), set the time zone and crontab syntax (e.g., daily at 1:15 AM), and select HTTP method GET. Once done, Scheduler will call the Cloud Function regularly to automatically run the script.

70a1409b149a.name: Will automating scripts with Google Cloud and Line Bot be expensive?
