acceptedAnswer.text: Google Cloud Functions offers 2 million free invocations per month, Line Bot provides 500 free messages monthly, and Firebase Cloud Firestore and Cloud Scheduler also have free quotas. Light usage typically incurs no cost unless there are frequent calls or large data traffic.

142244e5f07a.name: Why can't password reset tokens be designed using serial numbers?

acceptedAnswer.text: Designing password reset tokens with serial numbers makes them easy to predict. Attackers can calculate the next serial number to obtain a valid token, bypass verification, and reset others' passwords. The article found that tokens were consecutive numbers, allowing attackers to successfully predict and exploit this vulnerability to compromise accounts.

142244e5f07a.name: Why shouldn't websites reuse the same key for encryption and decryption functions?

acceptedAnswer.text: Reusing the same key can lead to it being exploited externally, compromising data security. The article points out that the website had an arbitrary encryption/decryption endpoint, exposing the key. Attackers could use this key to decrypt password reset tokens, causing serious security risks.

142244e5f07a.name: Why does the website backend need to validate user input a second time?

acceptedAnswer.text: Backend secondary validation prevents malicious input that bypasses frontend restrictions, stopping attackers from exploiting incorrect formats or illegal data to intrude. The article mentions the website only validated email format on the frontend but not on the backend, allowing attackers to successfully modify emails and bypass restrictions.

142244e5f07a.name: What security risks arise from lacking two-factor authentication?

acceptedAnswer.text: Without two-factor authentication, accounts are easily impersonated. Once a password is reset or stolen, attackers can directly log into the account. In the article's case, after resetting the password, the absence of two-factor protection allowed attackers to successfully impersonate the target account.

142244e5f07a.name: Why does publicly exposing user emails increase the risk of password reset vulnerabilities?

acceptedAnswer.text: Publicly available emails make it easy for attackers to obtain target account information. Combined with predictable password reset tokens, attackers can easily initiate the reset process and compromise accounts. The article identifies public emails as one of the key factors triggering the vulnerability.

142244e5f07a.name: How should password reset tokens be generated to ensure security?

acceptedAnswer.text: Tokens should be randomly generated and uniquely identifiable strings that are hard to predict or reuse. The article recommends using random unique tokens instead of serial numbers to prevent attackers from calculating valid tokens.

142244e5f07a.name: How can websites prevent attackers from exploiting arbitrary encryption and decryption functions?

acceptedAnswer.text: The use of encryption and decryption functions should be restricted to prevent arbitrary external operations, and different keys should be used to separate functionalities. The article points out that the website's reuse of the same key and presence of arbitrary encryption/decryption endpoints led to key compromise and security risks.

d9a95d4224ea.name: How to use Medium's custom domain feature now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Medium's custom domain feature was reopened in 2024. Paid members can enter their own domain name and bind it in the settings page. The setup process includes adding an A Record pointing to the IP provided by Medium on your domain registrar platform (such as Namecheap), then completing the binding on Medium's settings page. DNS propagation can take up to 48 hours, with a minimum of about 15 minutes.

d9a95d4224ea.name: Why does Medium's custom domain feature have on-and-off availability?

acceptedAnswer.text: Since Medium first launched the custom domain feature in 2012, it has been turned off due to business considerations, reopened in 2021, announced to close again in 2022, and returned once more in 2024. The main reason is that custom domains reduce Medium's brand recognition and affect platform consistency.

d9a95d4224ea.name: What SEO impacts does setting up a Medium custom domain have?

acceptedAnswer.text: After using a custom domain, you will initially lose the high Domain Authority of medium.com, which may cause a drop in search traffic. However, in the long term, you can build authority for your own domain, which is beneficial if you plan to migrate platforms or self-host a website in the future. If you only set the Profile domain and not the Publication domain, you can retain both Medium's original traffic and the advantages of a custom domain.

d9a95d4224ea.name: Which creators are suitable for Medium's custom domain feature?

acceptedAnswer.text: Custom domains are suitable for creators who already own their own website or domain, especially those with a certain level of Domain Authority. If you want to build a community and rely on the Medium platform, using a custom subdomain (such as blog.musicplayer.com) is more appropriate, balancing brand recognition and SEO.

d9a95d4224ea.name: How to set up a domain bought on Namecheap for Medium?

acceptedAnswer.text: After logging into Namecheap, go to Domain List to find your domain, switch to Advanced DNS, and add two A Record entries. For the host name, enter the subdomain (such as www or blog), and for the IP, enter the address provided by Medium. After completing this, return to Medium's settings page to continue binding.

d9a95d4224ea.name: Will canceling Medium paid membership affect the custom domain?

acceptedAnswer.text: Even if your Medium paid membership expires, the custom domain will remain valid and continue to function normally. This means the custom domain you have set up will not become invalid due to membership cancellation, so users can use it with confidence.

d9a95d4224ea.name: How is the cost of Medium's custom domain feature calculated?

acceptedAnswer.text: Medium has removed the original one-time $75 setup fee and now includes the custom domain feature as part of the paid membership (either $5 per month or $50 per year). The domain registration cost depends on the registrar and the domain extension; a common .com domain typically costs around 200 to 500 TWD per year.

5ea3311119d8.name: Why did the article output decrease but article performance improve when managing Medium in 2020?

acceptedAnswer.text: The decrease in article output in 2020 was mainly because the author experienced a major setback and paused updates for three to four months, but focused on article quality rather than quantity, resulting in better article performance than before. This quality-over-quantity strategy enhanced the impact of each article, showing clear progress.

5ea3311119d8.name: How to embed color-highlighted code in Medium articles?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can use the Chrome extension Code Medium, which allows you to embed GitHub Gist code directly into Medium articles with color syntax highlighting. To use it, click the "<>" symbol at the end of a new block, enter your code on the right side, and submit.

5ea3311119d8.name: What are the limitations of Medium's official article backup?

acceptedAnswer.text: Medium's official backup only allows downloading plain text content; images remain externally linked, and embedded code cannot be directly converted to Markdown. This limits the completeness of backups and requires additional manual handling of images and code.

5ea3311119d8.name: Why is the author considering replacing the Medium subdomain with an independent domain?

acceptedAnswer.text: The author has deployed a custom domain blog.zhgchg.li to enhance personal branding but found that this might affect Google SEO performance, so testing is underway to decide whether to use it. An independent domain helps control the website but may impact traffic rankings.

5ea3311119d8.name: What tool is used to convert Medium articles to Markdown and back them up to GitHub?

acceptedAnswer.text: The author uses the Typora editor to convert Medium articles into Markdown format and plans to upload them to GitHub for backup. Typora supports easy Markdown editing, and backing up prevents data loss on the Medium platform.

5ea3311119d8.name: What useful features does the Medium Next Generation Stats extension have?

acceptedAnswer.text: This Chrome extension enhances Medium's backend statistics by aggregating daily traffic data, clearly showing the traffic composition of each article for the day, as well as statistics like new followers and claps, making it easy to track article performance.

5ea3311119d8.name: How did Medium articles perform overall in 2020?

acceptedAnswer.text: In 2020, a total of 16 articles were published, accumulating about 180,000 views, doubling the previous count; claps reached about 11,000, doubling as well; and followers surpassed 400, also doubling, indicating a significant overall improvement.

99a6cef90190.name: Why is a 4-digit SMS verification code easily brute-forced?

acceptedAnswer.text: A 4-digit verification code has only 10,000 possible combinations, so brute-forcing requires at most 10,000 attempts. Assuming each verification request takes 0.1 seconds, a single thread can try all combinations in about 16 minutes. Using multiple threads speeds this up further, resulting in high security risks.

99a6cef90190.name: Besides insufficient length, what other security issues of SMS verification codes are often overlooked?

acceptedAnswer.text: In addition to insufficient length and complexity, key issues include lack of attempt limits and excessively long validity periods. Without limiting the number of attempts, attackers can brute-force indefinitely; a long validity period increases the window of opportunity for exploitation.

99a6cef90190.name: Why is the security of SMS verification codes on the app side more prone to issues than on the web side?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apps often simplify the password reset process for user convenience, sometimes allowing login with just phone number verification. If the API lacks protections such as CAPTCHA or request limits, the risk of brute-force attacks is higher than on the web side.

99a6cef90190.name: How to implement brute-force cracking of SMS verification codes using a Python script?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use Python multithreading to send POST requests to the verification API, trying all 4-digit combinations. The script attempts codes from 0000 to 9999 in batches, sending the phone number and verification code each time. The process stops when the server returns success. In the example, the verification code is 1743.

99a6cef90190.name: What methods can improve the security of SMS verification codes?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can increase the code length (e.g., 6 digits), complexity, limit the number of attempts (invalidate after more than 3 tries), shorten the validity period, add CAPTCHA, strengthen app-side SSL Pinning and transmission encryption, and incorporate multi-factor verification such as birthdate or security questions.

99a6cef90190.name: Why does Apple's iOS Password AutoFill feature affect verification code design?

acceptedAnswer.text: iOS Password AutoFill supports automatic filling of purely numeric verification codes, but Apple has not publicly disclosed the parsing rules. It is believed to only support numeric combinations, making alphanumeric codes unsupported and thus limiting verification code complexity design.

99a6cef90190.name: What risks arise if an SMS verification code API does not set an attempt limit?

acceptedAnswer.text: If the API has no attempt limit, attackers can try all verification code combinations infinitely. Once brute-force succeeds, they can reset passwords or log into accounts, leading to account theft and extremely high security risks.

9659db1357e4.name: How does Firebase Functions help me quickly set up a test API service?

acceptedAnswer.text: Firebase Functions is a serverless service provided by Google. You only need to write JavaScript (node.js) logic, and Google will automatically manage the servers and runtime environment, eliminating concerns about server scaling and traffic management. You can write a function, deploy it, and get a URL that accepts HTTP requests and returns responses, allowing you to quickly set up a simple API.

9659db1357e4.name: Why use Firebase Firestore with Functions to create an API?

acceptedAnswer.text: Firebase Firestore is a NoSQL database. Combined with Functions, you can directly access and manipulate the database during API requests, making it easy to implement create, update, delete, and query operations, quickly building a RESTful API. This combination avoids the hassle of setting up a backend server and is suitable for rapid development and testing.

9659db1357e4.name: How to implement a CRUD API in Firebase Functions using the Express framework?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Firebase Functions, import Express and cors, create an app instance, then define routes and HTTP methods (POST, PUT, DELETE, GET) corresponding to create, update, delete, and read operations. Finally, deploy the Express app as an HTTP function using exports.post = functions.https.onRequest(app). This approach makes API paths clearer and aligns with RESTful design.

9659db1357e4.name: How to solve Firestore write speed limits on like counters?

acceptedAnswer.text: Firestore allows only one write per document per second, which can cause write bottlenecks when likes are high. The official recommendation is to use distributed counters by spreading the like count across multiple subdocuments (e.g., 1 to 10 likeCount_x). Each write randomly updates one subdocument, and when reading, sum the counts from all subdocuments. This avoids high single-point writes that cause latency or data loss.

9659db1357e4.name: How to implement precise statistics for push notification banners using Firebase Functions?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use the iOS Notification Service Extension to call an API when the push notification banner appears. Firebase Functions receives requests containing platform, push notification ID, and action type (received or clicked), and records them using a distributed counter method to avoid invalid devices affecting statistics. This enables accurate calculation of actual display and click rates of push notifications, reducing errors caused by relying solely on APNS/FCM success callbacks.

9659db1357e4.name: How are Firebase Functions and Firestore costs calculated? Are they expensive?

acceptedAnswer.text: Firebase Functions offers a free quota including 2 million invocations, 400,000 GB-seconds, 200,000 GHz-seconds of compute time, and 5GB of outbound networking per month. Firestore charges are based on read/write operations and storage volume. Although there is a free tier, costs can increase quickly with high traffic or large data volumes. In practice, Firebase is suitable for testing and prototypes; for large-scale production, it may not be recommended long-term due to cost and data migration challenges.

9659db1357e4.name: How to test Functions and Firestore locally using Firebase Emulator?

acceptedAnswer.text: During firebase init, you can choose to install the Emulator. After setup, you can simulate Functions and Firestore services locally to test API logic and data operations without incurring actual costs or deploying to the cloud. This is ideal for rapid feature verification during development, avoiding the wait time caused by frequent deployments.

9659db1357e4.name: How to perform stress testing on Firebase Functions API using the siege tool?

acceptedAnswer.text: Install siege (on macOS use brew install siege), then use a command like: siege -c 100 -r 1 -H 'Content-Type: application/json' 'https://your-cloudfunction-url POST {}' where -c 100 means 100 concurrent tasks, -r 1 means each task runs once, and -H adds HTTP headers. After running, you can see the number of successful transactions and verify in Firebase Firestore whether any data was lost.

cb0c68c33994.name: Why couldn't the App Store Connect API directly read app reviews before?

acceptedAnswer.text: Previously, Apple's official App Store Connect API did not provide functionality to read app reviews, so developers could not directly obtain reviews through the official API. It wasn't until July 2022 that the App Store Connect API added support for reading and managing Customer Reviews, making it easier for developers to access review data.

cb0c68c33994.name: Why does AppReviewBot sometimes send duplicate or old review notifications on Slack?

acceptedAnswer.text: The free open-source tool TradeMe/ReviewMe used is outdated and retrieves reviews through Apple's public RSS API, which has data inconsistency issues, causing occasional bursts of old data. This is because the RSS API is outdated, only allows access to up to 10 pages, and lacks updates, resulting in inaccurate review notifications.

cb0c68c33994.name: How to use Fastlane to automatically get App Store Connect login cookies to fetch reviews?

acceptedAnswer.text: Fastlane provides the command fastlane spaceauth -u <account>, which completes web login including two-factor authentication and saves the login session cookie into a FASTLANE_SESSION file. From this, you can obtain the myacinfo token and include it in private API request headers to automate fetching the latest review data.

cb0c68c33994.name: What are the pros and cons of using the Private URL API to fetch app reviews?

acceptedAnswer.text: The advantage is that the data is consistent and complete with Apple's backend, supports more detailed filtering, and many professional tools like AppRadar also use this method. The downside is that it is a non-public API, and due to two-factor login restrictions, the session cookie lasts about 30 days before requiring re-authentication, increasing maintenance costs.

cb0c68c33994.name: How to extend Fastlane SpaceShip in Ruby to get app reviews for all regions and all versions?

acceptedAnswer.text: Add a get_recent_reviews method to the Spaceship::TunesClient class, passing in app_id, platform (ios), and page index, calling the private API to get the latest review list. Use a while loop to iterate through pages until no new data is found, achieving complete retrieval across all regions and versions.

cb0c68c33994.name: How to avoid flooding with a large number of old reviews when running the app review notification bot for the first time?

acceptedAnswer.text: By recording the lastModified time of the most recent review, the first run does not send notifications but only updates the lastModified value. Afterwards, only reviews newer than that time will trigger notifications, preventing a flood of old reviews on initial startup and reducing noise.

cb0c68c33994.name: Why is it recommended to use a separate App Store Connect account with only Customer Support permissions?

acceptedAnswer.text: Using a separate account reduces security risks by preventing external tools or bots from directly accessing sensitive functions through the developer's main account. Granting only Customer Support permissions is sufficient to read reviews, and limiting permissions helps prevent accidental operations or data leaks.

cb0c68c33994.name: How should app review notification messages be presented clearly in the Slack Bot?

acceptedAnswer.text: Set colors based on rating (green for 4 stars and above, orange for 2-3 stars, red for 1 star), display star symbols and helpfulness counts. If the user updates their review, mark it with a pencil icon and show creation or update time. If customer support has replied and the reply is outdated, also mark it with a reminder, making notifications more readable.

33f6aabb744f.name: How does ZReviewsBot automatically track iOS and Android app reviews for me?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZReviewsBot uses updated and reliable API endpoints to track iOS app reviews, while also supporting Google Play Android reviews. It automatically sends the latest reviews to a designated Slack channel, allowing you to stay updated on your app's status in real time. It also supports keyword filtering to avoid spam and advertisement flooding.

33f6aabb744f.name: Why did ZReviewsBot switch to using the App Store Connect API to obtain iOS reviews?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZReviewsBot switched to the App Store Connect API because it provides an official and more stable way to read and manage customer reviews, replacing the previously unstable method of fetching reviews from the backend using Fastlane Spaceship. This ensures that review data is more timely and accurate.

33f6aabb744f.name: What kind of customization settings does ZReviewsBot support?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZReviewsBot allows users to set keyword notification filters, enabling customization of which reviews trigger Slack notifications to avoid unnecessary spam or advertisement flooding. Additionally, it supports deployment via Github Actions, allowing the bot to run automatically on a schedule for easier management.

33f6aabb744f.name: How does ZReviewsBot use Github Actions for automatic deployment and scheduling?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZReviewsBot supports using Github Actions to set up automatic scheduling, enabling the bot to regularly execute review fetching tasks. This approach requires no self-hosted servers; through the Github platform, Slack notifications are updated automatically on schedule, improving maintenance convenience.

33f6aabb744f.name: Does ZReviewsBot support tracking app reviews on both iOS and Android platforms simultaneously?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, ZReviewsBot supports dual-platform review tracking, including iOS App Store and Google Play Android. It helps you centrally monitor the latest user reviews from both major platforms, saving you the hassle of managing them separately.

33f6aabb744f.name: How does ZReviewsBot prevent receiving a large number of meaningless advertisement review notifications?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZReviewsBot has a built-in keyword notification filtering feature that allows you to skip reviews containing specific keywords, preventing spam or advertisement flooding in your Slack channel. This way, you only receive truly important user feedback.

33f6aabb744f.name: Is ZReviewsBot a free and open-source project?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, ZReviewsBot is completely free and open source. You can find the source code on GitHub to deploy or modify it yourself. It is suitable for developers and teams who want to automatically track app reviews and integrate Slack notifications.

d61062833c1a.name: How does Slack Workflow Form solve the inconvenience of filling out Google Forms on mobile devices?

acceptedAnswer.text: Slack Workflow Form allows users to fill out forms directly within Slack without opening a browser, eliminating the hassle of switching apps, which is especially convenient for mobile users. This method can also automatically capture the respondent's email to avoid duplicate entries. For example, employees can open the form via a shortcut in Slack to report their health status, improving the efficiency of form submission.

d61062833c1a.name: How to use Google Apps Script to automatically organize Google Form responses and categorize them into Sheets by different dates?

acceptedAnswer.text: By writing a function in Google Apps Script that reads response data from the Responses Sheet, creates or selects a Sheet named after the submission date, moves the data there, and then deletes the moved data from the original Responses Sheet. This allows daily responses to be managed separately, making it easier for later queries and comparisons. The example code uses the US date format MM/DD/YYYY as the Sheet name.

d61062833c1a.name: How to use Slack App API to automatically import employee lists into Google Sheets?

acceptedAnswer.text: Create a Slack App and set OAuth permissions, use the conversations.members API to get the member list of a specified Channel, then use the users.info API to retrieve member emails, names, and other info, and finally write the data into the employee list tab in Google Sheets. This avoids manual input errors and ensures the list stays synchronized with the Slack Channel.

d61062833c1a.name: How does App Script compare the employee list with the daily submission records to generate a list of those who have not submitted?

acceptedAnswer.text: The script reads the employee list and the Sheet named after the current date containing submission data, retrieves the emails of submitters, then compares whether each employee email exists in the submission list. If not found, the employee is considered as not submitted, and their data is written into a 'not submitted' list Sheet. This quickly identifies employees who have not reported.

d61062833c1a.name: How to use Slack Incoming Webhook to automatically send reminders to those who have not submitted and tag employees?

acceptedAnswer.text: Using the Incoming Webhook URL, the App Script composes a message from the list of those who have not submitted, tags employees using the Slack UID format `<@UID>`, and sends the message in JSON POST format to the specified Channel. This automatically reminds those who have not submitted, saving time from manual individual reminders.

d61062833c1a.name: How does Slack Workflow Form obtain the submission timestamp and store it in Google Sheets?

acceptedAnswer.text: Since Slack Workflow does not have a built-in timestamp variable, you can create a dedicated Timestamp Sheet in Google Sheets with a column set to `=NOW()`. Use App Script to update this cell to trigger a time refresh, then read this time variable in the Workflow steps and finally store it in the submission data row. This method indirectly captures the current submission time.

d61062833c1a.name: How to set up Slack Workflow Form to send a confirmation message to the form submitter?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Workflow Builder, add a 'Send a message' step, select the message recipient as the person who filled out the form, and include form response variables and the Timestamp in the message content. After publishing, the submitter will receive an immediate confirmation message upon submission, confirming successful form completion.

d61062833c1a.name: How to prevent Google Apps Script from being triggered repeatedly during execution?

acceptedAnswer.text: At the beginning of the function, use PropertiesService to set a global variable flag indicating the execution status. If the script is already running, show a warning and stop execution. At the end of the function, reset the flag to indicate the script is no longer running. This prevents users from triggering the script multiple times simultaneously, avoiding data conflicts.

ba5773a7bfea.name: Why is using the Visitor Pattern better for designing sharing features in iOS?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Visitor Pattern allows the sharing logic to be separated from the data models, preventing the mixing of logic for all sharing platforms and models. This way, adding new sharing platforms or models won't affect existing code, reducing coupling and improving maintainability. For example, adding WeChat sharing only requires adding a new Visitor without modifying existing models or other sharing logic.

ba5773a7bfea.name: How is the Visitor Pattern implemented in Swift to share multiple models across multiple platforms?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, all shareable models implement an accept(visitor:) method that takes a Visitor conforming to the SharePolicy protocol. SharePolicy defines visit methods for each model type, and specific sharing platforms implement these methods to compose the sharing content. When called, the model calls accept, passing in the corresponding sharing Visitor, enabling sharing to different platforms.

ba5773a7bfea.name: How does the Visitor Pattern solve the problem of varying data requirements in traditional sharing features?

acceptedAnswer.text: Traditional approaches often cram all data needed by every sharing platform into the model or ShareManager, leading to a messy architecture. The Visitor Pattern lets each sharing platform's Visitor decide which data to extract from which model. For example, Instagram sharing a Playlist requires multiple images, and only the InstagramVisitor handles this without polluting other platform logic, achieving high cohesion and low coupling.

ba5773a7bfea.name: How does double dispatch in the Visitor Pattern work in iOS development?

acceptedAnswer.text: Double dispatch means the model first calls accept(visitor:), then the Visitor calls the corresponding visit(model:) method. This allows executing the correct sharing logic based on the concrete types of both the model and the Visitor. For example, when sharing a PlaylistModel to Facebook, PlaylistModel calls accept, and FacebookVisitor executes visit(PlaylistModel).

ba5773a7bfea.name: Why not just implement Visitor with a generic visit(model: Shareable) method?

acceptedAnswer.text: Using a generic visit(model: Shareable) loses recognition of the specific model types, requiring type checks inside the method, which complicates the code and makes it hard to maintain. The Visitor Pattern emphasizes defining different visit methods for each concrete type, ensuring each model has dedicated handling logic, avoiding type confusion, and improving code readability and extensibility.

ba5773a7bfea.name: How to implement adding a new sharing platform using the Visitor Pattern?

acceptedAnswer.text: To add a new sharing platform, simply implement a new Visitor class conforming to the SharePolicy protocol and implement visit methods for all models. This defines the sharing logic for that platform without modifying existing models or other Visitors. For example, to add WeChat sharing, just add ShareToWeChatVisitor and implement visit(UserModel), visit(SongModel), and visit(PlaylistModel).

ba5773a7bfea.name: What iOS development scenarios are suitable for the Visitor Pattern?

acceptedAnswer.text: Besides sharing features, the Visitor Pattern suits scenarios where multiple operations need to act on various data models, such as data storage (SaveToCoreData), Like features, or complex tableview/collectionview cell configurations. It separates operation logic from data models, making it easier to extend and maintain, especially when there are many operation types and data types.

1c9eafd4a190.name: How can engineers transitioning to managers balance the Maker and Manager roles?

acceptedAnswer.text: To play both the engineer (Maker) and manager (Manager) roles simultaneously, it is recommended to create two separate calendars and spend 15–30 minutes daily planning the day's schedule. Managers need to reserve uninterrupted focus time to solve technical tasks while frequently stepping out to assist the team. Start by dedicating 20% of your time to engineering work and gradually adjust the ratio to avoid completely losing touch with technology and maintain connection with the team.

1c9eafd4a190.name: How can managers effectively review and improve their management decisions?

acceptedAnswer.text: Managers should develop the habit of recording all important decisions and regularly (e.g., monthly) review them, inviting anonymous feedback from bosses or colleagues. During reviews, assess whether the decisions were necessary, if team members' opinions were sought, and whether you would make the same choices again. This repeated reflection helps improve management skills and reduces feelings of isolation.

1c9eafd4a190.name: How can managers encourage team members to step out of their comfort zones without damaging relationships?

acceptedAnswer.text: Managers need to show empathy rather than sympathy, courageously share honest thoughts, clearly state expectations, and lead by example. Fear of hurting team members is often self-imagined; honest communication is essential to lead the team’s growth. Avoid common pitfalls such as not openly sharing failures, forgetting to document discussion outcomes, using inappropriate feedback channels, and delaying feedback to ensure timely and effective communication.

1c9eafd4a190.name: How to delegate tasks while maintaining quality and visibility?

acceptedAnswer.text: Before delegating tasks, ask whether the task can only be done by yourself and if it is a manager’s responsibility; if not, delegate it to the appropriate team member. Set clear expectations and trust your team’s abilities, providing simple documentation explaining goals and examples. Managers should record important task progress to maintain control over quality and status, and protect team members from excessive pressure.

1c9eafd4a190.name: How can managers build trust and good collaboration with other teams?

acceptedAnswer.text: Managers should regularly communicate with other team managers, share team status and obstacles, and explain the reasons behind priorities. Proactively understand external teams’ needs and follow up on assistance, while also expressing your own needs. Maintain transparent and timely communication to avoid complaints behind backs. Methods like thank-you notes, work exchanges, and technical sharing sessions can strengthen relationships.

1c9eafd4a190.name: How to build an engineering management culture centered on business learning?

acceptedAnswer.text: Managers should focus on solving business problems and avoid premature optimization, continuously learning and adjusting to enhance product value. Use the AARRR model to analyze user behavior and drive decisions with data. Prioritize efficiency in the early stages and throughput later. Regularly track team output, identify bottlenecks, and optimize communication, automation, and infrastructure.

1c9eafd4a190.name: How to use Inbound Recruiting to attract excellent engineers?

acceptedAnswer.text: Consistently build a strong engineering culture and an attractive work environment that team members are proud of. Externally, regularly answer community questions, hide recruitment easter eggs, share solutions, host hackathons, and establish open-source projects. Allocate team members to participate in these activities to collectively enhance team exposure and attractiveness.

1c9eafd4a190.name: What key points should be considered when building a scalable team?

acceptedAnswer.text: Align the team vision with company goals and establish core values that are continuously updated. Balance members’ functions and growth needs, distributing different visions and autonomy. Define team and member expectations of the manager, such as providing a productive environment, honest feedback, and leadership opportunities. These contractual expectations require time and patience to gradually realize.

118e924a1477.name: How to seamlessly transfer Chrome browsing data and extensions to the Sidekick browser?

acceptedAnswer.text: Sidekick allows painless migration of Chrome bookmarks, browsing history, saved passwords, logged-in website sessions, and extensions through its import feature. The steps include first installing and signing into Chrome to sync your Google account data, fully closing Chrome, then creating a Sidekick account and choosing to import from Chrome. After importing, most data and extensions will transfer automatically, with only a few services requiring re-login.

118e924a1477.name: Why can't the Sidekick browser directly sync Google account browsing data?

acceptedAnswer.text: Because Google currently blocks all third-party services from accessing the sync feature, Sidekick cannot directly sync or import browsing data from your Google account through account login. This means that when signing into Sidekick, Chrome synced data won't be automatically imported and must be manually transferred via the import process.

118e924a1477.name: What special features does the Sidekick browser offer to boost work productivity?

acceptedAnswer.text: Sidekick’s special features include painless Chrome data migration, powerful homepage search with data-driven anti-tracking display, a left-side Application toolbar similar to the MacOS Dock supporting multi-account switching and notifications, built-in window splitting, Spotlight full-browser search, Tab Saver for work tab management, and integrated Google Keep note-taking. These features help users quickly switch work contexts and improve efficiency.

118e924a1477.name: Which platforms and extensions does the Sidekick browser support?

acceptedAnswer.text: Sidekick supports Windows, Mac OS (including M1), and Linux (deb, rpm) platforms. Built on the Chromium core, it supports all Chrome Store extensions such as bitwarden, lastpass, 1password, grammarly, and google translate. This allows users to seamlessly use familiar Chrome extension tools.

118e924a1477.name: How does the Sidekick browser perform in memory management and privacy protection?

acceptedAnswer.text: Sidekick optimizes memory management based on Chromium. Although the author hasn’t conducted detailed tests, it feels better than Chrome. On privacy, Sidekick has built-in anti-tracking and ad-blocking features that enhance user privacy protection. Compared to Chrome, it places more emphasis on privacy and security, with clear displays of anti-tracking data and ad blocking.

118e924a1477.name: How to use the Sidekick browser’s Application feature, and what workflows is it suited for?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Application feature is similar to the MacOS Dock, allowing users to pin frequently used websites to the left toolbar with persistent presence, supporting multi-account switching and real-time notifications. It’s ideal for users who frequently switch between multiple work platforms, such as code reviews, project management, and customer service systems, making workflows smoother and more efficient.

118e924a1477.name: What are the limitations of Sidekick’s free plan, and how to get the Pro plan?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Sidekick free plan is sufficient for personal use. The official version is currently in Early Access, and there is no limit even if you have more than 5 Sidebar apps. The Pro plan is available by invitation: inviting 10 users grants 6 months of Pro access, and inviting 20 users grants lifetime Pro. Users can download via the official link and support the development team.

d414bdbdb8c9.name: How to use Gmail Filter with Google Apps Script to automatically forward emails to Slack?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, create a filter in Gmail, set the conditions, and automatically add a label for identification, such as "forward-to-slack." Then, write a script in Google Apps Script to periodically read emails with that label, convert the content into the desired format, and send the message to a specified Slack channel using Slack's Incoming Webhook URL. Finally, remove the label to indicate the email has been forwarded. This achieves an automated forwarding process.

d414bdbdb8c9.name: Why use Slack Incoming Webhook to send Gmail email content?

acceptedAnswer.text: Slack Incoming Webhook provides a simple HTTP POST interface that can directly send formatted email content to a specified channel, making it easy to notify the team in real time. This method requires no complex authentication, is quick to set up, and is suitable for fast forwarding of Gmail emails to Slack, supporting customized message formats.

d414bdbdb8c9.name: How does Google Apps Script read Gmail emails and format the content?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use GmailApp.getUserLabelByName() to get threads with the specified label, then use getMessages() to retrieve all emails. Use methods like getFrom(), getTo(), getSubject(), and getPlainBody() to read details. Combine this information into the desired string format before sending it to Slack. The script demonstrates processing all emails in a loop and formatting the output.

d414bdbdb8c9.name: How to set up automatic scheduled forwarding of Gmail emails in Google Apps Script?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the Apps Script editor, select "Triggers" on the left, add a new trigger, choose the function to run (e.g., forwardEmailsToSlack), set the event source to time-driven, and select the frequency (such as every minute or every hour). This allows the script to run automatically on schedule, regularly checking Gmail and forwarding new emails to Slack.

d414bdbdb8c9.name: Can Google Apps Script customize message formats when forwarding Gmail emails to Slack?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. The script extracts the email content and assembles the text format according to needs, such as bolding the title, adding line breaks to display sender, recipient, subject, and email body. It can also use Slack’s message formatting structure or regular expressions to extract specific information, making forwarded messages better suited to team requirements.

d414bdbdb8c9.name: How to parse version number and Build Number from Apple Testflight review emails using Regex?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use JavaScript Regex with the match function on the email subject or content, combined with the regular expression /(Bundle Version Short String: ){1}(\S+){1}[\S\s]*(Build Number: ){1}(\S+){1}/ to extract the version number and Build Number. The script demonstrates storing the results in variables version and build for convenient subsequent automation.

d414bdbdb8c9.name: How to resolve "Authorization Required" error when running Google Apps Script?

acceptedAnswer.text: This occurs because the script needs permission to access Gmail and send network requests. When running, click "Continue" to authorize. If you see the "Google hasn’t verified this app" message, click "Advanced" at the bottom left, then select "Go to ForwardEmailsToSlack (unsafe)," and click "Allow" to complete authorization. This enables the script to function properly.

11f6c8568154.name: How does the Pinkoi team use Squads to improve work efficiency?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Pinkoi team is composed of multiple Squads focused on different areas, such as the Buyer Squad responsible for buyer-side features and the Seller Squad responsible for designer-side features. Each Squad includes roles like PM, Product Designer, Data, Frontend, Backend, iOS, and Android, and is responsible for ongoing work goals. This clear and specialized division of tasks enhances collaboration efficiency.

11f6c8568154.name: How does an increase in team size affect communication and efficiency?

acceptedAnswer.text: As the number of people increases, direct communication becomes more difficult, requiring more asynchronous communication and priority management, which increases communication time. Although having multiple roles allows for finer division of labor and improves productivity and quality, it also amplifies the cost of repetitive tasks. For example, 20 people spending time posting reports daily can waste up to 750 hours cumulatively.

11f6c8568154.name: How does the Pinkoi team use interface communication to avoid ambiguity and repeated confirmations?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pinkoi defines communication interfaces such as documents, processes, checklists, or tools to serve as bridges in communication, avoiding ambiguity and repeated confirmations. For example, the App Team and Backend use automatically generated API documentation as an interface, Product Designers establish consensus through a Design System component library, and Customer Service uses an automated forwarding bot for store reviews. This keeps communication focused and effective.

11f6c8568154.name: How does Pinkoi automate the release of iOS test versions?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pinkoi uses Slack Workflow as an input interface to trigger Bitrise to run Fastlane scripts that automatically package and upload test versions to Firebase App Distribution. Testers only need to install certificates and register their devices to self-select the version to install, reducing manual installation workload and improving efficiency.

11f6c8568154.name: What is a Release Train, and how does the Pinkoi team use it to manage official App releases?

acceptedAnswer.text: Release Train is the concept of a fixed release schedule. Pinkoi sets Monday for new version updates, Wednesday for Code Freeze, Thursday for QA, and Friday for packaging the official release. This allows multiple teams to have consensus and a regular process, avoiding chaotic updates and improving collaboration efficiency and user experience.

11f6c8568154.name: How does the Pinkoi team use automation tools to reduce the time cost of packaging official App releases?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pinkoi automates the packaging process by scheduling release dates on Google Calendar. When the time comes, Google Apps Script triggers Bitrise to run Fastlane scripts to complete packaging and submission. This saves about one hour of manual packaging switching costs, improves efficiency, and allows flexible schedule adjustments.

11f6c8568154.name: How does the Pinkoi team improve collaboration efficiency through Github Action?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pinkoi uses Github Action to automate the PR Review process, such as automatically tagging Reviewers after merging and regularly sending lists of PRs pending review to Slack, reducing manual management workload. Github Action can be bound to various events and offers free usage quotas, making it suitable for enhancing team collaboration efficiency.

11f6c8568154.name: How does Pinkoi's team culture support problem-solving and driving change?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pinkoi encourages everyone to be a leader in problem-solving, regardless of role. The culture emphasizes continuous growth, proactive communication, exerting influence, and courageous implementation. After success, sharing results promotes cross-departmental integration. This supports the team in jointly driving process optimization and automation to improve efficiency.

e77b80cc6f89.name: Why is Crashlytics not suitable for directly automating crash data queries?

acceptedAnswer.text: Crashlytics does not provide an API for querying crash data and only stores crash records for the past 90 days. Its integrations support and flexibility are also very limited. This makes it impossible to write scripts to automatically fetch data, relying solely on manual checks, which is very inconvenient for needs that require real-time automated crash tracking.

e77b80cc6f89.name: What crash tracking issues can be solved by integrating Crashlytics with Big Query?

acceptedAnswer.text: Integrating Crashlytics with Big Query allows crash data to be imported into Big Query, enabling custom data analysis using SQL queries. Combined with Google Apps Script, you can automate queries and forward results to Slack, improving the timeliness and convenience of crash tracking and overcoming Crashlytics' lack of API and limited flexibility.

e77b80cc6f89.name: How to query the top 10 most frequent crash issues in the last 7 days using Big Query?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can use the official example SQL to select events where is_fatal is true within the last 7 days, order by crash count, and limit to the top 10. For example: SELECT DISTINCT issue_id, issue_title, issue_subtitle, COUNT(DISTINCT event_id) AS number_of_crashes FROM `ProjectID.firebase_crashlytics.TableName` WHERE is_fatal = true AND event_timestamp >= TIMESTAMP_SUB(CURRENT_TIMESTAMP(), INTERVAL 7 DAY) GROUP BY issue_id, issue_title, issue_subtitle ORDER BY number_of_crashes DESC LIMIT 10.

e77b80cc6f89.name: How to use Google Apps Script to automatically send the top 10 Crashlytics crash issues to Slack?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, write a function in Google Apps Script to query Crashlytics data from Big Query. Then format the results into Slack message format, and finally use UrlFetchApp to call Slack's Incoming WebHook URL to send the message. This allows you to regularly and automatically push the top 10 crash issues to a specified Slack channel.

e77b80cc6f89.name: Will querying Crashlytics data in Big Query incur additional costs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Big Query offers 10 GB of storage and 1 TB of query capacity per month for free, but charges apply beyond these free quotas. Each SQL query execution counts the amount of data processed and accumulates query usage, so be careful not to run unnecessary queries or schedule frequent executions to avoid high costs.

e77b80cc6f89.name: What are the limitations of Crashlytics' Slack notification feature?

acceptedAnswer.text: Crashlytics' built-in Slack notifications only cover a fixed set of events (such as new crashes, trend alerts, etc.), and the notification content and rules cannot be customized. This can lead to receiving a large number of unimportant notifications frequently, affecting the team's judgment and handling efficiency.

e77b80cc6f89.name: What is Crash-Free Rate? Why is it impossible to reach 100%?

acceptedAnswer.text: Crash-Free Rate refers to the percentage of users who do not experience app crashes, with an ideal value around 99.9%. However, since bugs are inevitable in code and some crashes originate from underlying systems or third-party SDKs, as daily active users increase, occasional crashes also rise, making it impossible to completely avoid crashes.

9a05f632eba0.name: How does iOS 17 Safari automatically remove tracking parameters from URLs?

acceptedAnswer.text: iOS 17 Safari automatically removes tracking parameters from URLs, such as fbclid and gclid, to prevent third-party tracking. This is done by identifying common tracking parameters and deleting them from the URL to protect user privacy. For example, after clicking https://zhgchg.li/post/1?gclid=124, the URL changes to https://zhgchg.li/post/1, removing the tracking code.

9a05f632eba0.name: How does Private Relay in iOS 15 and later protect user IP addresses?

acceptedAnswer.text: Private Relay sends the user's original request first to Apple's Ingress Proxy, then forwards it through a partnered CDN Egress Proxy. The process is encrypted, and each party only knows partial information. This prevents websites from directly obtaining the real IP address, showing instead a shared CDN IP that is guaranteed to be within the same country and time zone. For example, with Private Relay enabled, a user originally in Taipei will have their IP replaced by a shared CDN IP still within the Taiwan time zone.

9a05f632eba0.name: Why is IDFA considered almost blocked since iOS 14.5?

acceptedAnswer.text: Because iOS 14.5 requires apps to obtain user consent before accessing the IDFA; otherwise, the IDFA value cannot be retrieved. Surveys show that about 70% to 90% of users refuse tracking, causing many apps to be unable to effectively use IDFA for ad tracking. This severely limits IDFA functionality, hence it is considered almost blocked.

9a05f632eba0.name: How does iOS restrict apps from using URL Schemes to detect other installed apps?

acceptedAnswer.text: Since iOS 9, apps must explicitly list the URL Schemes they want to query in their settings, and starting from iOS 15, this is limited to a maximum of 50 URL Schemes. This prevents apps from unlimitedly probing other apps. These restrictions reduce the risk of cross-app data collection and enhance user privacy protection.

9a05f632eba0.name: How does iOS 16 handle apps actively reading the clipboard?

acceptedAnswer.text: iOS 16 requires that clipboard reads not initiated by user actions must trigger a prompt asking for user permission before the app can access the clipboard. This prevents apps from secretly reading clipboard data in the background and improves privacy transparency. For example, if an app reads the clipboard while in the background, a prompt appears and the user must tap allow to access the content.

9a05f632eba0.name: What technical methods are blocked on iOS to prevent cross-site or cross-app user tracking?

acceptedAnswer.text: iOS blocks Third-Party Cookies (Safari implemented Intelligent Tracking Prevention since iOS 11), prohibits apps from accessing Device UUID (since iOS 7), restricts IDFA usage (requires user consent since iOS 14.5), limits URL Scheme probing (since iOS 9), and uses Private Relay to obfuscate IP addresses (since iOS 15). These measures effectively prevent cross-site and cross-app tracking.

9a05f632eba0.name: What are the four key points of Apple's privacy principles?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apple's privacy principles include: 1. Data Minimization (only collecting necessary data), 2. On-Device Processing (processing private data locally as much as possible), 3. User Transparency and Control (letting users know what data is collected and how it is used, and allowing control), 4. Security (ensuring data storage and transmission are secure). These principles guide the design of iOS privacy features.

9a05f632eba0.name: How does iOS help developers transparently disclose their app's privacy practices on the App Store?

acceptedAnswer.text: iOS requires developers to provide privacy usage disclosures on the App Store, detailing what data the app collects and its purposes. Xcode 15 also supports generating privacy reports through Privacy Manifest, making it easier for developers to configure and for users to understand. This helps users clearly know the app's privacy behavior, increasing transparency and trust.

793cb8f89b72.name: Why can't Big Query be used to query Crash-Free Users Rate?

acceptedAnswer.text: Big Query only contains raw data of crashes and does not include data of users without crashes, so it cannot calculate the Crash-Free Users Rate. This percentage requires the status of all users and must be queried through Google Analytics to have the data.

793cb8f89b72.name: How to query Crash-Free Users Rate using Google Analytics API?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Analytics Data API (GA4) provides two metrics: crashFreeUsersRate and crashAffectedUsers. By setting the dimension to platform and adding date ranges and filters, you can obtain the Crash-Free Users Rate for specified devices. For example, using GA4 Query Explorer, set the platform to iOS and the date range to the past 30 days to get the crash rate for that period.

793cb8f89b72.name: How to automatically fill Crash-Free Users Rate into Google Sheet?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can use Google Apps Script to call the Google Analytics Data API to query Crash-Free Users Rate, then open the specified Google Sheet using SpreadsheetApp.openById(), and automatically add rows with sheet.appendRow(). Combined with time-driven triggers, the data can be updated regularly and fully automated.

793cb8f89b72.name: How to set authorization in Google Apps Script to call Analytics Data API?

acceptedAnswer.text: The first time you run an Apps Script that includes the Analytics Data API, an authorization window will pop up. You need to sign in with your Google account and grant permission. After authorization is completed, the script can successfully call the API and retrieve the Crash-Free Users Rate.

793cb8f89b72.name: How to quickly test Crash-Free Users Rate queries using GA4 Query Explorer tool?

acceptedAnswer.text: GA4 Query Explorer is a web GUI tool provided by Google. You can select your GA4 account, set the date range, add the crashFreeUsersRate metric, and use dimension filters to filter platforms (such as iOS or Android). Click Make Request to see the query results, making it convenient to test query conditions.

793cb8f89b72.name: Will Crash-Free Users Rate data differ from Firebase Crashlytics numbers?

acceptedAnswer.text: When querying Crash-Free Users Rate from Google Analytics, there may be slight differences from the numbers shown in Firebase Crashlytics (e.g., 0.0002). The reason is unknown but within an acceptable range. If you consistently use GA data, this difference can be ignored.

793cb8f89b72.name: How does the sample Google Apps Script code fetch Crash-Free Users Rate for iOS and Android platforms?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the sample code, the fetchCrashFreeUsersRate function takes a platform parameter (iOS or android) and uses the AnalyticsData API to set dimensions, metrics, date ranges, and filters, returning the Crash-Free Users Rate for that platform. Then the execute function calls it twice to fetch iOS and Android separately, and finally writes the results into Google Sheet.

78507a8de6a5.name: Why use Singleton, Flyweight, and Factory Pattern to manage Socket.IO connection objects?

acceptedAnswer.text: These three patterns ensure that only one connection object is created per URL path, reducing memory usage and duplicate connection costs, while encapsulating an abstract interface for easy future framework replacement. Singleton guarantees a single instance of ConnectionManager, Flyweight shares connection objects with repeated states, and Factory is responsible for abstracting connection creation. In the example, two requests to the same URL return the same UUID, proving object reuse.

78507a8de6a5.name: iOS Socket.IO Swift Client does not support offline event sending, how to solve this with Command Pattern?

acceptedAnswer.text: Since the Swift Client does not support offline sending, the Command Pattern is used to encapsulate send operations into command objects. When the connection is disconnected, commands are queued, and once the connection is restored, they are executed in FIFO order. This unifies cross-platform behavior and makes managing delayed sends easier. In the example, send commands are cached when offline and automatically executed when the connection becomes connected.

78507a8de6a5.name: Socket.IO connection states are complex, why use Finite State Machine and State Pattern to manage states?

acceptedAnswer.text: Connections have multiple states and valid transition rules; using if..else directly makes the code hard to maintain. Finite State Machine manages state transition rules, while State Pattern encapsulates behaviors of different states into objects, making operations between states clear and extensible. In the example, states like Created, Connected, and Disconnected each implement allowed operations, preventing illegal calls.

78507a8de6a5.name: How to use Chain of Responsibility Pattern to manage polling and operations of Connection states?

acceptedAnswer.text: Chain of Responsibility allows multiple nodes to sequentially decide whether to handle connection state changes, avoiding complex checks in a single method. Each node checks if conditions match; if so, it performs the operation and stops propagation; otherwise, it passes to the next node. In the example, Disconnected, Reconnect, and Released handlers respectively handle different states and usage checks, ensuring state operations are mutually exclusive and clear.

78507a8de6a5.name: Why use Builder Pattern to encapsulate the construction process of Connection?

acceptedAnswer.text: Builder Pattern allows step-by-step setting of Connection-related parameters, ensuring that required configurations are completed before obtaining a usable connection object, preventing incorrect external usage. This improves construction flexibility and reusability while centralizing construction logic. In the example, SIOConnectionBuilder sets the config then builds ConnectionClient, calling connect() to get the connection instance.

78507a8de6a5.name: What pitfalls should be noted when using Socket.IO Swift Client?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Swift Client has not been updated for a long time, so you must ensure the Client and Server versions align (allowEIO3 can be used); otherwise, connections will fail. The Swift Client may not support all features of the official Web version, such as offline event sending, which requires custom implementation. It is based on Starscream; if necessary, you can downgrade to use Starscream directly. It is recommended to first test if required features are supported before adopting it.

78507a8de6a5.name: How to ensure Feature Teams using Socket.IO encapsulation do not need to understand underlying connection details?

acceptedAnswer.text: By encapsulating objects like ConnectionPool, ConnectionManager, and ConnectionKeeper, a unified and simplified interface is provided to manage connection states and reconnection logic. Feature Teams only need to use the high-level interface to send and receive events without worrying about Socket.IO connection details and state management. This design improves reusability and reduces the learning curve.

ddd88a84e177.name: How to use ZMediumToMarkdown to download all articles of a Medium user and convert them to Markdown?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMediumToMarkdown obtains the UserID by entering the Medium username, then uses the GraphQL API to paginate and fetch all articles of that user, parsing the content into Markdown format. It supports login-free operation, automatically downloads all articles and embedded images, and converts image paths to local paths for convenient and complete backup.

ddd88a84e177.name: Why is the official Medium export feature not suitable for backing up as Markdown?

acceptedAnswer.text: The official Medium export feature can only export in a format that can be imported back into the Medium platform; it cannot convert to Markdown and does not back up embedded Gists or images. Additionally, the official API only provides publishing capabilities and lacks full support for downloading and converting articles, making it unsuitable for Markdown backups.

ddd88a84e177.name: How does ZMediumToMarkdown handle embedded Gist source code in Medium articles?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMediumToMarkdown parses embedded Gists in articles and converts them into Markdown code blocks with the corresponding programming language. This allows users to fully preserve the original code format without manual copy-pasting, which is ideal for Medium articles that extensively use Gist embeds.

ddd88a84e177.name: How does ZMediumToMarkdown ensure the backup Markdown files have dates consistent with the Medium articles?

acceptedAnswer.text: It automatically adjusts the last modified time of the downloaded Markdown files to match the Medium article's publish or last modified date, facilitating management and comparison. If an article has not been updated, the download is skipped to save bandwidth and time, making it suitable for automatic synchronization backups.

ddd88a84e177.name: Does ZMediumToMarkdown support downloading paid Medium articles?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, ZMediumToMarkdown supports downloading Medium paid content. It can back up paid articles and embedded images without requiring login, allowing users to fully preserve all article content.

ddd88a84e177.name: How does ZMediumToMarkdown parse paragraph text styles in Medium articles and convert them to Markdown?

acceptedAnswer.text: It parses the JSON paragraph structure generated by Medium through Apollo Client, converting text markings (such as code, links, bold, italic) into corresponding Markdown syntax. For nested markings, it uses correct Markdown nested syntax to avoid formatting errors, ensuring the article layout remains intact.

ddd88a84e177.name: What automated backup methods does ZMediumToMarkdown support?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMediumToMarkdown supports a command-line interface (CLI) and can be integrated with GitHub Actions as a free automatic synchronization or backup service. It automatically skips articles that have not been updated, saving server bandwidth and execution time, making it ideal for scheduled automatic backups of Medium articles.

a8c2d26cc734.name: Does iOS NSAttributedString using DocumentType.html cause crash issues?

acceptedAnswer.text: iOS NSAttributedString using DocumentType.html experienced numerous crash issues in iOS versions 15.0.X to 15.X.X, causing over 2400 crashes affecting more than 1400 users within the last 90 days. This is due to a bug in the underlying Foundation framework, which Apple has started fixing or mitigating from iOS 15.2 onwards.

a8c2d26cc734.name: Why is the performance of directly using NSAttributedString HTML parsing so poor?

acceptedAnswer.text: Directly using NSAttributedString DocumentType.html to parse HTML has extremely poor performance, tested to be 5 to 20 times slower than a custom rendering implementation. This is because HTML is inherently a web format, which the app side cannot effectively optimize, and parsing can only be done on the main thread, causing a performance bottleneck.

a8c2d26cc734.name: How to avoid NSAttributedString HTML parsing crash issues?

acceptedAnswer.text: To avoid crashes, the official recommendation is: if rendering complex and unconstrained HTML, use WKWebView; if there are clear rendering constraints, implement your own HTML parser and render NSAttributedString natively; or switch to using the native Markdown rendering supported in iOS 15 and above.

a8c2d26cc734.name: What are the limitations and pros and cons of using XMLParser to parse HTML?

acceptedAnswer.text: Using XMLParser to parse HTML requires the HTML string to fully comply with XML rules, with zero tolerance for errors; any non-compliant tags will cause failure. The advantage is that it can convert HTML entities to characters and makes it easy to implement custom tag parsing and rendering. However, since HTML often contains non-XML format tags, input must be corrected or restricted before use.

a8c2d26cc734.name: What is the general process of implementing HTML to NSAttributedString conversion yourself?

acceptedAnswer.text: The process is to first use Regex or XMLParser to convert the HTML string into tokens, then fix incomplete or misaligned tags, next construct an abstract syntax tree (AST), and finally use the Visitor Pattern to map tags to custom AttributedStringStyles, producing a styled NSMutableAttributedString.

a8c2d26cc734.name: What are HTML rendering constraints? Why impose limitations?

acceptedAnswer.text: Rendering constraints limit the range of HTML tags and styles supported by the app, for example only supporting bold, italic, links, etc. Having limitations prevents crashes and performance issues caused by parsing complex HTML, and also makes it easier for developers to manage and maintain, ensuring the app can render content correctly and efficiently.

a8c2d26cc734.name: Why is it recommended for new apps to use Markdown instead of HTML parsing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Markdown rendering is natively supported in iOS 15 and above, offering much better performance and avoiding the NSAttributedString HTML parsing crash issues. Markdown has a simple specification and is easy to extend, resulting in lower development and maintenance costs, making it suitable for new projects to adopt directly.

60473cb47550.name: Why does not using the Visitor Pattern in UITableView lead to difficulties in maintenance and testing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Without using the Visitor Pattern, the logic for UITableView's cellForRowAt, heightForRowAt, and others are all written in the same ViewController, making the code hard to extend and maintain. Adding new types requires modifying multiple places, and the scattered logic is difficult to test, prone to forgetting updates or errors. This centralized switch-case approach makes reading and managing the code complex.

60473cb47550.name: How does the Visitor Pattern help manage multiple types of UITableView Cells?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Visitor Pattern uses the FeedVisitor interface to define operation methods that various ViewObjects can accept. Each operator implements this interface to handle different types of Cells, Cell heights, and configurations. This way, when adding new types, you only need to add methods to the interface and implement them, avoiding centralized modifications and improving extensibility and testability.

60473cb47550.name: How do ViewObjects in UITableView interact with Visitors to obtain the corresponding Cell?

acceptedAnswer.text: ViewObjects implement the FeedViewObject protocol, providing the accept(visitor:) method, which calls visitor.visit(self). This allows the Visitor to return the corresponding UITableViewCell class based on the specific ViewObject type. Thus, UITableView only needs to call accept on the Visitor to dynamically get the correct Cell.

60473cb47550.name: When adding a new dynamic wall block type, what parts need to be changed in the Visitor Pattern?

acceptedAnswer.text: Add a new ViewObject class implementing the FeedViewObject protocol, then add the corresponding visit method to the FeedVisitor interface, and finally implement the new visit logic in each operator (such as FeedCellVisitor, FeedCellHeightVisitor, FeedCellConfiguratorVisitor). This completes the extension without modifying the original ViewController.

60473cb47550.name: How does the Visitor Pattern ensure the single responsibility of each operator when used in UITableView?

acceptedAnswer.text: Each operator implements the FeedVisitor protocol and focuses on a single task. For example, FeedCellVisitor is responsible for returning the Cell class, FeedCellHeightVisitor returns the Cell height, and FeedCellConfiguratorVisitor configures the Cell. This separation ensures each operator has a single responsibility, making testing and maintenance easier.

60473cb47550.name: After using the Visitor Pattern, how do the implementations of cellForRowAt and heightForRowAt in UITableView differ?

acceptedAnswer.text: After adopting the Visitor Pattern, cellForRowAt and heightForRowAt first obtain the corresponding ViewObject, then call accept(visitor:) to let the Visitor return the appropriate Cell class or height. This avoids writing switch-case statements in the ViewController, distributes logic across Visitors, and improves readability and extensibility.

60473cb47550.name: How does the Visitor Pattern help improve the readability of UITableView?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Visitor Pattern encapsulates the logic for different types of Cells within separate operator objects, so the ViewController does not need to write lengthy switch-case statements. By simply reviewing the implementations of the operators, one can understand the composition logic of each View on the page, making the code structure clearer and reading more intuitive.

48a8526c1300.name: What are the risks of format errors in iOS Localizable.strings files?

acceptedAnswer.text: Localizable.strings is a plain text file, and format errors such as missing semicolons can cause the entire localization file to fail. The app will display the key directly to users, which reduces user confidence. Such errors cannot be detected early by Xcode at build time and are usually only reported after release.

48a8526c1300.name: How does SwiftGen help prevent typos or missing keys in multilingual string keys?

acceptedAnswer.text: SwiftGen changes NSLocalizedString string access to object access, preventing typos or forgetting to declare keys. It generates Swift code that allows developers to call strings like L10n.homeTitle, enabling compile-time error detection. This avoids referencing undefined keys in the code.

48a8526c1300.name: Why use UnitTest to check for missing or duplicate keys in multilingual files?

acceptedAnswer.text: SwiftGen can only generate code from a single language and cannot guarantee that other languages have the same keys. UnitTests read all language .strings files to check for missing, duplicate, or extra keys, ensuring multilingual content is complete and consistent, preventing incomplete localization issues after release.

48a8526c1300.name: What are the advantages of Xcode 13's built-in Localizable.strings format checking?

acceptedAnswer.text: Xcode 13's built-in format checking automatically validates the .strings file format at build time. Issues like missing semicolons or extra strings are blocked, preventing format errors that cause app display problems. This makes the development process safer and saves the trouble of manual checks.

48a8526c1300.name: How to automatically run SwiftGen to generate multilingual code in Xcode Build Phases?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can add a Run Script in the Build Phases of your Xcode project to execute the SwiftGen command (e.g., "${PODS_ROOT}/SwiftGen/bin/swiftgen"). This automatically generates the latest multilingual Swift code on every build, ensuring the code stays synchronized with the .strings files and avoids manual update omissions.

48a8526c1300.name: Why is reading .strings files as String better than NSDictionary for checking duplicate keys in multilingual files?

acceptedAnswer.text: When NSDictionary reads .strings files, it automatically overwrites duplicate keys and cannot detect duplicates. Reading the file as a String and parsing with regular expressions can retrieve all keys, including duplicates, making it easier for UnitTests to check for duplicate key issues and ensure the accuracy of multilingual files.

48a8526c1300.name: How to handle parameterized NSLocalizedString when converting multilingual strings to SwiftGen format?

acceptedAnswer.text: Converting parameterized NSLocalizedString is more complex and requires writing a custom Command Line Tool that uses regex to scan and convert them into SwiftGen-supported function formats. It is recommended to convert in stages: first convert non-parameterized Swift, then parameterized Swift, and finally handle the Objective-C version, ensuring each stage builds successfully.

a0c08d579ab1.name: Why move Medium articles to a self-hosted Github Pages site?

acceptedAnswer.text: Moving Medium articles to a self-hosted site allows you to control your own content without restrictions from third-party platforms, preventing loss if the platform shuts down. A self-hosted static site offers fast loading and customization features, enhancing the reading experience. For example, the author has accumulated over 65 articles and wants to publish them simultaneously while preserving all historical versions.

a0c08d579ab1.name: How to build a static blog using Jekyll and Github Pages?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, install Ruby and Bundler, then install Jekyll using gem. Choose a Jekyll template (like Chirpy) from Github to create a repository, clone it, and modify the _config.yml settings. Finally, preview the local site with bundle exec jekyll s. When deploying, push the content to Github, and Github Actions will automatically deploy the static site.

a0c08d579ab1.name: How to batch download Medium articles and convert them to Markdown for Jekyll?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use the author's tool ZMediumToMarkdown. Run the command `ZMediumToMarkdown -j your Medium username` to automatically download all articles from that account, convert them to Markdown, and place them in Jekyll's _posts directory for easy local preview and editing.

a0c08d579ab1.name: How to set up Github Actions to automatically sync Medium articles to a self-hosted site?

acceptedAnswer.text: Add a workflow in the Github Repo's Actions named ZMediumToMarkdown.yml. Set a schedule (e.g., run at midnight on the 15th of each month) and use the ZhgChgLi/ZMediumToMarkdown@main Action, filling in the Medium username as a command parameter. Once completed, the system will automatically download and deploy the latest articles without manual intervention.

a0c08d579ab1.name: How to bind a custom domain to a Github Pages static site?

acceptedAnswer.text: After purchasing a domain, add four Github Pages specified A Records in your domain's DNS settings, pointing to IPs 185.199.108.153, 185.199.109.153, 185.199.110.153, and 185.199.111.153 respectively. Then, enter the domain in the Custom domain field under Github Repo Settings and save. After DNS propagation, you can access the site using your custom domain.

a0c08d579ab1.name: How should Jekyll article filenames and content be formatted correctly?

acceptedAnswer.text: The filename format should be `YYYY-MM-DD-article-name.md`, and it's recommended to use English for the article name to improve SEO. The content must include YAML Front Matter at the top, setting layout, title, description, date, author, categories, and tags, for example: `--- layout: post title: "Title" date: 2022-07-16 author: ZhgChgLi tags: [ios] categories: Jekyll Life ---`. Ensuring correct formatting is essential for proper rendering.

a0c08d579ab1.name: Why use rbenv to install Ruby instead of the system's default version?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Ruby version that comes with macOS is older and harder to manage. Using rbenv allows you to install and switch between multiple Ruby versions, avoiding conflicts between the system version and project requirements. The author demonstrates installing Ruby 2.6.5 with rbenv to ensure compatibility with tools like Jekyll and maintain an isolated environment.

f1365e51902c.name: How to Read and Manage Customer Reviews with App Store Connect API?

acceptedAnswer.text: App Store Connect API 2.0+ allows developers to directly call the API to retrieve Customer Reviews for a specified app through an authorized JWT Token, supporting management functions. This eliminates the need for unstable RSS feeds or manual login web scraping, providing complete data and automation. For example, using curl with a Bearer Token to call https://api.appstoreconnect.apple.com/v1/apps/APPID/customerReviews can fetch the reviews.

f1365e51902c.name: Why Were There Issues When Using Public RSS to Get App Reviews Before?

acceptedAnswer.text: Public RSS provides limited app review information, lacks flexible filtering, has quantity limits, and occasionally experiences data inconsistencies, causing instability. This makes automated monitoring and analysis difficult, leaving developers passively waiting for updates.

f1365e51902c.name: What Are the Limitations and Challenges of Using Fastlane SpaceShip to Obtain App Reviews?

acceptedAnswer.text: Fastlane SpaceShip scrapes data by simulating a browser login to the App Store Connect backend. Its advantage is comprehensive and stable data, but the session expires monthly and requires manual re-login, with the Apple ID needing two-factor authentication. Sessions become invalid immediately if used from different IPs, limiting deployment flexibility.

f1365e51902c.name: How to Generate the JWT Token for App Store Connect API?

acceptedAnswer.text: The JWT Token must be signed using the private key (.p8 file) of the App Store Connect API Key. The payload includes iss (Issuer ID), iat (issued at time), exp (expiration time), and aud (fixed as appstoreconnect-v1). You can generate it following official documentation or Ruby sample code. The token has a time limit, usually expiring within 20 minutes, so it needs to be regenerated regularly.

f1365e51902c.name: How to Create and Manage App Store Connect API Keys?

acceptedAnswer.text: Log in to the App Store Connect backend and go to the “Users and Access” page, then switch to the “Keys” tab. Add a new App Store Connect API Key, set its name and permissions (such as App Manager). After creation, you can download the .p8 private key file only once, so be sure to keep it safe. If lost, you must revoke and recreate the key. Avoid leaking the private key to ensure security.

f1365e51902c.name: When Will Apple Cancel the XML (RSS) Access Method for App Reviews?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apple plans to discontinue the original XML (RSS) access method in November 2022. Developers need to switch to using the App Store Connect API to read and manage app reviews to avoid service interruptions.

f1365e51902c.name: Does Using App Store Connect API to Read App Reviews Require Manual Session Maintenance?
