acceptedAnswer.text: No. The App Store Connect API uses JWT Token-based authorization, and the private key does not expire. Each request can generate a new token signed by the private key, so manual session maintenance is not needed. This also avoids the hassle of manual login and session updates required by Fastlane web scraping.

e36e48bb9265.name: How does ZReviewTender automatically monitor the latest reviews on the App Store and Google Play?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZReviewTender uses Apple's new App Store Connect API and Google's AndroidpublisherV3 API to automatically fetch the latest user reviews for iOS/macOS and Android apps, filtering out content that has not been previously retrieved. It runs on a schedule (default every 6 hours) and pushes new reviews to Slack or logs them in Google Sheets, allowing the team to stay updated on user feedback in real time.

e36e48bb9265.name: How can I quickly deploy the ZReviewTender review monitoring bot using GitHub Actions?

acceptedAnswer.text: Simply use the GitHub Repo Template provided by ZReviewTender, click "Use this template" to create a private repo, configure the android.yml and apple.yml files and upload the keys, then manually trigger or schedule the workflow via GitHub Actions. GitHub Actions runs by default every 6 hours and consumes about 60 minutes of execution time per month, requiring no self-hosted server space.

e36e48bb9265.name: How does ZReviewTender's FilterProcessor help me filter the reviews I want?

acceptedAnswer.text: FilterProcessor allows you to filter reviews based on keywords, rating scores, and region/language conditions. By specifying parameters like keywordsInclude, ratingsInclude, and territoriesInclude in the configuration file, you can process only reviews that meet your criteria, avoiding irrelevant or unimportant comments and improving monitoring accuracy.

e36e48bb9265.name: How does ZReviewTender automatically translate app reviews that are not in the specified language?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZReviewTender has a built-in GoogleTranslateProcessor which, when enabled, uses the Google Translate API to automatically translate reviews not in the specified language or region into your target language (e.g., zh-TW). By providing the Google Translate API Service Account key and setting the target language and excluded languages, translation is done automatically, helping the team quickly understand multilingual reviews.

e36e48bb9265.name: Which platforms does ZReviewTender support for app review monitoring?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZReviewTender supports monitoring reviews for Apple App Store iOS/macOS apps and Google Play Android apps. It uses the Apple App Store Connect API and Google AndroidpublisherV3 API respectively to fetch review data, and supports features such as translation, filtering, forwarding to Slack, and logging to Google Sheets, meeting cross-platform needs.

e36e48bb9265.name: How do I configure ZReviewTender's SlackProcessor to push reviews to a specified Slack channel?

acceptedAnswer.text: SlackProcessor requires setting slackBotToken (recommended to use a Slack Bot Token with postMessages permission) and slackBotTargetChannel (Channel ID, not the name), and adding the bot to that channel. Once enabled, reviews are sent in batches according to the configured merge count, with review timestamps displayed in the specified time zone, allowing the team to receive and respond to user reviews promptly.

e36e48bb9265.name: How does ZReviewTender ensure it does not fetch the same app review multiple times?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZReviewTender records the timestamp of the last successfully fetched review, stored in the latestCheckTimestamp directory. Each run only fetches new reviews after that timestamp to avoid duplicates. If needed, you can reset by deleting the timestamp file via command, allowing a fresh fetch from the beginning.

e36e48bb9265.name: What are the limitations of ZReviewTender's Android review API?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Google AndroidpublisherV3 API only provides reviews added or edited within the last 7 days and cannot retrieve all historical reviews. Therefore, ZReviewTender recommends scheduling fetches no less frequently than every 7 days to avoid missing reviews. This is an official API limitation and cannot be bypassed programmatically.

4b9d09cea5f0.name: What tools do Pinkoi engineers usually use to improve team collaboration efficiency?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Pinkoi engineering team uses cross-team collaboration tools such as Slack, Asana, and Figma to enhance communication efficiency and manage repetitive tasks. These tools help the team maintain connections between people as the team scales, promoting smooth workflows.

4b9d09cea5f0.name: How do Pinkoi engineers help advance the company's business goals?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pinkoi engineers do more than just write code; they contribute life experiences and creative ideas during project planning stages, maintain system stability and scalability with strong development skills to reduce technical debt. Additionally, they strengthen cross-department communication and coordination, reduce rework, increase team productivity, and thereby drive business goals forward.

4b9d09cea5f0.name: Why do Pinkoi engineers choose to join a startup culture instead of a traditional large company?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pinkoi engineers value five key traits of startup culture: transparency, equality, vision, flexibility, and vibrancy. These traits allow employees to clearly understand the company's status, promote opinion expression through flat management, offer high flexibility in work and roles, and foster a young and energetic team that is more willing to try new things—qualities less commonly found in traditional large companies.

4b9d09cea5f0.name: What mindset and career planning should fresh graduates aiming to become software engineers pay attention to?

acceptedAnswer.text: Fresh graduates should stay out of their comfort zones, continuously challenge themselves, and explore multiple directions to find the field that suits them best. Lifelong learning and adaptability are key, while maintaining a work-life balance. It is recommended to focus on learning during the early career stage, choose companies with products, and stay for at least one year to benefit future career development.

4b9d09cea5f0.name: Which platforms and languages does Pinkoi's product support?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pinkoi's product supports four platforms: desktop, mobile web, iOS, and Android, and offers six languages: Traditional Chinese, Hong Kong Traditional Chinese, Simplified Chinese, Japanese, Thai, and English to meet diverse user needs.

4b9d09cea5f0.name: How is the Pinkoi engineering team organized and divided?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pinkoi engineers are assigned to more than eight different Squad Teams, each responsible for different areas such as buyer-side, seller-side, underlying platform, AI algorithms, and more. Although the division of labor is clear, the team emphasizes unity and maintains a cooperative atmosphere like a family.

4b9d09cea5f0.name: How do Pinkoi engineers conduct technical exchanges and collaboration?

acceptedAnswer.text: Teammates with the same functions, such as iOS, Android, backend, frontend, and data teams, regularly hold technical sharing sessions. They also perform mutual code reviews and discuss system design during daily development, fostering technical growth and team collaboration through these exchanges.

a5643de271e4.name: How does ZMarkupParser fix HTML tags that are missing end tags or are misaligned?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMarkupParser parses and tokenizes using Regex, automatically analyzing and correcting HTML tags that lack end tags or are misaligned. For example, it will fix `BoldBold+ItalicItalic` into `BoldBold+ItalicItalic`, ensuring the tag structure is correct before converting to NSAttributedString.

a5643de271e4.name: Which HTML tags and style customization features does ZMarkupParser support?

acceptedAnswer.text:
ZMarkupParser supports various HTML tags by default such as ``, ``, ``, `
    `, `
      `, `
    1. `, ``, ``, etc., and allows customization of corresponding MarkupStyle for styling. Users can also extend and define custom tag names and styles, for example parsing `` and applying a specified background color.

a5643de271e4.name: Compared to NSAttributedString.DocumentType.html, where does ZMarkupParser differ in performance?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMarkupParser outperforms NSAttributedString.DocumentType.html, which crashes (EXC_BAD_ACCESS) when processing HTML strings longer than 54,600 characters. In tests on macOS 13.2 with M2 hardware, ZMarkupParser rendered significantly faster and more stably, making it suitable for handling much longer HTML strings.

a5643de271e4.name: How does ZMarkupParser support specifying text styles from HTML style attributes?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMarkupParser can parse the style attribute within HTML tags and convert CSS styles into corresponding MarkupStyle. For example, `` is parsed as bold text with a font size of 20px. It supports various CSS properties including color, font size, font weight, and also allows custom extensions for new style attribute parsing.

a5643de271e4.name: How does ZMarkupParser support asynchronous rendering of long strings to avoid UI blocking?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMarkupParser provides asynchronous versions of render, stripper, and selector methods that return results via closures. This allows processing of long strings without blocking the main thread, keeping the UI responsive. For example, `parser.render(htmlString) { result in /* use result */ }` runs rendering in the background and calls back upon completion.

a5643de271e4.name: Why do UITextView and UILabel sometimes fail to apply NSAttributedString.key settings when displaying hyperlinks?

acceptedAnswer.text: UITextView’s hyperlink style is controlled by linkTextAttributes; if this property is not set, NSAttributedString.key settings will be ineffective. UILabel does not support custom URL styles natively, so even if NSAttributedString.key is set, the expected effect may not appear. This is a limitation of iOS UI components.

a5643de271e4.name: Which platforms and installation methods does ZMarkupParser support?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMarkupParser supports iOS and macOS platforms and can be installed via Swift Package Manager (SPM) or CocoaPods. Users can refer to the official Readme documentation for installation and configuration, making it easy to integrate into existing Swift projects.

2724f02f6e7.name: Why is it not recommended to render HTML strings directly using NSAttributedString.DocumentType.html?

acceptedAnswer.text: NSAttributedString.DocumentType.html renders through the WebView Core, which has poor performance and is prone to crashes, especially on iOS versions before 15 where low power mode or very long strings (over 54,600 characters) cause a 100% crash rate. Additionally, this method does not allow style customization and incorrectly removes non-standard tags like <Congratulation!>. In tests, rendering 300 characters takes 0.03 seconds, causing lag during list rendering.

2724f02f6e7.name: What issues arise when using XMLParser to parse HTML?

acceptedAnswer.text:
XMLParser requires the HTML string to fully comply with XML rules, with zero tolerance for errors. It will throw errors and return blank results when encountering tags like
, misaligned tags, or non-standard tags (such as ). This prevents proper parsing of imperfect everyday HTML and limits practical use.

2724f02f6e7.name: How does ZMarkupParser handle HTML string parsing and error tolerance?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMarkupParser uses pure Swift and regular expressions to break down the HTML string into single-tag tokens, recording start, end, and self-closing tags. It then automatically corrects missing end tags, misaligned tags, and non-standard tags through normalization, finally converting the result into an abstract syntax tree (AST) for further operations. This method does not rely on any external parsing libraries and can dynamically fix erroneous tags.

2724f02f6e7.name: How does ZMarkupParser convert HTML tags into NSAttributedString styles?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMarkupParser defines a MarkupStyle structure that abstracts NSAttributedString.Key and uses the Visitor Pattern to map HTML tags to styles. It supports dynamically parsing colors, font sizes, and more from HTML style attributes, with two inheritance strategies (favoring code or HTML styles) to merge styles, ensuring correct inheritance and application of string styles.

2724f02f6e7.name: How does ZMarkupParser support complex HTML lists and table displays?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMarkupParser obtains list item indices through the AST and uses NSTextList.MarkerFormat to generate symbols. However, since NSTextList cannot customize whitespace width, it currently presents symbols as string prefixes. For tables, it simulates column widths and truncates or pads strings with spaces. Although not perfect, this achieves an acceptable effect because native table rendering often relies on private macOS APIs.

2724f02f6e7.name: How does ZMarkupParser handle image display issues in HTML?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMarkupParser uses a custom ZNSTextAttachment object to wrap image URLs and placeholders. Images are downloaded asynchronously during loading and replace attachments to update the UI. It avoids invalidating NSLayoutManager and instead replaces NSAttributedString to ensure screen refresh. It also provides Delegate/DataSource interfaces for external extensions of image caching and download strategies.

2724f02f6e7.name: What convenient extensions does ZMarkupParser offer for UIKit users?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMarkupParser provides extensions for UITextView and UILabel, offering a setHtmlString() method to directly set HTML strings while automatically handling NSAttributedString conversion and link styling. UITextView sets linkTextAttributes, and UILabel supports NSTextAttachment image loading, simplifying UIKit interface binding workflows.

2724f02f6e7.name: How are testing and continuous integration handled for ZMarkupParser?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMarkupParser includes unit tests and snapshot tests. Snapshot tests use the swift-snapshot-testing framework to compare UITextView rendered images to ensure consistent results. Codecov monitors test coverage at about 85%. Continuous integration is handled via GitHub Actions, which automatically runs tests and uploads coverage reports to ensure stable quality.

e7c547a5be22.name: How does ZMediumToJekyll help me sync Medium articles to my Jekyll blog?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMediumToJekyll automatically downloads your articles from Medium, converts them into Markdown format, and then pushes them to your GitHub repository. It uses GitHub Actions to sync regularly. Once set up, it keeps your Medium and Jekyll blog content synchronized continuously, with a default sync frequency of once per day.

e7c547a5be22.name: How do I set my Medium username when using ZMediumToJekyll for the first time?

acceptedAnswer.text: You need to fill in your Medium username in the _zmediumtomarkdown.yml file within the project. This allows the tool to automatically download and sync your Medium articles. After setting it up, GitHub Actions will fetch content based on this account.

e7c547a5be22.name: How do I manually run the ZMediumToMarkdown sync process using GitHub Actions?

acceptedAnswer.text: Go to your GitHub repository, click on the Actions tab at the top, select the ZMediumToMarkdown workflow, then click Run workflow. Choose the main branch and execute. This will immediately trigger the download, conversion, and commit of Medium articles, allowing you to update your blog content anytime.

e7c547a5be22.name: Why does my GitHub Pages site show a 404 error or articles are not updated?

acceptedAnswer.text: This usually happens because GitHub Actions has not completed all build processes, or the Pages settings do not have the correct branch (gh-pages) and path selected. Please wait patiently for all Actions to finish running, and verify that the Settings -> Pages configuration is correct.

e7c547a5be22.name: Does ZMediumToJekyll support custom sync frequency?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. You can adjust the sync interval in the .github/workflows/ZMediumToMarkdown.yml file. The default is to run once daily. You can also manually trigger syncs at any time, allowing you to control update frequency as needed.

e7c547a5be22.name: How do I remove the watermark generated by ZMediumToMarkdown?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can edit lines 10 to 12 in tools/optimize_markdown.rb to uncomment the relevant code. After uncommenting, Jekyll will automatically remove the watermark during build, so the ZMediumToMarkdown mark will no longer appear at the bottom of your articles.

e7c547a5be22.name: If I create a repository with an organization account, how do I enable read/write permissions for GitHub Actions?

acceptedAnswer.text: Organization accounts cannot set read/write permissions on a single repository. You must enable read/write permissions for Actions in the organization settings page to allow the sync process to function properly.

76d66c2e34af.name: How to handle online check-in for a Japan Kansai independent trip to save time?

acceptedAnswer.text: Online check-in can be done at Taipei MRT A1 Taipei Main Station or A13 New Taipei Industrial Park Station, including check-in, luggage security check, and baggage drop-off. Upon arrival at the airport, you can go directly through immigration without queuing. It must be completed at least 3 hours before the flight departure and is supported by only some airlines. Service hours are 06:00~21:30 at A1 station and 09:00~16:00 at A13 station. This saves waiting time at the airport and avoids long lines at the counter.

76d66c2e34af.name: Which transportation card is more convenient for traveling in Japan? What should I pay attention to when topping up iPhone Suica?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Suica card is commonly used for transportation in the Kansai area, accepted on subways, buses, and some convenience stores. On iPhone, you can add a virtual Suica card via Apple Pay, but it is recommended to use a MasterCard credit card for topping up, as Visa cards often fail. It's best to top up in Taiwan first to avoid being unable to use it in Japan and not receiving verification SMS. Physical Suica cards are currently out of stock; only a 28-day limited edition is available and non-refundable after expiration.

76d66c2e34af.name: How to use the Visit Japan website to speed up immigration when entering Japan?

acceptedAnswer.text: After booking your flight and accommodation, register an account on the Visit Japan website and fill in your arrival flight, accommodation contact information, and basic details in order. Upon arrival in Japan, connect to the internet, log in to the website to generate a combined QR code for immigration inspection and customs declaration, then present your passport and QR code to the immigration officer for quick clearance. This saves time from filling out paper forms on-site and makes the process smoother.

76d66c2e34af.name: How to arrange transportation smoothly for an independent trip to Kyoto, Osaka, and Kobe in Kansai, Japan?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is recommended to stay near train stations. For example, in Kyoto, stay at Toyoko INN Shijo Omiya, which is conveniently located between Kinkaku-ji and Kiyomizu-dera, making it easy to visit Arashiyama. In Osaka, stay at APA Hotel Osaka Umeda, about a 15-minute walk from Umeda Station. From Kansai Airport, take the JR Kansai Airport Limited Express HARUKA directly to Kyoto Station in about 1.5 hours. The trip between Kyoto and Osaka takes about 1 hour, and Kobe is easily accessible by train.

76d66c2e34af.name: How to enjoy Universal Studios Japan without a Express Pass more comfortably?

acceptedAnswer.text: If you don't have an Express Pass, you can take a relaxed approach by entering the park later to avoid the morning rush and use the official app to draw entry tickets for special attractions (like Super Nintendo World). Start by visiting popular areas such as the Harry Potter zone, where wait times are about 45 minutes. Near closing time, when crowds thin out, many interactive scenes have almost no lines, allowing you to fully enjoy the experience.

76d66c2e34af.name: How much Japanese yen cash is appropriate to exchange for a trip to Japan?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is recommended to exchange about 50,000 yen in cash because most temple admission fees, souvenirs, omamori (charms), and some trains (especially those with reserved seats) only accept cash. On this trip, exchanging 50,000 yen left about 15,000 yen remaining, which was enough for daily expenses. Although electronic payments are widespread in Japan, cash is still indispensable.

76d66c2e34af.name: Which is more convenient for Japan travel on KKday: SIM card or eSIM?

acceptedAnswer.text: KKday offers both physical SIM cards and eSIM versions. Physical SIM cards are more commonly used and provide peace of mind, costing about 700 TWD for 8 days of unlimited data. You can swap the card and set up roaming immediately after the plane lands. eSIM is more convenient since you don’t need to change cards, but some people may not be used to it. Note that unlimited data plans in Japan will throttle speed after exceeding the data limit, so it’s recommended to use Wi-Fi for uploading or watching videos.

76d66c2e34af.name: Which hotel in Kansai offers the best value and convenient transportation for an independent trip?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Kyoto, it is recommended to stay at Toyoko INN Kyoto Shijo Omiya, which offers great value and includes a Japanese-style breakfast. It is about 3 kilometers from Kyoto Station but close to Hankyu Omiya Station, which provides direct access to Arashiyama and Osaka. In Osaka, choose APA Hotel Osaka Umeda, about a 15-minute walk from Umeda Station. It features a swimming pool and public bath, though no breakfast is provided, but transportation is convenient. Both hotels are suitable for independent travelers.

9da2c51fa4f2.name: Is it worth buying the FastPass at Tokyo DisneySea?

acceptedAnswer.text: The FastPass at Tokyo DisneySea is definitely worth buying because the queue times for popular attractions often exceed 90 minutes. The author used FastPasses for popular rides like "Soaring: Fantastic Flight," significantly saving waiting time and making the overall experience smoother. Especially on rainy days or holidays, the FastPass effectively helps avoid the fatigue of long lines.

9da2c51fa4f2.name: How to arrange accommodation conveniently for a free trip in Tokyo?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is recommended to choose a hotel near train or subway stations and located in a transportation hub area. For example, the author stayed at Hotel Villa Fontaine Grand Tokyo-Shiodome, which is right next to Shiodome Station. Shimbashi Station is about a 10-minute walk away, offering convenient access to Odaiba or Shinjuku. Such hotels are reasonably priced, clean, and comfortable, making them suitable for groups.

9da2c51fa4f2.name: When is the best time to stay at the capsule hotel near Taoyuan Airport?

acceptedAnswer.text: The capsule hotel near Taoyuan Airport is ideal for staying the night before an early flight to reduce the fatigue of waking up very early. The hotel is located on the 5th floor of the south side of Terminal 2, about a 5-minute walk from the terminal. The rooms are clean with thick mattresses, the environment is quiet and comfortable, and earplugs and toiletries are provided. The author stayed in a single bed capsule and felt well rested for an easy departure the next day.

9da2c51fa4f2.name: How to enjoy the area around Asakusa Kaminarimon in Tokyo?

acceptedAnswer.text: The area around Asakusa Kaminarimon is crowded with tourists and is great for taking photos and experiencing kimono rentals and rickshaw rides. Nearby are Senso-ji Temple and shopping streets. Although the area is quite commercialized, you can find unique souvenirs and snacks. The author recommends taking a bus to experience local transportation culture, noting that the bus card payment method differs from Kyoto.

9da2c51fa4f2.name: How to prepare transportation cards and SIM cards conveniently before a Tokyo trip?

acceptedAnswer.text: For iPhone users, it is recommended to use the Suica card directly. Android users can purchase a limited-time Welcome Suica card at Narita Airport. For SIM cards, you can buy a 5-day unlimited Japanese eSIM from KKday for about 500 TWD, which includes high-speed data, making it convenient for real-time internet access and navigation.

9da2c51fa4f2.name: How to plan visiting DisneySea and Disneyland during a Tokyo trip?

acceptedAnswer.text: If time allows, it is recommended to spend two days visiting Tokyo DisneySea and Disneyland separately because DisneySea does not have the iconic castle or parades found at Disneyland. The author only visited DisneySea this time, experiencing multiple immersive attractions and the evening fireworks show, which was fun enough but lacked the classic castle and parade activities of Disneyland.

9da2c51fa4f2.name: What are the differences in observation experiences between Tokyo Tower and Tokyo Skytree?

acceptedAnswer.text: Tokyo Tower is close to Shimbashi Station, about a 20-minute walk away. Purchasing a Top Deck ticket allows access to the very top with Chinese audio guides and commemorative photos. Tokyo Skytree is taller with a wider view, but the author only got tickets for the middle observation deck. The night view is beautiful but the glass reflections are more noticeable. The Skytree mall has ice cream shops for resting. Both have their unique features.

9da2c51fa4f2.name: How much Japanese yen is appropriate to bring for a free trip in Tokyo?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is recommended to bring at least 10,000 yen in cash as a backup in case cards cannot be used or Visa cards are declined. The author brought 60,000 yen but ended up with about 5,000 left. At a drugstore in Shinjuku, the card was declined and over 10,000 yen had to be paid in cash. Also, at Tokyo Station, cash was required to buy Narita Airport train tickets, so having cash on hand is very important.

382218e15697.name: Why use Google Apps Script to create a Github Repo Star Notifier instead of existing tools?

acceptedAnswer.text: Existing Star Notifier tools on the Github Marketplace often have notification delays, only support batch notifications (e.g., notify every 5 or 10 stars), or only support Email notifications, and some have stopped working. Using Google Apps Script to build your own solution is free, can push notifications to Line in real-time, avoids security risks, and meets the need for small projects to receive every star instantly.

382218e15697.name: How to obtain a Line Notify token for Github Repo Star notifications?

acceptedAnswer.text: After logging into Line Notify, go to the "Generate access token (For developers)" page, click "Generate token", enter the bot name, and select the message recipient (e.g., 1-on-1 chat). Finally, click "Generate token" and copy the generated token. This token will be used to authorize message sending in Google Apps Script.

382218e15697.name: How to implement Github Webhook security verification in Google Apps Script?

acceptedAnswer.text: Since Google Apps Script doPost cannot access HTTP headers, it cannot use Github's standard signature verification. Instead, a custom random string is matched via the URL query parameter ?secret=. When Github Webhook sends a request, GAS compares e.parameter.secret with the preset githubWebhookSecret to ensure the request is secure.

382218e15697.name: How to compose the Github Star event notification message inside Google Apps Script?

acceptedAnswer.text: The script retrieves the repository name, URL, star count, fork count, and the stargazer's username from the webhook payload. The message format is "🎉🎉 'User' starred your 'Repo Name' Repo 🎉🎉", listing the current total stars, forks, and the Repo URL, for example, "Current total stars: 187." This provides complete and easy-to-read information.

382218e15697.name: What settings should be noted when deploying Google Apps Script as a web app?

acceptedAnswer.text: When deploying, select the "Web app" type and set access permissions to "Anyone" to ensure Github Webhook can call it. The first deployment requires authorization; bypass the Google unverified warning by choosing "Advanced" then "Go to...". After modifying the code, create a new version and redeploy to apply updates.

382218e15697.name: How to configure Github Webhook to listen for star events?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the Github Repo or Organization settings, set up a Webhook with the Payload URL pointing to the Google Apps Script URL plus ?secret=randomstring, and set Content type to application/json. Choose "Let me select individual events," uncheck Pushes, check Watches (since Stars are part of Watches events), and enable the Webhook.

382218e15697.name: When will the Line Notify service be discontinued? How to switch to other notification methods?

acceptedAnswer.text: Line Notify will stop service on 2025/04/01. It is recommended to switch to Telegram Bot for notification integration. The author provides an up-to-date tutorial on how to quickly migrate from Line Notify to Telegram Bot within 10 minutes to keep notifications uninterrupted.

5a5c4b25a83d.name: Why is it difficult to add Unit Testing directly to existing Apps?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most existing Apps use Swift/Kotlin with static strong typing or have migrated from Objective-C to Swift, and during development, interface dependencies were not properly separated. This makes it almost impossible to add Unit Testing later. The refactoring process introduces instability, creating a chicken-and-egg dilemma.

5a5c4b25a83d.name: How does End-to-End Testing help identify process issues in existing projects?

acceptedAnswer.text: End-to-End Testing approaches from the user's perspective, operating key flows such as registration or checkout to verify if the process works correctly. It helps prevent user interruptions caused by process or logic errors. For example, errors in the checkout process directly impact revenue, and E2E Testing can detect and prevent such critical issues promptly.

5a5c4b25a83d.name: How does unstable API data affect App End-to-End Testing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Unstable API data leads to inconsistent test results, increasing the risk of false positives and causing the team to lose confidence in the Test Plan. For instance, if a product is removed or disappears during the checkout process, changes outside the App's control make tests unreliable and reduce testing effectiveness.

5a5c4b25a83d.name: How does Snapshot API Local Mock Server solve the dependency on API data?

acceptedAnswer.text: Snapshot API Local Mock Server records API Requests and Responses and replays this data during testing, removing dependency on real API data. This allows simulating API responses locally, avoiding test instability caused by data changes and improving test reliability.

5a5c4b25a83d.name: How can an iOS App automatically switch to using a local Mock Server during testing?

acceptedAnswer.text: By adding a parameter (such as "duringE2ETesting") when launching the UI Testing Target, the App's Network layer detects this parameter and replaces the API Host with the local Mock Server address (e.g., http://127.0.0.1:8080). This directs all API requests to the local server during testing, achieving environment isolation.

5a5c4b25a83d.name: How does MITMProxy implement recording and replaying of API Requests & Responses?

acceptedAnswer.text: MITMProxy itself does not provide Record & Replay functionality; it requires custom Python scripts to intercept HTTP requests and responses, hash data based on Host, method, path, and request content, and save it. During replay, the request hash is used to read the local response file and return it to the App. This method effectively simulates API responses and supports local testing.

5a5c4b25a83d.name: What is the difference between using forward proxy and reverse proxy in testing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A forward proxy requires setting a proxy in the device or simulator's network settings, routing all traffic through the proxy, which is more complicated and may conflict with other tools. A reverse proxy requires changing the API Host in the App code to the proxy server and specifying the proxy domain; only traffic to these domains goes through the proxy, making it easier to control and not affecting other traffic.

5a5c4b25a83d.name: How to avoid Replay mapping failures caused by changing parameters like timestamps in API requests?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can configure config.json to ignore specific Endpoint paths or global Query and Form Data parameters, such as timestamps, so these parameters are excluded from the request hash calculation. This allows correct mapping to local response files even if parameters change, preventing Replay failures.

7b8a0563c157.name: What are the restrictions and prices for Peach Aviation's Nagoya same-day round-trip tickets?

acceptedAnswer.text: Peach Aviation's Nagoya same-day round-trip ticket costs about 5,600 TWD, including airport service fees, but excluding checked baggage, meals, and seat selection. Carry-on luggage is limited to two pieces per person with a total weight not exceeding 7 kilograms. Both outbound and return flights are red-eye flights, departing Taipei at 2:25 AM and Nagoya at 11:15 PM respectively.

7b8a0563c157.name: How to speed up immigration when doing a Nagoya one-day quick trip to Japan?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can fill in your entry information in advance on Japan's official Visit Japan website and generate a QR Code. Presenting this QR Code upon arrival can speed up the immigration process. You can fill in the stay duration as one day and use Chubu Centrair International Airport as the contact point. The author tested this and was not asked additional questions, passing through immigration quickly.

7b8a0563c157.name: What is the most convenient way to get from Nagoya Chubu Centrair International Airport to Nagoya city center?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is recommended to take the Meitetsu Airport Limited Express train directly to Nagoya Station. The author purchased a one-way ticket on KKday for about 271 yen, which includes reserved seating and a comfortable, stable carriage. If you want to save money, you can also buy Semi-Express or Local train tickets on-site. Local train tickets allow direct station entry and boarding, making it convenient and cheaper.

7b8a0563c157.name: What are the recommended must-visit spots for a one-day Nagoya trip?

acceptedAnswer.text: Recommended spots include Nagoya Castle (the Honmaru Palace is open for visits but the main keep is under renovation), Chubu Electric Power MIRAI TOWER (formerly Nagoya TV Tower) with an observation deck overlooking the city, Osu Shopping Street, and Osu Kannon Temple. You can also try local specialties like fried shrimp toast and miso pork cutlet.

7b8a0563c157.name: How to prepare a power bank for a Nagoya one-day quick trip to ensure it lasts?

acceptedAnswer.text: The author brought a 20,000mAh Xiaomi power bank, which is enough to charge an iPhone 13 about 4–5 times, supporting all-day phone navigation, information lookup, and photography. Phone battery life is a major challenge during the trip, so it is recommended to carry a high-capacity power bank as a backup.

7b8a0563c157.name: Is the carry-on luggage inspection strict at Nagoya airport?

acceptedAnswer.text: Nagoya airport has strict carry-on luggage inspections, limiting to two pieces with a total weight not exceeding 7 kilograms. There is a re-check before boarding, and if the number or weight exceeds the limit, you will be asked to repack on site or pay extra fees. The author observed that some travelers became one-day quick trip targets because they bought a PS5.

7b8a0563c157.name: How to arrange transportation after returning to Taiwan from a Nagoya one-day quick trip?

acceptedAnswer.text: Since the flight back to Taiwan is a red-eye flight, arriving at Taoyuan Airport around 2–3 AM, public transportation is very limited. You can only take a taxi or the more expensive Uber to Taipei. Public buses usually start running around 4–5 AM, so it is recommended to arrange transportation in advance.

d78e0b15a08a.name: Is the JR Pass worth buying for a free independent trip in Kyushu?

acceptedAnswer.text: The JR Pass does save money for a free independent trip in Kyushu; the author saved at least over 1,000 TWD using the five-day pass. It allows travel on most Kyushu Shinkansen and JR trains, but does not include the Nozomi and Mizuho trains, which require an additional fare. Considering Kyushu's vast area, the JR Pass effectively reduces transportation costs.

d78e0b15a08a.name: What are the differences between accommodations in Kumamoto and Fukuoka in Kyushu?

acceptedAnswer.text: Fukuoka offers more accommodation options with reasonable prices and better quality. For example, the Benikea Calton Hotel where the author stayed costs about 1,516 TWD per night and is conveniently located. Kumamoto has fewer choices and higher prices; for instance, the Green Rich Hotel costs about 2,039 TWD per night, and the hotel is older with poor soundproofing and slow WiFi. Overall, Fukuoka accommodations offer better value than Kumamoto.

d78e0b15a08a.name: What interesting experiences might solo travelers encounter on a free independent trip in Kyushu?

acceptedAnswer.text: Although solo travel can be lonely, it offers many interesting interactions. For example, the author once helped a Japanese family carry souvenirs, met a kind foreign lady who helped take photos, and traveled with a Taiwanese family and a TSMC engineer to Mount Aso. These experiences make solo travel richer and more meaningful.

d78e0b15a08a.name: What are the must-visit recommended spots for a free independent trip in Kyushu?

acceptedAnswer.text: Recommended spots include Nagasaki's Shinchi Chinatown, Glover Garden, Atomic Bomb Museum, and Inasayama night view; Fukuoka's Dazaifu Tenmangu Shrine, Yanagawa boat tours, Canal City Hakata, and Fukuoka Tower; Kumamoto's Kumamoto Castle, Suizenji Jojuen Garden, and Mount Aso. These locations cover history, culture, and natural beauty, perfect for an in-depth Kyushu experience.

d78e0b15a08a.name: What should I pay attention to when taking buses and trams during a free independent trip in Kyushu?

acceptedAnswer.text: Buses usually board from the rear and exit from the front; some routes require taking a numbered ticket upon boarding from a machine. Electronic payments like Suica can be tapped directly, but the balance cannot go negative. Japanese buses wait until passengers have fully boarded or alighted before moving and do not give change. When getting off, pay the fare corresponding to your ticket number. These rules differ from Taiwan, so first-time riders should be careful.

d78e0b15a08a.name: How to arrange transportation between Fukuoka and Kumamoto for a free independent trip in Kyushu?

acceptedAnswer.text: Transportation between Fukuoka and Kumamoto can be done via JR trains, taking about one hour. The author suggests planning your desired attractions first, then arranging accommodations to avoid staying too long in Kumamoto. Kumamoto Airport is convenient, with buses directly connecting nearby hotels. Fukuoka offers more transportation options, with easy access to subways and buses. Properly planning routes can save time and money.

d78e0b15a08a.name: How can solo travelers overcome language barriers during a free independent trip in Kyushu?

acceptedAnswer.text: The author learned simple Japanese words such as numbers, checkout phrases, and plastic bag requests, using Google Translate to confirm pronunciation, reducing language barriers. Solo travelers also encounter kind people who help with photos or communication. Although language difficulties can feel isolating, embracing solitude and self-reflection is part of the value of solo travel.

d78e0b15a08a.name: What food and drinks are recommended for a free independent trip in Kyushu?

acceptedAnswer.text: Recommended foods include Miyazaki beef steak, Hakata Gion iron pot gyoza, Katsuretsu-tei thick-cut pork cutlet rice, fruit ice near Yanagawa boat tours, and Hōraku steamed buns. For drinks, draft cola, peach water, Akiya plum wine, and FamilyMart’s fruit juices are all delicious. Draft cola has strong carbonation without being overly sweet, ideal for those wanting to try something new.

31b9b3a63abc.name: How are the ticket prices and flight times for Tigerair's service to and from Okayama?

acceptedAnswer.text: Tigerair's round-trip ticket to and from Okayama, including 20KG checked baggage, costs about 7,012 TWD. The flight times are very convenient, with the outbound flight departing at 11:10 and the return flight at 15:25. This was the best price and time option the author found, including seat selection and fees, suitable for budget-conscious travelers who want to leave early and return late.

31b9b3a63abc.name: Is the JR Pass for Okayama, Hiroshima, and Yamaguchi area worth buying?

acceptedAnswer.text: The JR Pass for the Okayama, Hiroshima, and Yamaguchi area costs about 17,000 yen and covers multiple round-trip tickets between Okayama and Hiroshima stations, which alone cost about 6,460 yen one way. It also includes transportation to places like Miyajima, Onomichi, and Kure. The author believes it is very cost-effective and convenient based on the itinerary, and buying the JR Pass directly is the easiest option.

31b9b3a63abc.name: How does the Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki at Hiroshima Okonomiyaki Story Station Front Plaza taste?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki at Hiroshima Okonomiyaki Story Station Front Plaza tastes average. The author ordered the fried noodle Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki with added mochi; it was filling but not particularly impressive in flavor. The floor hosts many Hiroshima-style okonomiyaki vendors, allowing visitors to choose freely, which is suitable for travelers wanting to try a variety.

31b9b3a63abc.name: What is the best way to take the Miyajima Ropeway?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Miyajima Ropeway is divided into two sections. The first section uses small cable cars that hold up to 6 people, run frequently, and cover a longer distance. The second section uses larger cable cars seating about 20 people, departing every 15 minutes, covering a shorter distance. The author recommends taking the ropeway directly to the Shishiiwa Observatory first because it is less crowded and offers panoramic views of the Seto Inland Sea, avoiding the crowds at Itsukushima Shrine.

31b9b3a63abc.name: Is the Cat Alley in Onomichi still worth visiting?

acceptedAnswer.text: Visiting Onomichi's Cat Alley this year felt somewhat desolate, with almost no stray cats seen and most cat cafés along the route closed. The author suggests that if you want to experience Cat Alley, expect a quieter atmosphere, and there are a few open cafés nearby where you can rest.

31b9b3a63abc.name: Are the Yamato Museum and the Maritime Self-Defense Force Kure Museum worth visiting?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Yamato Museum exhibits models of the Yamato battleship, war history, and fighter planes, making it a must-visit for military enthusiasts. The Maritime Self-Defense Force Kure Museum offers free admission and displays the interior life and equipment of submarines, including the opportunity to enter real submarine compartments. Both museums are highly recommended, especially for those interested in military history.

31b9b3a63abc.name: When is the nighttime illumination event held at Okayama Korakuen and Okayama Castle?

acceptedAnswer.text: The nighttime illumination event at Okayama Korakuen and Okayama Castle is held annually in mid to late November. The author visited during this period and enjoyed the romantic illuminated night scenery, with maple leaves beautifully complementing the lights, making it a great autumn travel plan.

31b9b3a63abc.name: What should I pay attention to when taking the shuttle bus at Okayama Momotaro Airport?

acceptedAnswer.text: Okayama Momotaro Airport has few shuttle bus departures, but extra runs are added to ensure all passengers can board. The airport is small, and the security X-ray machine is located in the lobby, so you must pass through X-ray screening before check-in. Passengers wearing boots need to remove their shoes for security checks. The author experienced a nearly one-hour flight delay and recommends allowing plenty of time.

bd94cc88f9c9.name: How can the Slack App set its own OpenAI API Key to use ChatGPT features?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Slack App itself does not officially provide a service to set your own OpenAI API Key, so you need to develop it yourself. This article demonstrates using Google Cloud Functions and Python to create an intermediary service that calls the OpenAI API when the Slack App receives a message event, returning ChatGPT responses with a custom API Key. This allows team members to freely use their own API Keys, avoiding subscription limits and data security concerns.

bd94cc88f9c9.name: Why use Google Cloud Functions to deploy the integration service between Slack and OpenAI API?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Cloud Functions offers Function as a Service (FaaS), making it easy and quick to deploy with free quotas, which is suitable for rapidly developing backend services for Slack Apps. It supports automatic scaling, and you can deploy directly after writing the code. However, the downside is that if the service is not called for a long time, it enters cold start, which may make it difficult to meet Slack's 3-second response time limit, and it's harder to have multiple services interact with each other.

bd94cc88f9c9.name: How does the Slack App handle user Direct Messages and reply with OpenAI API responses?

acceptedAnswer.text: When the Slack App receives a Direct Message event, Cloud Functions parses the event JSON to extract the channel, message content, and timestamp, then calls the OpenAI API to generate a response. The response is sent back in segments using Slack API's chat.postMessage and chat.update, with the X-Slack-No-Retry header to prevent duplicate requests. This enables real-time interaction similar to ChatGPT.

bd94cc88f9c9.name: How does the Slack App implement the function to interrupt OpenAI API responses to save tokens?

acceptedAnswer.text: By adding a message Shortcut in the Slack App (e.g., 'Stop OpenAI API Response'), users can click on a message to interrupt the response. Cloud Functions obtains the message ID from the Shortcut payload and uses the chat.update API to add metadata marking it as interrupted. The OpenAI API response stream checks this mark and stops responding if an interruption is detected, preventing token waste.

bd94cc88f9c9.name: How to enable the Slack App to include context within the same thread to improve conversation coherence?

acceptedAnswer.text: When processing message events, if a thread_ts (thread ID) is detected, the Slack API conversations.replies is used to retrieve previous messages in that thread. These historical messages are labeled as assistant or user depending on whether they are Slack App responses. They are added to the OpenAI API's messages parameter so the model can understand the context, enhancing multi-turn conversation coherence and accuracy.

bd94cc88f9c9.name: How does the Slack App respond when mentioned (app_mention) in a channel?

acceptedAnswer.text: You need to add the app_mention event in the Slack App event subscriptions and reinstall the App. When an app_mention event is received, Cloud Functions removes the mention prefix (e.g., <@SLACKAPPID>) from the message to get the plain text, then calls the OpenAI API to generate a response. This allows the bot to answer questions whenever it is mentioned in any channel.

bd94cc88f9c9.name: How to troubleshoot if the Slack App deployed on Google Cloud Functions does not respond?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, verify that the Slack Bot Token is correct and that Cloud Functions is successfully deployed without errors. Check Cloud Functions Logs for any abnormal messages and confirm that the Slack App has been added to the channel where questions are asked (bots need to be invited to non-1:1 channels). Finally, add logging in the code to record Slack API responses to help identify the problem.

bd94cc88f9c9.name: How to add security verification to Cloud Functions to prevent abuse of the public URL?

acceptedAnswer.text: Add a custom security verification token in Cloud Functions and require requests to include a token query parameter. The code checks if request.args contains the correct token, and if not, returns a 400 access denied response. This way, even if the URL is public, unauthorized users cannot call the service.

f6713ba3fee3.name: How to use Google Apps Script to automatically fetch daily data from Google Analytics and fill it into Google Sheets?

acceptedAnswer.text: Using Google Apps Script, you can connect to the Google Analytics Data API, write a script to retrieve device category (PC, Mobile) Sessions data for a specified date, and then fill the data into a designated Google Sheet. This is done by querying with the AnalyticsData.Properties.runReport method and manipulating the Sheet with SpreadsheetApp to complete the daily data auto-fill. The example connects with GA Property ID 318495208 and fills the corresponding columns.

f6713ba3fee3.name: What is the difference between Google Apps Script and Cloud Functions when doing RPA automation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Apps Script is suitable for quick, free, and tightly integrated simple automation with Google services, requiring no complex authentication, with a 6-minute execution time limit. Cloud Functions supports multiple languages, package management, more complex external API integrations, and authentication, making it suitable for complex or cross-platform needs but has cold start and cost issues. If mainly operating within Google Workspace, Google Apps Script is the preferred choice.

f6713ba3fee3.name: How to set up a schedule in Google Apps Script to automatically run daily data report updates?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the Google Apps Script editor, add a trigger in the 'Triggers' tab, select the function to run (e.g., cronjob), choose the event source as time-driven, select the trigger type as a daily timer, and set the execution time window (e.g., between 4 AM and 5 AM). This way, the script will run automatically every day to update the previous day's data report. Error notifications can also be configured for real-time or daily summaries.

f6713ba3fee3.name: What execution time or feature limitations of Google Apps Script should be noted?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Apps Script has a maximum script execution time of 6 minutes; exceeding this will terminate the script. It does not allow customizing the HTTP Request User-Agent and does not support package management or version control features. Users must authorize the script to access relevant Google service permissions and be mindful of quota limits. These limitations make it suitable for simple and quick Google service automation, while more complex needs are better served by Cloud Functions.

f6713ba3fee3.name: Why use Google Apps Script to create a Web GUI Dashboard to display daily data?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Apps Script can use HtmlService to convert data inside Google Sheets into HTML tables, combined with JavaScript for timed Ajax requests to auto-update, achieving a simple Web Dashboard. This method allows teams to monitor daily data changes in real-time on large screens, is completely free, and requires no external servers. The example is designed to refresh automatically every hour.

f6713ba3fee3.name: How does Google Apps Script get or create the corresponding date's Google Sheet tab?

acceptedAnswer.text: The script names the tab by year and month (yyyy-MM), tries to get the tab first, and if it doesn't exist, creates a new one. When adding, it sets the first column headers, width, freezes rows and columns, and adds a total column. This allows managing report data by month, making maintenance and queries easier.

f6713ba3fee3.name: How to evaluate whether investing resources in RPA automation is worthwhile?

acceptedAnswer.text: The evaluation criterion is whether the product of development time and task frequency exceeds the manual time spent. For example, if a routine task takes 15 minutes each time, occurs 10 times a week, wasting about 130 hours a year, plus context switching costs possibly near 200 hours, investing 40 hours to develop RPA is very cost-effective. The chart's green area indicates automation is worthwhile, and red strongly recommends it.

f6713ba3fee3.name: How does Google Apps Script authorize and ensure the execution identity has permission to access related resources?

acceptedAnswer.text: When running for the first time or introducing new services, an account selection and authorization window will pop up, requiring authorization with a Google account that has the necessary permissions. After authorization, the script runs under that identity, ensuring access to Google Sheets, Analytics, and other resources. If the account's permissions change or are disabled, the script may fail.

b04f4fba3cf2.name: How to backup .ipa installation files using Apple Configurator 2 after an app is removed from the store?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, install Apple Configurator 2 on your Mac, connect your iPhone and trust the computer, and confirm the target app is installed on the phone. Open Apple Configurator 2, select the iPhone home screen to enter the info page, switch to "Apps" and log in with your App Store account. Search for and add the target app. Do not operate during the download process. Then open Finder and go to the path ~/Library/Group Containers/K36BKF7T3D.group.com.apple.configurator/Library/Caches/Assets/TemporaryItems/MobileApps, copy the appearing .ipa file to complete the backup.

b04f4fba3cf2.name: How to use mitmproxy to backup an app's API Response Data?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, install mitmproxy on your Mac, set your phone's network proxy to point to your Mac's IP at port 8080, and install and trust the mitmproxy root certificate on your phone. After starting mitmproxy, the app traffic on your phone will be intercepted, allowing you to capture API requests and responses. Use the open-source mitmproxy-rodo script to record requests and replay them. By matching responses through hash values, you can backup and reproduce API Response Data.

b04f4fba3cf2.name: How to restore .ipa installation files after an app is deleted from the store?

acceptedAnswer.text: Connect the iPhone of the app you want to restore to your Mac, open Apple Configurator 2, go to the app adding interface, select "Choose from my Mac..." at the bottom left, select the backed-up .ipa file and click "Add." After the transfer and installation are complete, you can reopen the app on your phone and successfully revive it.

b04f4fba3cf2.name: Why exclude certain API parameters to improve mitmproxy replay success rate?

acceptedAnswer.text: mitmproxy-rodo matches corresponding responses by calculating the hash value of API request parameters. If certain parameters, such as timestamps (ts), change with each request, the hash will not match and the backup response cannot be found. Excluding these parameters that do not affect the returned content can improve replay hit rates, ensuring the app displays the recorded data correctly locally.

b04f4fba3cf2.name: How to record iPhone app operation screen using QuickTime Player?

acceptedAnswer.text: Open QuickTime Player on your Mac, select "File" -> "New Movie Recording," click the dropdown arrow next to the record button, and choose the connected iPhone screen and speaker. Click the record button to start recording, operate the app on your phone, then click the record button again to stop. Finally, use Command+S to save the video.

b04f4fba3cf2.name: In app technical backup, what do 'bone' and 'meat' refer to?

acceptedAnswer.text: "Bone" refers to the app's installation file (.ipa), which disappears when the app is removed from the App Store; "meat" refers to the data content obtained from the app's API server, which disappears when the server is shut down. Backing up the bone allows app installation recovery, backing up the meat allows reproduction of app content data. Together, they preserve the app's final state completely.

b04f4fba3cf2.name: What is the man-in-the-middle attack principle of mitmproxy?

acceptedAnswer.text: mitmproxy intercepts network requests between the phone and server by setting up a proxy server and replaces HTTPS encrypted connections with a self-signed root certificate, so traffic passes through mitmproxy first. This allows monitoring and recording of request and response content, achieving network traffic sniffing and analysis.

9903c9783a97.name: Why is it no longer recommended to use the self-hosted version of Plane.so?

acceptedAnswer.text: Subsequent versions of Plane.so have incorporated many features into paid plans. Even for self-hosting, a license must be purchased to activate the service, and there are user number limits. The pricing plans are confusing, and there is no guarantee that paid plans won’t introduce exclusive features in the future. This no longer aligns with the original vision of open-source and free usage.

9903c9783a97.name: How to install the self-hosted Plane.so version using Docker?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, create a directory and download the official installation script. After ensuring the Docker environment is running, execute the script and select the installation option. Wait for the Docker images to finish downloading, then enter the plane-app directory to adjust the .env configuration file. Finally, start the Plane service. For example, on macOS with Docker, the installation process includes mkdir, curl to download setup.sh, chmod to set execution permission, and then running ./setup.sh and choosing option 1 to install.

9903c9783a97.name: How to back up data for the self-hosted Plane.so version?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can use the custom plane-backup.sh script to back up PostgreSQL, Redis, and uploaded files. Backups are stored in timestamped directories, and you can set a maximum number of backups to automatically delete the oldest ones. Ensure Plane is running when executing the script. The command format is ./plane-backup.sh backup_target_folder Docker_project_name max_backup_count, with a default retention of 7 backups.

9903c9783a97.name: How to restore data for the self-hosted Plane.so version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use the plane-restore.sh script. After running it, input the path of the backup folder you want to restore and the Docker project name (can be left blank to default to plane-app). Upon confirmation, it will restore the PostgreSQL dump.sql, Redis, and uploaded files. After completion, you need to restart the Plane service for changes to take effect.

9903c9783a97.name: How to configure Nginx reverse proxy to support custom domains for the self-hosted Plane.so version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Change the Plane service's NGINX_PORT to a port other than 80 (e.g., 8081), and set Plane's WEB_URL to point to your custom domain. Then create a separate Nginx Docker reverse proxy service configured with proxy_pass pointing to the Plane Docker network service. Finally, use DNS or the hosts file to point the domain to the server's IP. Users can then access Plane via the custom domain.

9903c9783a97.name: What should be noted when upgrading the self-hosted Plane.so version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Be sure to back up your data before upgrading, as there is no guarantee that upgrading during the development stage won’t cause data loss. The upgrade process is to run ./setup.sh and enter 5 to pull the latest images and restart the service. After upgrading, check if the .env configuration needs adjustment and confirm that the backup script still works properly.

9903c9783a97.name: How many users does the self-hosted Plane.so version support?

acceptedAnswer.text: The official recommendation is that on infrastructure with 4GB RAM and 2vCPU, performance will degrade when the number of users exceeds 50. If the user count goes beyond this, hardware upgrades are necessary to maintain smooth operation.

9d0f23784359.name: Why is Plane.so no longer recommended for use?

acceptedAnswer.text: Plane.so is not recommended because it originally attracted users with its open-source and self-hosting advantages, but later versions incorporated many features into paid plans. Self-hosting now requires purchasing certificates and has user limits. The pricing plans are confusing and frequently updated, causing paying users to potentially miss out on new features.

9d0f23784359.name: What are the limitations of Plane.so's Self-Hosted version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Plane.so's Self-Hosted version, called the Community Edition, offers basic features for free, but advanced features require purchasing the Pro plan. Self-hosting also requires buying certificates to enable some functions and has user limits, so it is not completely free and unrestricted as initially expected.

9d0f23784359.name: How does the Cycle feature work in Plane.so?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cycle is similar to a Sprint; it is an iterative time period where each Issue can only belong to one Cycle. Users must manually create Cycles, and the time periods cannot overlap or be set in the past. The Cycle page provides a burndown chart and execution status to help track Sprint progress.

9d0f23784359.name: How does Plane.so use AI to assist in creating Issue content?

acceptedAnswer.text: Plane.so offers an AI button where users input prompts, and the system automatically generates default Issue content. Users can directly apply this to the Issue description, improving creation efficiency. This feature speeds up content generation, especially useful when quickly filling in task details.

9d0f23784359.name: What is the difference between Plane.so and Asana in task assignment?

acceptedAnswer.text: Plane.so supports assigning multiple Assignees to one Issue, which is more flexible than Asana that only allows one Assignee per Task. This enables multiple team members to be responsible for the same task, facilitating team collaboration and division of work.

9d0f23784359.name: What are the ways to view Issues in Plane.so?

acceptedAnswer.text: Plane.so provides various viewing modes including Gantt chart, calendar, kanban board, list, and sheet. Users can save filter conditions and display settings as custom Views, making it easy to quickly switch and manage diverse task needs.

9d0f23784359.name: Which third-party integrations does Plane.so support?

acceptedAnswer.text: Currently, the free version of Plane.so only supports third-party integrations with GitHub and Slack, which need to be enabled in the Workspace settings. Other integrations and automation features are mostly developed in paid plans or future versions.

9d0f23784359.name: How does Plane.so solve the issue of comments being auto-submitted when typing Chinese?

acceptedAnswer.text: When typing Chinese in Plane.so's Issue comments, selecting characters causes the comment to be submitted immediately, and the official fix is unlikely. I wrote a JavaScript script combined with a browser injection plugin to intercept the Enter key event, so pressing Enter does not auto-submit. Instead, Shift+Enter creates a new line, and comments are submitted manually by clicking the Comment button.

2981dc0fcd58.name: Which is more suitable for implementing list indentation in iOS NSAttributedString: NSTextList or NSTextTab?

acceptedAnswer.text: Both NSTextList and NSTextTab can achieve list indentation, but NSTextTab offers more flexible control, such as customizing bullet symbols, precise control over indentation spacing, and line alignment. Although NSTextList can easily add nested lists, its spacing is too large and it cannot add a dot after numbers, making it less practical. In practice, using NSTextTab combined with calculating font width to set tabStops and headIndent can achieve perfect list indentation.

2981dc0fcd58.name: What issues arise when using NSTextList to implement multi-level nested lists in iOS NSAttributedString?

acceptedAnswer.text: When implementing multi-level nested lists with NSTextList, you cannot control the spacing between bullet symbols and text, numeric items cannot automatically add a dot (e.g., "1."), and when the parent list is unordered and the child list is ordered, the starting number of the child list becomes invalid. Additionally, the spacing is too wide and multi-line alignment is poor, making the nested structure unclear and reducing practicality.

2981dc0fcd58.name: How to use NSTextTab to set list indentation positions in iOS NSAttributedString?

acceptedAnswer.text: When using NSTextTab to implement list indentation, set the text position of each "	" through the tabStops array of NSMutableParagraphStyle, adjust the line start point with headIndent, and control the default tab spacing with defaultTabInterval. You need to calculate the bullet symbol width and indentation distance yourself to ensure multi-line text aligns with the bullet symbol, achieving precise indentation.

2981dc0fcd58.name: What are the limitations of NSTextList's marker(forItemNumber:) method?

acceptedAnswer.text: The marker(forItemNumber:) method generates the symbol string for list items but cannot customize the symbol format, such as automatically adding a dot after numbers. NSTextList's API also restricts usage to default MarkerFormat styles and does not allow adjusting the spacing between the symbol and text, limiting customization.

2981dc0fcd58.name: Why do you need to use font width to calculate indentation when using NSTextTab?

acceptedAnswer.text: NSTextTab requires precise setting of each tabStop's position (location), and the length of the bullet symbol affects the indentation start point. Therefore, you must calculate the actual width of the symbol string using the font width to correctly adjust headIndent and tabStops, ensuring multi-line text aligns with the symbol after wrapping and avoiding layout issues.

2981dc0fcd58.name: How to support line wrapping while maintaining alignment in list indentation for iOS NSAttributedString?

acceptedAnswer.text: By setting multiple tabStops with NSTextTab and setting NSMutableParagraphStyle's headIndent to the position of the second tabStop, wrapped lines will align starting after the bullet symbol, maintaining neat indentation for multi-line list items and preventing misalignment on line breaks.

2981dc0fcd58.name: Can NSTextList and NSTextTab be used together to implement list indentation?

acceptedAnswer.text: NSTextList and NSTextTab are independent attributes and do not need to be used together. The article emphasizes an "or" relationship; both can independently implement list indentation, but NSTextTab offers finer typography control, so usually one method is chosen.

cb65fd5ab770.name: What are the important notes and restrictions when using the JR Pass for a Kyushu self-guided trip?

acceptedAnswer.text: When using the JR Pass, note that limited express tickets and regular tickets are separate; you need to purchase a limited express ticket separately to board limited express trains, but JR Pass holders can board without extra charge. Some Shinkansen trains like the Nozomi and Mizuho are not allowed with the JR Pass and require separate tickets. At small stations without a stationmaster, you must exit from the first car. When exchanging the JR Pass with an online reservation, you must bring the same credit card used for verification. When riding non-reserved seats, conductors will check tickets and ask for your destination.

cb65fd5ab770.name: What are the boarding experiences and important notes when taking the New Camellia cruise from Busan to Hakata?

acceptedAnswer.text: The New Camellia cruise's economy dormitory is mixed-gender with limited space and lights out at 11 PM. The public bath requires full nudity. There is no food service on board, so you need to bring your own food or buy microwave meals. The hot water dispenser is located at the rear of the cabins. Luggage is carried on board by passengers with no checked baggage restrictions. Meat products are prohibited from entering Japan. The ship experiences slight rocking during the voyage; those prone to seasickness are advised to take motion sickness medicine.

cb65fd5ab770.name: How to book and use the Yufuin no Mori train during a Kyushu trip?

acceptedAnswer.text: You must purchase a JR Pass before booking the Yufuin no Mori train, and it is recommended to reserve through designated travel agencies like KLOOK. If Yufuin no Mori is fully booked, you can reserve the regular Yufu No.1 train. When booking, ensure the train name is correct and note that reservation hours are from 5:30 AM to 11:00 PM Japan time. The reservation fee is 1000 yen per adult, and you must bring the credit card used for booking for verification when collecting tickets.

cb65fd5ab770.name: What are the differences and usage tips for Wowpass and Tmoney transportation cards when traveling in Busan, South Korea?

acceptedAnswer.text: Wowpass is a stored-value card that also functions as a cash card usable for shopping and currency exchange, while Tmoney is dedicated to transportation. Their chips are separate; when taking subways or buses, use the Tmoney chip to avoid failed scans caused by Wowpass. Wowpass balance can be checked via an app, while Tmoney balance requires a card reader. Wowpass issuance requires a 5000 KRW deposit but can be applied for free by skipping the deposit.

cb65fd5ab770.name: What are the recommended spots and touring tips for the Beppu Jigoku (Hell) Tour in Kyushu?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Beppu Jigoku Tour recommends visiting the seven major hells: Umi Jigoku (Sea Hell), Oniishibozu Jigoku, Kamado Jigoku, Oniyama Jigoku, Shiraike Jigoku, Chinoike Jigoku (Blood Pond Hell), and Tatsumaki Jigoku (Tornado Hell). Buying a set of seven tickets is more cost-effective. Kamado Jigoku offers a special smoke experience and foot baths, while Tatsumaki Jigoku erupts intermittently every 30-40 minutes. Each hell has a free foot bath area. It is advised to visit in order and allocate time to watch the eruption at Tatsumaki Jigoku.

cb65fd5ab770.name: How to effectively use luggage storage lockers (Coin Lockers) during a Kyushu trip?

acceptedAnswer.text: Major stations and attractions in Kyushu have Coin Lockers, but capacity is limited and popular locations often fill up quickly. It is recommended to check availability in advance and use them early. Some locations lack electronic reservation systems, and manual counters have limited storage hours, so pay attention to business hours. Larger suitcases have fewer available lockers, and storage fees are around 1000 yen. Websites like cloak.ecbo.io can help check availability, but not all storage locations are listed.

cb65fd5ab770.name: What should be noted when rowing boats at Takachiho Gorge during a Kyushu trip?

acceptedAnswer.text: Boat rowing at Takachiho Gorge requires booking at least three days in advance due to limited slots. It is recommended to schedule the 12:00 or 12:30 time slots as part of a day tour. The rowing trip lasts about 30-45 minutes, with the tour ending around 13:20 when participants gather to return. If you lack stamina or did not book the boat, shuttle buses are available to reach the gorge directly, saving walking time. Confirm details with official sources or guides before booking.

cb65fd5ab770.name: How to choose accommodation and transportation in Kyushu to save costs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Choosing chain economy hotels like Toyoko INN can reduce accommodation costs, averaging under 1400 yen per night, with members enjoying various discounts. For transportation, using the JR Pass for limited express and non-reserved trains saves fare and time, although some Shinkansen services require separate tickets. Luggage lockers and transportation cards like Tmoney and Wowpass improve convenience. Economy dormitory cabins on cruises are budget-friendly but have simple facilities, suitable for travelers on a tight budget.

5033090c18ba.name: Why can't iOS WKWebView implement a complete cache mechanism by itself?

acceptedAnswer.text: iOS WKWebView runs on a separate thread outside the app's main thread, and all requests and operations do not go through the app. Therefore, it is impossible to intercept or customize the complete cache process. The system has a built-in HTTP Cache that automatically handles caching based on Cache Headers, so developers neither need nor are recommended to implement it themselves. For example, URLProtocol cannot intercept WKWebView requests, which makes custom cache solutions unfeasible.

5033090c18ba.name: Does preloading the entire page in WKWebView cause performance or data usage issues?

acceptedAnswer.text: Although preloading the entire page is theoretically possible, it consumes a large amount of network traffic and system resources because users may not browse all preloaded pages. Testing has shown that this approach has more drawbacks than benefits, easily causing performance bottlenecks and data waste, so it is not recommended in practice.

5033090c18ba.name: How to use HTML Link Preload to achieve effective resource preloading in WKWebView?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can use the <link rel="preload"> tag in the head section of the HTML page to preload specific resource files (such as .js, .css, fonts, images), allowing WKWebView to download and cache these cross-page shared resources in advance. This enables users to use the cache directly when entering the page, reducing network requests. The prerequisite is that these resources must support HTTP Cache; otherwise, they will still be fetched over the network.

5033090c18ba.name: Why is WKURLSchemeHandler not suitable for implementing complete preload caching in WKWebView?

acceptedAnswer.text: WKURLSchemeHandler can only intercept custom scheme requests that the system does not recognize and cannot handle http/https protocols. Using it requires changing the page resource paths and handling cross-origin request (CORS) issues, as well as implementing your own caching strategy. This involves high cost and is difficult to maintain. After testing and evaluation, although this solution is feasible, its benefits are limited and it is complex, so it is generally not recommended.

5033090c18ba.name: How to enable Safari Developer Tools on iOS WKWebView to inspect cache status?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Debug Builds on iOS 16.4 and above, set WKWebView's isInspectable property to true and start page loading while debugging step-by-step in Xcode. Then, in Safari's developer menu, select the corresponding project for the simulator to see WKWebView's developer tools, including resource loading and cache status. This helps verify whether the HTTP Cache is effective.

5033090c18ba.name: How to configure URLSession cache size in an iOS app to optimize WKWebView resource caching?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can adjust the cache size by setting URLCache's memoryCapacity and diskCapacity, for example, setting 512MB memory cache and 10GB disk cache, and assign this URLCache to the URLSessionConfiguration's urlCache. This can increase resource cache capacity, but note that WKWebView and URLSession caches are not shared and must be managed separately.

5033090c18ba.name: Why is front-end optimization more effective in improving WKWebView page load speed?

acceptedAnswer.text: The core of WKWebView is the web page itself, and the load speed is limited by the optimization of the page's HTML, JavaScript, and resource files. Front-end optimizations such as improving First Contentful Paint, implementing proper HTTP Cache, and reducing script size can directly shorten user wait times and have a much greater effect than forcibly preloading on the app side. This also improves performance on mobile devices and SEO.

cefdf4d41746.name: How can Taiwanese writers join the Medium Partner Program?

acceptedAnswer.text: Taiwanese writers who want to join the Medium Partner Program must first become a Medium Member by subscribing to a paid membership (minimum $4 per month). Then, register a Stripe account and link it, fill out tax information (non-US citizens should select the W-8BEN form), and finally apply to join and agree to the terms on the Medium Partner Program page.

cefdf4d41746.name: How can earnings from the Medium Partner Program be withdrawn?

acceptedAnswer.text: Earnings from the Medium Partner Program can only be withdrawn through Stripe. Stripe's fees are cheaper than PayPal and it focuses on payment processing. Taiwanese users need to provide bank account information (including SWIFT code) to complete the setup. However, there are currently issues with Stripe withdrawals in Taiwan, and a US bank account may be required for smooth transfers.

cefdf4d41746.name: Why do Medium Partner Program articles need to be behind a paywall to earn money?

acceptedAnswer.text: Only articles set behind a paywall can be fully read by paying members, and Medium allocates earnings based on the reading behavior of these paying members. Articles not behind a paywall cannot generate revenue share.

cefdf4d41746.name: How is the revenue distribution calculated in the Medium Partner Program?

acceptedAnswer.text: Revenue is mainly distributed based on the number of reads by paying members. A 'read' is counted when a paying user stays on the article for more than 30 seconds. The payout amount is adjusted according to factors such as traffic, claps, and shares, with each read generating approximately $0.01 to $0.07.

cefdf4d41746.name: Is a paid membership required to join the Medium Partner Program?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, you must be a Medium Member with a paid subscription before joining the Medium Partner Program. The subscription fee is at least $4 per month. This is Medium's requirement to start earning revenue.

cefdf4d41746.name: How do you use Medium's custom domain feature?

acceptedAnswer.text: Medium's custom domain feature has recently reopened. Users must be Medium Members with a paid subscription to link their own domain. Once set up, article URLs can use the custom domain, enhancing brand image and professionalism.

cefdf4d41746.name: How do non-US citizens handle tax reporting when joining the Medium Partner Program?

acceptedAnswer.text: Non-US citizens should select the W-8BEN form when filling out tax information for the Medium Partner Program. Fill in personal information in English and check the box indicating no claim for tax treaty benefits. After completing and submitting the form, no US tax identification number is required.

755509180ca8.name: What is the relationship between the Vision framework and Vision Pro?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Vision framework is Apple's image recognition machine learning framework and has no direct relation to Vision Pro.
