The article compares their relationship to that between a hot dog and a dog, emphasizing that the Vision framework focuses on image analysis and recognition features.

755509180ca8.name: What important new features does the Vision framework add in iOS 18?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Vision framework in iOS 18 introduces an image aesthetics scoring feature that evaluates photo quality, lighting, angle, and memorable points. It also supports simultaneous detection of body and hand gestures, facilitating the development of more complex motion applications. Additionally, the API adopts Swift Concurrency to improve performance and ease of use.

755509180ca8.name: How does the Vision framework in iOS 18 optimize image recognition performance using Swift Concurrency?

acceptedAnswer.text: The iOS 18 Vision framework uses Swift Concurrency's async/await and TaskGroup to process multiple image recognitions concurrently, enhancing loading efficiency. It also allows limiting the maximum number of concurrent tasks (e.g., up to 5) to avoid excessive memory consumption and crashes, balancing performance and stability.

755509180ca8.name: What is the difference between the Vision framework and CoreML?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Vision framework primarily provides built-in image recognition and tracking features without requiring custom models, performing all computations on-device. CoreML is a machine learning framework that allows developers to train and integrate custom models into apps, suitable for applications needing to recognize specific objects or documents. Both support on-device computation.

755509180ca8.name: How does the new iOS 18 Vision framework API detect both human body and hand poses simultaneously?

acceptedAnswer.text: iOS 18 offers the DetectHumanBodyPoseRequest API, and by setting the detectsHands property to true, it can detect both human body and hand poses simultaneously. It returns a combined result object, making it easy to obtain key joint coordinates for the body and both hands, supporting more comprehensive motion analysis.

755509180ca8.name: How does the Vision framework recognize barcodes and text labels in images?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Vision framework provides DetectBarcodesRequest for scanning barcodes (such as EAN13, QRCode) and RecognizeTextRequest for recognizing text content in images. In iOS 18, multiple requests can be executed simultaneously, and the performAll() method streams back results, enabling real-time scanning of barcodes and text labels.

755509180ca8.name: What issues arise when testing the Vision framework on the simulator?

acceptedAnswer.text: On Mac simulators with M1 chips, the Vision framework throws errors due to the inability to create an Espresso context and cannot run properly. Testing must be done on physical iOS devices, especially for features in iOS 18 and later.

755509180ca8.name: What are the differences between the iOS 18 Vision framework API and the older VNRequest?

acceptedAnswer.text: The iOS 18 Vision framework removes all VN prefixes; for example, VNDetectBarcodesRequest becomes DetectBarcodesRequest. It replaces VNRequestCompletionHandler with async/await and uses *Request.perform() instead of VNImageRequestHandler.perform(). The API is more Swift-friendly and supports Swift Concurrency, improving code simplicity and execution performance.

309d0302877b.name: How to set up iOS Shortcuts to automatically forward SMS containing specific keywords to Line?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, open the Shortcuts app, switch to the "Automation" tab, and create a new message automation. Set the condition to messages containing specific keywords (such as "http", "notification", "verification code"), and choose to run immediately. When adding an action, search for and select Line's "Send Message" action, set the message content to the Shortcut input "Content", and specify the recipient. You need to create one automation for each keyword.

309d0302877b.name: Why can't iOS Shortcuts be set to automatically forward all SMS without any condition?

acceptedAnswer.text: The message trigger condition in iOS Shortcuts automation must include at least one condition, such as "Message Contains" or "Sender"; it cannot be set to handle all SMS unconditionally. This is a restriction by Apple for privacy and security reasons to prevent all SMS from being automatically processed indiscriminately.

309d0302877b.name: How to use iOS Shortcuts to automatically create reminders for package deliveries?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the Shortcuts app, create a new message automation and set the condition to SMS containing "has arrived" or "delivered", then choose to run immediately. Add an action to "Adjust Date" to set the reminder time (for example, 3 days after receiving the message), then use the "Add Reminder" action to include the SMS content in the reminder and set the reminder time and alert. This way, when you receive a package delivery SMS, a reminder will be automatically created.

309d0302877b.name: How to set iOS Shortcuts to automatically create reminders when receiving credit card billing emails?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, ensure you are using the Apple Mail app and have completed mailbox setup. In Shortcuts, create a new email automation with the condition set to the subject containing "credit card bill", and choose to run immediately. Since emails are fetched in batches, use the "Repeat Each" and "If" actions to filter emails with the keyword in the subject, then use the "Add Reminder" action to create reminders and set reminder time and alerts.

309d0302877b.name: Can the notification after iOS Shortcuts automation runs be turned off?

acceptedAnswer.text: A notification pops up after a Shortcut automation runs, but currently this notification cannot be turned off or dismissed. This is an iOS system design limitation, and it is not possible to hide such automation execution notifications through Shortcuts or system settings.

309d0302877b.name: If the same SMS contains multiple keywords, how many times will iOS Shortcuts automatically forward it?

acceptedAnswer.text: Because each keyword requires a separate Shortcut automation, if the same SMS contains multiple configured keywords, the Shortcut will trigger multiple times, resulting in multiple forwards. This is a limitation of the current functionality.

309d0302877b.name: Why can't the Line send message action in iOS Shortcuts automation use phone numbers from contacts?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Line send message action in Shortcuts does not support using phone numbers from contacts as the target; you can only select existing chat contacts or groups within Line. If the target does not appear, you can first send a few messages to that contact in Line to update the system, then return to Shortcuts to set it up.

7584f643c0aa.name: Why does the iOS simulator show a black screen and get stuck after multiple XCode Build & Run attempts?

acceptedAnswer.text: Starting from XCode version 14, some projects experience the simulator getting stuck on a black screen at Launching Application after multiple Build & Run cycles. This is suspected to be a bug in XCode or related to the M1 architecture. Older projects with more complex settings are more prone to this issue, and simply rebuilding and running does not resolve it; the entire simulator must be restarted.

7584f643c0aa.name: How to find the Device UUID of an iOS simulator using a command?

acceptedAnswer.text: Run the command `xcrun simctl list devices` in Terminal to list all simulator devices along with their Device UUIDs. Locate the UUID next to the simulator name you want to use, for example, the UUID for iPhone 15 Pro (iOS 17.5) might be `08C43D34–9BF0–42CF-B1B9–1E92838413CC`.

7584f643c0aa.name: How to write a Shell Script to automatically reboot a specified iOS simulator?

acceptedAnswer.text: Create a Shell Script named `auto-reboot.sh` that uses `xcrun simctl shutdown [Device UUID]` to shut down the simulator, then `xcrun simctl boot [Device UUID]` to restart it. Make sure to replace `[Device UUID]` with the target simulator's UUID, and run `chmod +x auto-reboot.sh` to grant execution permission.

7584f643c0aa.name: How to configure XCode to automatically reboot the simulator before each Build & Run?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use XCode Behaviors to set up running a Shell Script at the Completes (Stop or Rebuild) event under the Running section. Select the previously created `auto-reboot.sh` script so that the specified simulator automatically restarts after each stop or rebuild, preventing the black screen issue.

7584f643c0aa.name: Does this simulator reboot workaround slow down the Build & Run process?

acceptedAnswer.text: The time taken to reboot the simulator mostly overlaps with the Build & Run process, so it has little impact on overall speed. Since Build & Run itself requires time, this period is sufficient for the simulator to reboot without noticeably slowing down usage.

7584f643c0aa.name: Why do new projects rarely encounter the simulator black screen stuck issue?

acceptedAnswer.text: New projects have fewer and cleaner settings. The black screen stuck issue in XCode mostly occurs in older projects due to complex configurations or legacy baggage causing abnormal simulator states. New projects have simpler environments and are less likely to trigger this bug.

7584f643c0aa.name: How to ensure the Device UUID in the Shell Script is correct?

acceptedAnswer.text: You must first use `xcrun simctl list devices` to check the simulator's UUID and manually replace `[Device UUID]` in the Shell Script. If the simulator changes, remember to update the UUID in the script; otherwise, the reboot command will not work.

b7e7c0938985.name: What are the advantages of AIS 5G high-speed network eSIM plans in Bangkok, Thailand?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AIS 5G high-speed network 8-day unlimited data + call eSIM plan costs about NT$232, offering 30GB of high-speed data. After exceeding the limit, speed is reduced but the connection remains active. It includes unlimited AIS in-network calls and a 50 THB call credit. When using Tiktok, Wechat, or Instagram for text messaging, an additional 10GB of data is provided. After purchase, you receive an activation voucher for quick and easy setup, with a physical number and SMS functionality.

b7e7c0938985.name: What common scams should travelers watch out for in Bangkok, Thailand?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common scams include incorrect billing, pickpocketing during currency exchange, false claims that attractions are closed to lure tourists onto illegal boats, tuk-tuk drivers overcharging or adding extra fees, fake staff tricking customers into ordering then running off with money, rental scams requiring passport deposits, dating app fraud, and ping-pong show venues charging exorbitant fees. Crowded places especially require vigilance against pickpockets. To avoid scams, stay alert and refuse any suspicious offers immediately.

b7e7c0938985.name: What are the dress code rules for the Grand Palace and Wat Arun in Bangkok, Thailand?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Grand Palace and Wat Arun prohibit wearing shorts, sleeveless tops, ripped jeans, capri pants, short skirts, and crop tops. Wat Arun also encourages wearing traditional Thai clothing for photos. These rules are to respect local culture and religious sites, and violators may be denied entry.

b7e7c0938985.name: How much is the ticket for the King Power Mahanakhon Building Observation Deck in Bangkok? What are its features?

acceptedAnswer.text: The ticket for the King Power Mahanakhon Building Observation Deck costs about THB 1,080. The building features a unique cyberpunk architectural style. The visit includes access to the indoor observation deck on the 74th floor and the outdoor deck on the 78th floor, which has a transparent glass walkway overlooking Bangkok’s cityscape. The ticket includes a free digital composite photo; physical photos require an additional fee. It is recommended to buy tickets in advance on KKday for a cheaper price and free cancellation.

b7e7c0938985.name: What are the restroom fees and cleanliness like at Chatuchak Market in Bangkok?

acceptedAnswer.text: Restrooms around the perimeter of Chatuchak Market charge about THB 5 per use. The restrooms are clean with individual stalls and well-maintained. If you prefer not to pay, you can use the free and equally clean restrooms at the nearby Mixt Chatuchak shopping mall.

b7e7c0938985.name: What are the recommended airport transfer options in Bangkok, Thailand?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is recommended to use KKday’s private airport transfer services, covering Suvarnabhumi Airport (BKK) and Don Mueang Airport (DMK) to and from hotels in Bangkok, Pattaya, and Hua Hin. The service includes private cars for convenience and speed, avoiding the hassle of public transport transfers. There is also a BKK departure meet and greet service for fast, VIP reception, ideal for peak times.

b7e7c0938985.name: How to use Grab ride-hailing service in Bangkok, Thailand?

acceptedAnswer.text: Register for Grab and link your credit card while still in Taiwan, so you can use the ride-hailing service immediately upon arrival in Bangkok. Grab fares can be paid by card. For some routes, such as toll roads, the driver pays the toll upfront and later charges it to your credit card. Using Grab is convenient and time-saving, avoiding language barriers and the risk of negotiating fares.

f4b02ee342a4.name: What are the benefits of managing WKWebView configuration parameters using the Builder Pattern?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Builder Pattern helps encapsulate WKWebView configuration parameters, preventing direct exposure of variables to the outside and allowing step-by-step setup and reuse of configurations. This avoids arbitrary injection of non-compliant JavaScript from outside, and by leveraging Swift Access Control, it restricts parameter usage permissions, enhancing maintainability and security. For example, MyWKWebViewConfigurator sets parameters like disableZoom and overrideTitleFromWebView through methods, ultimately producing an immutable MyWKWebViewConfiguration.

f4b02ee342a4.name: How does the Chain of Responsibility Pattern solve complex navigationAction judgments in WKWebView?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Chain of Responsibility Pattern breaks down complex navigationAction judgments into multiple Handlers, each responsible for specific conditions; if one cannot handle it, it passes to the next. This avoids putting all judgments in a single method and allows flexible adjustment of processing order. For WKWebView, handlers like DeeplinkManagerNavigationActionHandler, PaymentNavigationActionHandler, and DefaultNavigationActionHandler are linked in sequence, using execute() to evaluate one by one and decide whether to allow navigation.

f4b02ee342a4.name: How is the Strategy Pattern used in WKWebView to handle JavaScript messages?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Strategy Pattern abstracts different WKScriptMessageHandler implementations into strategies, deciding which strategy to execute based on the JavaScript message's name. Each strategy implements the ScriptMessageStrategy Protocol and defines a unique identifier; WKWebView registers the corresponding handler according to the identifier. For example, PageScriptMessageStrategy handles "page" messages, UserScriptMessageStrategy handles "user" messages, making message dispatch clear and easily extensible.

f4b02ee342a4.name: Why is the Strategy Pattern in WKWebView not suitable to be replaced by Chain of Responsibility?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Strategy Pattern is suitable for scenarios where execution strategies are clearly dispatched and have no dependencies among them, whereas Chain of Responsibility passes requests down the chain based on whether they can be handled. WKWebView's ScriptMessageHandler dispatches directly based on message names without chain dependencies, so the Strategy Pattern is more appropriate. Chain of Responsibility is considered only if strategies have chain dependencies or require fallback.

f4b02ee342a4.name: How does WKWebView's Builder Pattern combine with Simple Factory to facilitate configuration for different scenarios?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Simple Factory encapsulates the creation process of MyWKWebViewConfigurator, returning default configurations based on different scenarios. For example, ForType includes default, productPage, and payment, each setting different scriptMessageStrategy, headNavigationHandler, and parameters. This allows users to quickly obtain a Builder that meets their needs by calling the factory method, simplifying the configuration process and improving reusability.

f4b02ee342a4.name: How is the NavigationActionHandler designed in WKWebView's Chain of Responsibility Pattern?

acceptedAnswer.text: NavigationActionHandler is a Protocol defining handle() to determine whether to process a navigationAction, returning true if handled, false to pass to the next. The execute() method calls each handler's handle() in sequence until one handles it or the chain end calls decisionHandler(.allow). This clearly separates different handling logic and allows flexible composition and order adjustment, such as PaymentNavigationActionHandler and DeeplinkManagerNavigationActionHandler.

f4b02ee342a4.name: How does WKWebView's Builder Pattern prevent arbitrary injection of WKUserScript from outside?

acceptedAnswer.text: By encapsulating WKUserScript injection within MyWKWebViewConfigurator using the Builder Pattern, external code can only set specific behaviors (like disableZoom) via public methods, rather than directly passing arbitrary WKUserScript. The Builder internally generates WKUserScript based on parameters and assembles them into the Configuration, preventing unsafe or non-compliant scripts from being injected externally, enhancing security and maintainability.

9e43897d99fc.name: Why does the behavior of NSAttributedString attributes Range merging change in iOS 18?

acceptedAnswer.text: Starting from iOS 18, NSAttributedString attributes use the Equatable protocol to determine if adjacent Ranges should be merged, unlike previous versions that only used Hashable. This means that if attributes with the same key implement Equatable, the system will use the == operator to decide merging, changing the merge results. For example, a custom breaklinePlaceholder attribute will be merged into a single range in iOS 18, whereas in iOS 17 it remains separate.

9e43897d99fc.name: Why does NSAttributedString attributes merging cause enumerateAttribute crashes in iOS 18?

acceptedAnswer.text: Because iOS 18 merges attributes by referencing Equatable, previously separate Ranges are merged into larger ones. When enumerateAttribute iterates and deletes ranges, the incorrect range boundaries can cause an NSRangeException crash. For instance, deleting during traversal of a custom .breaklinePlaceholder attribute can exceed range limits and cause a crash.

9e43897d99fc.name: How to avoid Hashable and Equatable conflict warnings when using NSAttributedString attributes in iOS 18?

acceptedAnswer.text: iOS 18 issues Obj-C -hash warnings on Swift structs that implement Equatable but not Hashable. It is recommended to implement both Equatable and Hashable protocols to avoid performance issues. For example, a custom BreaklinePlaceholder struct should conform to both Equatable and Hashable to comply with the underlying merging logic and prevent warnings.

9e43897d99fc.name: How should custom NSAttributedString.Key values be designed to avoid merging issues in iOS 18?

acceptedAnswer.text: Custom NSAttributedString.Key values should implement both Equatable and Hashable protocols. This allows iOS 18 to correctly determine equality when merging Ranges and avoid incorrect merges. For example, if BreaklinePlaceholder only implements Equatable, it can cause abnormal merging behavior and crashes.

9e43897d99fc.name: Why is the underlying merging of NSAttributedString attributes in iOS 18 suspected to use Set<Hashable>?

acceptedAnswer.text: Since NSAttributedString attributes are of type [NSAttributedString.Key: Any?], and the Value is not constrained to Hashable, the system attempts to cast to Hashable and uses a Set to remove duplicate attributes. In iOS 18, merging first uses Equatable to check equality, then uses Hashable for management, improving performance and ensuring correct merging.

9e43897d99fc.name: What are the specific differences in NSAttributedString attributes merging results before and after iOS 18?

acceptedAnswer.text: Before iOS 18 (i.e., iOS 17 and earlier), attributes with the same key would not merge based on Equatable even if their values were identical, resulting in multiple separate Ranges. After iOS 18, merging is based on Equatable and combines them into a single Range. For example, breaklinePlaceholder attributes that were kept as three separate Ranges in iOS 17 are merged into one large Range in iOS 18.

483af5d93297.name: What types of files can Github Pages host?

acceptedAnswer.text: Github Pages can only host static file resources, including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, font files, images, PDFs, audio, and text files. This is because it specifically provides free hosting services for static websites and does not support dynamic server-side programs.

483af5d93297.name: How to create a main Github Pages repo on Github so the URL is yourusername.github.io?

acceptedAnswer.text: Create a public repository on Github with the name "yourusername.github.io". This repo will become the main Github Pages repo, and the URL will be https://yourusername.github.io. Other repos will be accessed in the form https://yourusername.github.io/reponame.

483af5d93297.name: How to set up Namecheap DNS for a custom domain on Github Pages?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, go to the domain management in the Namecheap dashboard and set the Nameservers to Namecheap BasicDNS. Then, in Advanced DNS, add four A Records with the IPs 185.199.108.153, 185.199.109.153, 185.199.110.153, and 185.199.111.153. Next, add a CNAME Record with the Host set to your subdomain (e.g., www) and the Value set to yourusername.github.io.

483af5d93297.name: How long does it take for a custom domain setting on Github Pages to take effect?

acceptedAnswer.text: DNS settings usually take effect within five minutes, but due to DNS mechanisms, it can take up to 72 hours. If the settings are correct but it still fails, it is recommended to wait a few days and check again.

483af5d93297.name: How to upload images to a Github repo and use them on a Github Pages website?

acceptedAnswer.text: On the Github repo page, click "Add file" and select "Upload files." Drag or select the image files to upload, then click "Commit changes" when done. After uploading, you can reference these images in your HTML using relative paths.

483af5d93297.name: Where can I check the deployment status of Github Pages?

acceptedAnswer.text: On the Github repo page, click the "Actions" tab at the top to see the deployment process and status. An orange light means deployment is in progress, while a green checkmark means deployment is complete and the website is accessible.

483af5d93297.name: How to enforce HTTPS on a Github Pages website?

acceptedAnswer.text: Go to the Github repo's Settings -> Pages. After setting up a custom domain, the "Enforce HTTPS" option will appear. Check this option to force the website to use HTTPS, enhancing access security.

0095528cf875.name: How long can the GitHub Actions free tier support ZReviewTender operations?

acceptedAnswer.text: The GitHub Actions free plan provides 2,000 minutes of runtime and 500 MB of storage per month. Each ZReviewTender run consumes less than 1 minute, and by default it runs every 6 hours, totaling about 4 minutes per day and approximately 120 minutes per month, which is well below the free tier limit. Therefore, the free plan is sufficient for stable operation.

0095528cf875.name: How to create a Slack App and obtain the Bot User OAuth Token for ZReviewTender?

acceptedAnswer.text: On the Slack Apps page, click "Create New App" and choose to create from scratch. Enter the App name and select the Workspace. Then, under the OAuth & Permissions tab, add the chat:write, chat:write.public, and links:write scopes. After that, install the App to the Workspace and grant the permissions. Once installed, you will see the Slack App Bot User OAuth Token on the Install App page. Copy this token for later configuration.

0095528cf875.name: How does ZReviewTender securely store App Store and Google Play credential keys?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZReviewTender recommends storing all credential keys in the /config directory of a GitHub Private Repository, ensuring that only those with access to the private repo can retrieve them. This avoids uploading credentials to third-party services, reducing the risk of credential leaks. Additionally, since it is an open-source project, all operations are transparent and auditable.

0095528cf875.name: How to configure ZReviewTender to automatically translate app reviews that are not in the target language?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, enable the Cloud Translation API in your Google Cloud Project and use the same service account credentials. Then, add a GoogleTranslateProcessor block in apple.yml or android.yml, specifying the credentials file path, target language (e.g., zh-TW), and any languages or regions to exclude from translation. This setup allows the review bot to automatically translate reviews not in the target language into the specified language.

0095528cf875.name: Which platforms' app reviews does ZReviewTender support monitoring?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZReviewTender supports monitoring app reviews from both the iOS App Store and Android Google Play platforms. It fetches the latest user reviews through official APIs and can be configured to monitor both platforms simultaneously or individually, ensuring accurate and timely review information.

0095528cf875.name: How to adjust the review check frequency in ZReviewTender?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can adjust the schedule cron expression in the ZReviewTender.yml file within GitHub Actions. The default is set to run every 6 hours (cron: "15 */6 * * *"). You can use the crontab.guru website to generate your desired schedule, then commit the changes. GitHub Actions will execute according to the new frequency.

0095528cf875.name: How to configure ZReviewTender to forward only reviews that meet specific criteria?

acceptedAnswer.text: Add a FilterProcessor block in the processors section of apple.yml or android.yml, and set conditions such as keywordsInclude, ratingsInclude, and territoriesInclude arrays. Once enabled, ZReviewTender will forward only the reviews that match these criteria, making it easier to filter out irrelevant or unimportant comments.

6922e90ba90c.name: Why switch from Line Notify to Telegram Bot notifications?

acceptedAnswer.text: Line Notify will end its service on March 31, 2025, and afterwards only the paid Messaging API will be available. Telegram Bot is free and offers richer features, supporting message grouping, diverse content formats, and interactive bot capabilities, which better meet personal notification needs.

6922e90ba90c.name: How to get the Group Chat ID using Telegram Bot?

acceptedAnswer.text: Telegram Bot cannot directly list groups. You must call the getUpdates API and find chat data with the group name and type 'group' in the returned messages. The id inside is the Group Chat ID. If the response is an empty array, try sending a message in the group first and then retry.

6922e90ba90c.name: How to send messages with Telegram Bot using Google Apps Script?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use Google Apps Script to call Telegram Bot's sendMessage API, POSTing JSON that includes chat_id, text, and parse_mode. The sample code uses URLFetchApp.fetch to send the request and can set different chat_id values for various groups to categorize notifications.

6922e90ba90c.name: How to set different notification sounds or mute for groups in Telegram Bot?

acceptedAnswer.text: Telegram allows users to set notification sounds or mute settings for different groups, which is more flexible than Line Notify. Users can adjust group notification settings as needed to enhance the experience, a feature not available in Line Notify.

6922e90ba90c.name: How to interact with users and execute commands using Telegram Bot?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can implement a doPost function in Google Apps Script to handle command messages sent to the Telegram Bot. For example, when receiving the /update command, execute a specific function and reply with a message. Register a Webhook with the Telegram API to enable the Bot to respond to user commands in real time.

6922e90ba90c.name: What is Telegram Bot's BotFather and how to use it to create a Bot?

acceptedAnswer.text: BotFather is the official Telegram bot through which you can create a new Bot by sending the /newbot command inside the Telegram App. You set the Bot's name and username and receive the BOT API Token, which is the necessary credential to connect to the Telegram Bot API.

6922e90ba90c.name: How to deploy Google Apps Script so Telegram Bot can receive Webhook?

acceptedAnswer.text: Create a project in the Google Apps Script editor and implement the doPost function. Then deploy it as a 'Web App' with access set to 'Anyone'. Obtain the Webhook URL and register it with the Telegram API using setWebhook to complete the Webhook deployment.

1e85b8df2348.name: How to generate query parameters for Google Analytics data using GA4 Query Explorer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Using GA4 Query Explorer, first select your property ID, then set the report's start and end dates (you can use YYYY-MM-DD or magic variables like 7daysAgo). Next, choose the metrics and dimensions you want to query, then set metric aggregations (such as total) and limit (e.g., 10 records). After completing these steps, click "MAKE REQUEST" to get the query parameters. This quickly generates query settings suitable for use with Google Apps Script.

1e85b8df2348.name: How to connect Google Apps Script with GA4 Data API to fetch website data?

acceptedAnswer.text: After adding the Google Analytics Data API service in Google Apps Script, write code to create metrics, dimensions, and dateRange, assemble a RunReportRequest, and call AnalyticsData.Properties.runReport. Using the fetchScreenPageViews example, set the propertyId, date range, and limit to successfully retrieve GA4 report data. The first execution requires authorization, ensuring the account has the corresponding GA access permissions.

1e85b8df2348.name: How to automatically send GA4 data notifications via Telegram Bot?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, create a Telegram Bot and obtain the Bot Token and target Channel Chat ID. Then, write a function in Google Apps Script to combine GA4 query results into a message string, and use UrlFetchApp to call Telegram API's sendMessage interface to send the message. The script handles the response status, and if it encounters rate limits (429), it will automatically delay and retry. This allows daily automatic pushing of website data to the specified Telegram channel.

1e85b8df2348.name: How to set up scheduled triggers in Google Apps Script to automatically run the GA4 data notification bot?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the Google Apps Script editor, click "Triggers" in the left menu. When adding a new trigger, select the function to execute (e.g., execute), the deployment (top), the event source (time-driven), then choose the trigger frequency and execution time. Finally, save it. This setup will automatically run the script at the specified time daily, completing GA4 data queries and sending notifications via the Telegram Bot.

1e85b8df2348.name: Why use Google Apps Script for automatic GA4 data notifications?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Apps Script is a free and easy-to-use cloud scripting platform that can connect to the GA4 Data API to automatically fetch website data and combine with Telegram Bot to send notifications, saving manual report checking time. For example, with 10 repeated tasks per month, each taking 30 minutes, the team can save thousands of hours of work, improving efficiency and output.

1e85b8df2348.name: How to verify that the GA4 data fetched by Google Apps Script is accurate?

acceptedAnswer.text: After fetching GA4 data, compare the results with the same report data on the GA4 web interface to confirm that metrics and dimensions match. The article demonstrates using a date range from 7 days ago to yesterday, retrieving the Top 10 page views, and the results match the GA4 backend, proving the code's data fetching and parsing are correct.

1e85b8df2348.name: How to extend Google Apps Script GA4 query code to query multiple metrics and dimensions?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the code, you can declare multiple metric and dimension objects, then place them into the request.metrics and request.dimensions arrays. For example, query both active1DayUsers and screenPageViews metrics, and pageTitle and firstUserSourceMedium dimensions simultaneously. This way, you can obtain multiple data sets at once, making it easier to combine into a more complete report.

70aeddb1fd9b.name: What are the free limits of GitHub Pages?

acceptedAnswer.text: GitHub Pages free accounts can only host static files. The recommended site size should not exceed 1 GB, the maximum deployment time is 10 minutes, with up to 10 deployments per hour, and a monthly bandwidth limit of about 100 GB. Frequent requests may receive HTTP 429 responses. These limits ensure stable and free service usage.

70aeddb1fd9b.name: How does linkyee support dynamic variable updates for link page content?

acceptedAnswer.text: linkyee has built-in plugins that automatically fetch Medium follower counts and GitHub Repo star counts, and inject them into the page as variables. The project redeploys automatically once a day to refresh these dynamic data, ensuring the page information is up to date.

70aeddb1fd9b.name: How to quickly deploy your own link page using linkyee?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, select "Use this template" in the linkyee template library to create a new Repo, check to include all branches, then adjust Actions permissions in Settings, set the Pages branch to gh-pages, wait for GitHub Actions to complete deployment, and finally visit the GitHub Pages URL to confirm success.

70aeddb1fd9b.name: How does the GitHub Pages Repo name affect the website URL?

acceptedAnswer.text: If the Repo name is your-username.github.io, the website URL will be https://your-username.github.io; if it has another name, the URL will be https://your-username.github.io/Repo-Name. The Repo name determines the access path.

70aeddb1fd9b.name: How can linkyee's page styles be customized?

acceptedAnswer.text: linkyee allows direct modification of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript source files. The default theme is located in the ./themes/default folder. To create a new theme, add a new folder and update the theme name in config.yml. You can even use ChatGPT to help generate custom styles.

70aeddb1fd9b.name: How to set up linkyee to use a custom domain?

acceptedAnswer.text: linkyee supports custom GitHub Pages domains. You can refer to the author's previous tutorial article on setting up custom domains for GitHub Pages. After purchasing and binding the domain, set the custom domain in the Repo Settings Pages section to use it.

70aeddb1fd9b.name: Why do you need to wait for GitHub Actions to complete deployment after modifying the linkyee configuration file?

acceptedAnswer.text: Every time you modify config.yml or other files, GitHub Actions automatically runs the build and deployment process to publish the updates to GitHub Pages. You must wait for the process to complete for the page changes to take effect on the public URL.

aacd5f5cacd1.name: How to reasonably plan a 7-day self-guided trip to the San'in region of Japan?

acceptedAnswer.text: For a 7-day self-guided trip, it is recommended to enter and exit through Okayama, first visiting Izumo Taisha, Matsue Castle, and the sunset at Lake Shinji in Shimane. Then proceed to Tottori to see the White Rabbit Shrine and Tottori Sand Dunes, and finally head to Himeji to admire Himeji Castle, with shopping stops in Osaka and Kobe. The travel distance covers about 1000 kilometers, so purchasing the Kansai & San'in Area Rail Pass (JR Pass) is advised for convenient JR train rides. The itinerary can include buses, sightseeing boats, and other transport modes to ensure smooth travel.

aacd5f5cacd1.name: How to best use the Kansai & San'in Area JR Pass in Japan?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Kansai & San'in Area Rail Pass (JR Pass) is ideal for entering and exiting via Okayama Airport and covers long-distance travel in Shimane, Tottori, Himeji, Osaka, Kobe, and other areas. This pass includes a 3-day ticket that allows free rides on the Lake Shinji sightseeing boat, Matsue Horikawa sightseeing boat, and transportation to attractions like the Adachi Museum of Art, saving at least 8,390 yen. You must exchange for a physical ticket before use and reserve seat reservations in advance, especially for the all-reserved-seat JR YAKUMO train.

aacd5f5cacd1.name: How to manually activate a Japanese eSIM on an iPhone?

acceptedAnswer.text: To manually activate a Japanese eSIM on an iPhone, go to Settings → Cellular → Add eSIM → Use Cellular QR Code, then scan the provided QR code to activate. If scanning is not possible, choose 'Enter Details Manually' and fill in the SM-DP+ address and activation code to complete the setup. Activation may take a few minutes, and once successful, the cellular service will show as active with signal bars.

aacd5f5cacd1.name: What is the most convenient way to pay for buses in the Tottori area of Japan?

acceptedAnswer.text: Tottori buses do not accept electronic payments and only accept cash. It is recommended to prepare small change in advance or exchange large bills with the driver. The fare is about 610 yen; passengers take a numbered ticket when boarding and pay the corresponding fare when alighting based on the ticket number. If you plan to take multiple buses, consider purchasing a sightseeing one-day pass for better value and convenience.

aacd5f5cacd1.name: How is the worship method at Izumo Taisha different from typical shrines in Japan?

acceptedAnswer.text: The worship method at Izumo Taisha involves bowing twice, clapping four times, and then bowing once more, which differs from the usual two claps at typical shrines. This is because Izumo Taisha enshrines Ōkuninushi no Mikoto and hosts the Kamiari Festival every November, when deities from all over Japan gather here, making the worship ritual particularly special.

aacd5f5cacd1.name: What is the recommended way to view the sunset at Lake Shinji in Japan?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is recommended to take the JR train to Nogi Station and walk to the Lake Shinji sunset viewing spot. Lake Shinji is Japan's seventh-largest lake and is famed for its beautiful sunsets. Sunset occurs around 17:05, so arriving early to wait is advised. Nearby are a stair platform and the Shimane Art Museum, with the stair platform offering a better viewing angle. If time permits, you can also take a sightseeing boat on Lake Shinji to enjoy the sunset; the 3-day JR Pass allows free rides on this boat, saving about 1,800 yen.

aacd5f5cacd1.name: Are there recommended photo spots near Himeji Castle in Japan?

acceptedAnswer.text: A recommended photo spot is inside Himeji City Zoo, located behind the Ferris wheel on an open space with a good view to capture the entire Himeji Castle. This spot requires walking in the opposite direction from the castle entrance and is somewhat hidden but offers beautiful scenery. It is advised to avoid taking photos too close to the castle, as being too near makes framing difficult.

aacd5f5cacd1.name: Is Kobe Animal Kingdom suitable for family visits with children?

acceptedAnswer.text: Kobe Animal Kingdom is suitable for family visits. The park is semi-indoor and semi-outdoor with well-planned pathways and offers close encounters with various birds and animals. Admission is about 2,200 yen. The animal conditions and environment are better than those at Himeji City Zoo. Outside food feeding is prohibited, and visitors must wash hands after touching animals like capybaras. Overall, it provides a comfortable experience and is ideal for spending half a day.

a8925ad9ed01.name: How to use a custom Namespace container in Swift to avoid naming conflicts in native type extension methods?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can use a generic container ExtensionContainer combined with the protocols ExtensionCompatibleObject or ExtensionCompatible. By encapsulating extension methods through a computed property, you create a dedicated Namespace. This way, extension methods on native types won’t be mixed directly, reducing naming conflicts. For example, after extending UIColor, you use color.zhg.toHexString() instead of calling toHexString() directly.

a8925ad9ed01.name: Why does directly extending UIColor cause project extension method confusion and naming conflicts?

acceptedAnswer.text: Because methods directly extending UIColor get mixed with native APIs, making it impossible to distinguish which are custom extensions. Also, if different modules or packages define the same method name like getCMYK(), it causes compilation errors. For example, if two packages both extend UIColor and define getCMYK(), it will report an Invalid redeclaration error.

a8925ad9ed01.name: How to write a Swift extension example for UIColor to convert to HEX string?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use extension UIColor to implement the toHexString(includeAlpha: Bool = false) method. First, get the CGFloat values of red, green, blue, and alpha, then format them into #RRGGBB or #RRGGBBAA using String(format:). Return nil if it fails. Example: UIColor.blue.toHexString() returns "#0000FF".

a8925ad9ed01.name: How to extend the URL class in Swift to get a dictionary of query parameters?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, make URL conform to ExtensionCompatible. Then define a queryParameters property inside ExtensionContainer where Base == URL, using URLComponents to parse the URL and convert queryItems into a dictionary. Usage: url.zhg.queryParameters returns a dictionary like ["a": "b", "c": "d"].

a8925ad9ed01.name: How to combine the Builder Pattern in Swift to manipulate URLs and encapsulate it with a custom Namespace?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, define a URLBuilder class containing URLComponents, providing methods to set queryParameters, scheme, etc., returning self, and finally build() to produce a URL. Make URL conform to ExtensionCompatible, then extend ExtensionContainer for URL to add a builder() method returning URLBuilder. Usage: url.zhg.builder().setQueryParameters(...).setScheme(...).build().

a8925ad9ed01.name: What is the difference between ExtensionCompatibleObject and ExtensionCompatible protocols in Swift?

acceptedAnswer.text: ExtensionCompatibleObject is suitable for classes (reference types), such as Foundation’s NSXXX classes; ExtensionCompatible is for structs (value types). Both define a computed property zhg returning a generic container ExtensionContainer, allowing different types to use a custom Namespace to encapsulate extension methods.

a8925ad9ed01.name: How to declare and use ExtensionContainer for custom Namespace extensions in Swift?

acceptedAnswer.text: Declare a generic struct ExtensionContainer<Base> holding a base instance. Extend the protocols ExtensionCompatibleObject or ExtensionCompatible to add a computed property zhg returning ExtensionContainer<Self>. When extending, write custom methods inside ExtensionContainer where Base == a specific type. Usage: call instance.zhg.method, for example color.zhg.toHexString().

4cb4437818f2.name: Why use Google Apps Script Web App to create a GUI form for Github Action CI/CD?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Apps Script Web App allows quick deployment of free web GUIs integrated with Google services, solving the limitations of Github Action's native forms which lack dynamic capabilities and data validation. This enables users without project permissions or engineering backgrounds to easily operate CI/CD, and access can be restricted to accounts within the organization to ensure security.

4cb4437818f2.name: What are the limitations of Github Action's native Workflow Dispatch form?

acceptedAnswer.text: Github Action's native Workflow Dispatch form only provides simple static inputs, cannot support dynamic form content or data validation, and requires users to have project permissions to use it, making it unfriendly for users without engineering backgrounds or permissions.

4cb4437818f2.name: Why is Slack App not suitable to implement CI/CD forms using Google Apps Script?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Apps Script has long cold start times, making it unable to respond within Slack's strict 3-second API response window. It also cannot access HTTP Headers, preventing it from passing Slack's official security verification. Additionally, its single-threaded limitation causes timeouts when integrating with other services. These factors make implementing Slack Apps with GAS unstable.

4cb4437818f2.name: How does Google Apps Script Web App restrict access to only users within the organization?

acceptedAnswer.text: When deploying the Web App, set "Who has access" to "Anyone within the same organization" and set "Execute as" to "User accessing the web app". This automatically restricts access to users logged in with Google accounts within the organization and runs the script as that user.

4cb4437818f2.name: How to use Google Apps Script to connect to Github API to get the Pull Request branch list?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use Google Apps Script's UrlFetchApp to call the Github RESTful API, passing a Personal Access Token for authorization. A GET request to the /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls endpoint retrieves the PR list. The example in the article uses the swiftlang/swift repository.

4cb4437818f2.name: How does Google Apps Script Web App trigger Github Action after form submission?

acceptedAnswer.text: After the form is submitted, GAS sends a POST request to Github API's /actions/workflows/{workflow_id}/dispatches endpoint, including the branch name and version number as inputs, triggering the specified Github Action Workflow Dispatch event to start the CI/CD process.

4cb4437818f2.name: Why do changes in Google Apps Script code require redeployment to take effect?

acceptedAnswer.text: GAS Web App deployment uses a version control mechanism. After code changes, a new version must be created and redeployed for the Web App to use the latest code. Without redeployment, the app still serves the old version, so changes won't take effect.

4cb4437818f2.name: How to query Slack User ID using a user's Google email and send notifications?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use a Slack Bot Token to call Slack API's users.lookupByEmail endpoint to find the Slack User ID corresponding to the email, then use the chat.postMessage API to send a message to that user. This allows real-time notification of CI/CD execution status on Slack.

5bb7d3a4954f.name: How to use the ZMediumToMarkdown tool to automatically back up Medium articles as Markdown?

acceptedAnswer.text: ZMediumToMarkdown is a tool I developed that can automatically download Medium articles and convert them into Markdown files including images and code. It parses the article format and generates Markdown suitable for Jekyll, making it easy to deploy as a static website. For example, I used it to back up over 100 Medium articles to ensure the content is not lost.

5bb7d3a4954f.name: Why use Jekyll (Chirpy Theme) to deploy a Medium backup website?

acceptedAnswer.text: Jekyll is a powerful Ruby static site generator, and the Chirpy Theme is feature-rich with an excellent user experience, including features like sticky posts, categories, tags, and comments. I use it together with the Markdown generated by ZMediumToMarkdown and deploy it on Github Pages, allowing me to quickly and freely create a static mirror site of Medium articles.

5bb7d3a4954f.name: What issues were encountered deploying Jekyll (Chirpy Theme) v7.x on Github Pages?

acceptedAnswer.text: Version 7.x removed the original deploy.sh script and switched to deploying via Github Actions' Upload Pages Artifact. However, because the site content exceeded the 1GB limit, the upload failed. I had to revert to using the original deploy.sh script and comment out the new deployment process to complete the deployment.

5bb7d3a4954f.name: How to use Jekyll Plugin's Hook feature to display Medium follower count and last page update time?

acceptedAnswer.text: I wrote a Ruby plugin that uses Jekyll's site:pre_render hook to scrape the Medium page and get the follower count, then dynamically insert it into the site's profile section. The footer also includes a string showing the last update time. This way, every time Github Actions automatically deploys, these dynamic contents are updated.

5bb7d3a4954f.name: How to customize article page content in Jekyll (Chirpy Theme)?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can copy the theme's post.html file into your own _layouts directory and directly modify the HTML content, such as adding custom text or sections. You can also place shared fragments in the _includes directory and include them with {% include %}. This method is very flexible but may cause conflicts that require adjustments when upgrading.

5bb7d3a4954f.name: Why use htmlproofer to test the site during Github Pages deployment?

acceptedAnswer.text: htmlproofer is used to check if website links are valid and if HTML tags are complete, ensuring site quality after deployment. Because some tags lack alt attributes or have HTTPS issues, I added --no-enforce-https and --ignore-empty-alt parameters to ignore these errors so the test can pass and deployment can continue.

5bb7d3a4954f.name: What are the features of the medium-to-jekyll-starter template repo?

acceptedAnswer.text: This is my Github Template Repo integrating the latest Jekyll (Chirpy Theme) v7.x with the ZMediumToMarkdown tool. It allows you to quickly set up a backup website that automatically downloads Medium articles, converts them to Markdown, and continuously deploys to Github Pages. Once configured, it can automatically back up your content for free permanently.

fd719053b376.name: Why did early virtual folders in Xcode cause project directory structure confusion?

acceptedAnswer.text: Early virtual folders in Xcode were just file references inside the project file, with the actual files flattened in the project root directory. This made the directory structure invisible to tools like Git, resulting in flattened files and difficult management. It complicated code reviews and third-party tool integration, and was unfavorable for modular development.

fd719053b376.name: Why can't XCodeGen and Tuist use virtual folders?

acceptedAnswer.text: When generating Xcode project files, XCodeGen and Tuist must create directory settings based on the actual physical directory structure of files. Virtual folders have no corresponding physical directories, so they cannot be correctly mapped, making it impossible to manage projects with these modern tools.

fd719053b376.name: Why can't Xcode 16's "Convert to folder" feature automatically convert virtual folders to physical folders?

acceptedAnswer.text: Xcode 16's "Convert to folder" only upgrades the folder setting to the new PBXFileSystemSynchronizedRootGroup type; it does not automatically create physical folders or move files. If the corresponding physical folder does not exist, it will report a "Missing Associated Folder" error. You must manually create the correct directory structure first.

fd719053b376.name: How do I use the XCFolder tool to convert virtual folders into physical folders?

acceptedAnswer.text: To use XCFolder, first clone the project, then run the command swift run XCFolder with your .xcodeproj file and the Configuration.yaml settings file. The tool will automatically parse the project file, move files to the correct physical directories, and update the .xcodeproj directory settings. Before running, ensure there are no uncommitted changes in Git to preserve the full file history.

fd719053b376.name: What else should I pay attention to after using the XCFolder tool?

acceptedAnswer.text: After execution, check if any files are missing from the project. If many files are missing, adjust the ignorePaths or ignoreFileTypes in Configuration.yaml. Also verify that the Build Setting path configurations are correct, and it is recommended to perform a Clean & Build test. If you do not want to maintain the existing .xcodeproj, you can also regenerate the project file directly using XCodeGen or Tuist.

fd719053b376.name: What are the differences between XCodeGen and Tuist in project management?

acceptedAnswer.text: XCodeGen uses YAML to define projects, has a low entry barrier, and is suitable for simple projects, but has weaker modularization and dependency management. Tuist uses a Swift DSL, offers more powerful features with built-in modularization and dependency management, but has a higher learning curve. Both generate consistent .xcodeproj files, avoid conflicts, and are suitable for team collaboration.

fd719053b376.name: Why should the .xcodeproj be added to .gitignore instead of managing it directly?

acceptedAnswer.text: The .xcodeproj file is dynamically generated by XCodeGen or Tuist based on configuration files. Managing it directly can cause conflicts during multi-developer collaboration. Adding it to .gitignore allows team members to regenerate the project file when switching branches, avoiding merge conflicts, and ensures project changes are reflected in the reviewable configuration files.

71400d408dc8.name: How to connect Google Apps Script with Firebase App Distribution API that has no built-in service?

acceptedAnswer.text: Connecting Google Apps Script to Firebase App Distribution API requires enabling advanced services and associating a GCP project, because this API does not have a built-in service. The setup includes enabling advanced services, activating the API in the GCP project, and adding the necessary oauthScopes in appsscript.json. The connection code can directly use ScriptApp.getOAuthToken() to obtain the token, simplifying the authentication process.

71400d408dc8.name: Why set the GCP project number in a Google Apps Script project?

acceptedAnswer.text: Setting the GCP project number allows Google Apps Script to use the permissions of Google APIs enabled in that project. Google API permissions are controlled by the GCP project; without setting it or with insufficient permissions, 401 or 403 authentication errors will occur. For example, with Firebase App Distribution API, you must associate a GCP project where this API is enabled and ensure the account has permission.

71400d408dc8.name: How to configure oauthScopes in appsscript.json for Google Apps Script to call external Google APIs?

acceptedAnswer.text: You must add "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/script.external_request" and "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform" to the oauthScopes in appsscript.json to authorize the script to call external Google APIs. Missing these authorizations will cause insufficient permission errors, leading to API request failures.

71400d408dc8.name: How to handle 403 permission errors when connecting Google Apps Script to Firebase App Distribution API?

acceptedAnswer.text: A 403 error usually indicates insufficient authorization scopes. Please check if appsscript.json includes the necessary oauthScopes, confirm that the Firebase App Distribution API is enabled in the GCP project, and ensure the current account has permission to use this API. Missing these settings will cause the request to be denied.

71400d408dc8.name: What is the simplest code to get the Firebase App Distribution beta download link using Google Apps Script?

acceptedAnswer.text: Once the GCP project is set up and the API is enabled, you can write less than 10 lines of code using UrlFetchApp to call the Firebase App Distribution API and use ScriptApp.getOAuthToken() to get the OAuth token. The code parses the returned releases object and extracts the testingUri, which is the download link.

71400d408dc8.name: Why don't you need to manage Service Accounts and token exchanges yourself when connecting Google Apps Script to Google APIs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Because Google Apps Script provides ScriptApp.getOAuthToken() to directly obtain the current user's OAuth token, eliminating the complicated process of creating Service Accounts and exchanging tokens yourself. This makes connecting to Google APIs simpler and faster, especially for APIs like Firebase App Distribution that have no built-in service.

71400d408dc8.name: Will the API be automatically enabled after enabling advanced services in Google Apps Script?

acceptedAnswer.text: If your script project uses the default GCP project created on or after April 8, 2019, enabling advanced services and saving will automatically enable the API. For standard GCP projects or older default projects, you need to manually enable the corresponding API in the GCP project and ensure you have edit permissions.

6fd11e9704f2.name: Why did it take seven years on Medium to reach the milestone of 1,000 followers?

acceptedAnswer.text: The author switched from web to app development in 2018, relying on self-study and continuous writing to accumulate experience. It took over 800 hours and 112 articles to reach the milestone. During the growth process, there were concerns about sharing misconceptions and content quality, but persistent writing enriched the content and gradually attracted followers.

6fd11e9704f2.name: What are the main reasons for the slowdown in follower growth on Medium?

acceptedAnswer.text: The recent slowdown in follower growth is mainly due to industry cooling, the impact of AI on digital content creation, and changes in Medium's SEO policies. These factors have reduced the number of new followers. As a result, the author has adopted a more relaxed attitude but continues to share experiences and content.

6fd11e9704f2.name: Why did the author start writing tutorial articles?

acceptedAnswer.text: When the author was self-learning app development, many online articles from predecessors were helpful. This inspired the author to practice the concept of "teaching and learning from each other" through writing, aiming to create a positive cycle that helps developers facing similar problems while deepening their own learning and skills.

6fd11e9704f2.name: How did the author overcome initial doubts about sharing content?

acceptedAnswer.text: At first, the author worried about incomplete content, being criticized, and not helping readers. Later, the mindset shifted to viewing writing as planting seeds that might help other developers in the future. Continuous writing improved skills, enriched content, and increased opportunities to be noticed.

6fd11e9704f2.name: What topics does the author share on Medium?

acceptedAnswer.text: Besides iOS development-related content, the author also shares daily life, travelogues, unboxing reviews, and personal interests related to automation (RPA). This diversity makes the content varied and relatable, attracting different reader groups.

6fd11e9704f2.name: What does reaching 1,000 followers mean to the author?

acceptedAnswer.text: Reaching 1,000 followers is a milestone representing seven years of the author's hard work, continuous sharing, and persistence. The author sees this as a stage goal and will continue to share knowledge and experiences, regardless of whether they continue writing about iOS-related topics.

7508328d8b8d.name: Why does the app crash or behave abnormally only in Release mode after upgrading XCode, while Debug mode works fine?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the Build Settings for Release mode, the Optimization Level is usually set to Fastest, Smallest [-Os], which enables compiler optimizations that cause the program behavior to differ from Debug mode (Optimization Level = None [-O0]). These optimizations can sometimes introduce compiler bugs, leading to logic errors or crashes. For example, the issue mentioned in the article where invitedTimes becomes a very large negative number is caused by such optimization errors.

7508328d8b8d.name: How to configure XCode to simulate Release optimization level in Debug mode to troubleshoot issues?

acceptedAnswer.text: In XCode, select the App Target, go to Build Settings, search for Optimization Level, and change the Debug environment's Optimization Level to match that of Release (e.g., Fastest, Smallest [-Os]). Then Build & Run. This allows you to locally simulate Release optimization behavior, making it easier to use breakpoints to locate errors that only occur in Release mode.

7508328d8b8d.name: How to handle optimization issues that only appear in Release mode?

acceptedAnswer.text: These issues are mostly caused by bugs in XCode compiler optimizations, which developers usually cannot fix directly. The solution is to add workarounds in the code to avoid triggering the error and wait for a new XCode version to be released to test if the issue is resolved. The article advises against simply setting the Release Optimization Level to None, as this may introduce more problems.

7508328d8b8d.name: Why is it not recommended to set the Release mode Optimization Level to None directly?

acceptedAnswer.text: Setting the Release Optimization Level to None disables compiler optimizations. While this might avoid certain optimization bugs, it significantly reduces performance and may cause other unknown issues. The article recommends using workarounds to solve the problem and waiting for official fixes rather than turning off optimization levels directly.

7508328d8b8d.name: What specific problems can the XCode optimization level Fastest, Smallest [-Os] cause?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Fastest, Smallest [-Os] optimization level can cause abnormal variable states. For example, in the article, invitedTimes becomes a very large negative number after being incremented in a defer block, causing users to be repeatedly invited for reviews. It can also cause Obj-C WebView variables to become null, leading to crashes. These are all logic errors or memory issues caused by optimization.

7508328d8b8d.name: Why not directly package and test on Testflight when issues appear in Release mode?

acceptedAnswer.text: Packaging and testing directly on Testflight is time-consuming and makes it difficult to locate issues in real-time, especially since breakpoints cannot be used for debugging. The article suggests first changing the Debug mode Optimization Level to match Release locally and using Build & Run to quickly reproduce the problem and find the root cause with breakpoints, saving testing and troubleshooting time.

7508328d8b8d.name: Why must you test the Optimization Level before the first packaging and release after upgrading XCode?

acceptedAnswer.text: Because the new version of XCode may introduce optimization strategies that cause crashes or logic errors only in Release mode, testing the Optimization Level can detect these issues early and prevent users from encountering ghost errors. The article recommends changing Debug optimization to match Release after upgrading to ensure issues can be reproduced and fixed locally before packaging and releasing.

c008a9e8ceca.name: Why use CI/CD to prevent frequent feature breakage issues in the development team?

acceptedAnswer.text: CI/CD ensures that all code changes pass through a unified build and test process via automated testing and continuous integration, preventing code that does not meet standards or fails tests from being merged into the main branch. This greatly reduces the risk of feature breakage and improves product stability. For example, after implementing CI, all adjustments must pass automated tests before merging, reducing about 720 minutes per month of fixing and communication time.

c008a9e8ceca.name: How will GitHub Actions charge for Self-hosted Runner usage starting in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: Starting March 1, 2026, GitHub Actions will charge a maintenance fee of $0.002 per minute for using Self-hosted Runners, regardless of the operating system. For example, 500 minutes would cost about $1, which remains cost-effective for most users. Public repositories will still have free usage quotas. This means teams using self-hosted machines for CI/CD will incur additional service fees, but these are still lower than the costs for cloud-hosted runners.

c008a9e8ceca.name: What are the differences between GitHub Actions and Jenkins in setting up App CI/CD?

acceptedAnswer.text: GitHub Actions defines workflows using YAML files and integrates tightly with GitHub repositories, making it easy to configure and maintain with a low barrier, suitable for app developers without dedicated DevOps teams. Jenkins is more powerful but complex to set up and maintain, requiring additional management of APIs/WebHooks and having higher security risks. In a survey of about 30 iOS developers, 10 used GitHub Actions and only 2 used Jenkins, showing GitHub Actions is more popular and user-friendly.

c008a9e8ceca.name: How does implementing CI/CD help the team save time packaging test version apps?

acceptedAnswer.text: After implementing CD, the packaging process is automated, so engineers don’t need to manually package or interrupt development work, reducing back-and-forth communication and operational errors. In this case, using GitHub Actions Self-hosted Runner to automate packaging and integrate with Firebase App Distribution API allows QA and PM to directly obtain test version download links, saving about 290 minutes per month on packaging and error correction time.

c008a9e8ceca.name: Why choose Google Apps Script Web App as a packaging platform for cross-functional teams?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Apps Script Web App provides a simple web interface that allows non-engineers to operate the CI/CD packaging process and integrates with Google Workspace permissions to ensure security. It is free, requires no server maintenance, can be used on both mobile and desktop, and AI tools like ChatGPT support its development and maintenance. Compared to GitHub Actions’ built-in form feature, which is too basic, GAS Web App is better suited for cross-team collaboration.

c008a9e8ceca.name: Is it cost-effective to run CI/CD on your own Mac Mini using GitHub Actions Self-hosted Runner?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is very cost-effective. Purchasing a Mac Mini 10-core (M4) for $600 as a Self-hosted Runner, after deducting the $0.002 per minute maintenance fee starting in 2026, can pay off within a year. Compared to cloud-hosted runners costing about $0.062 per minute, using a self-hosted machine allows unlimited task execution with better performance, and you can even use old MacBook Pros being replaced at zero cost.

c008a9e8ceca.name: How does CI/CD help the team reduce context-switching costs?

acceptedAnswer.text: CI/CD automates testing and deployment processes, preventing engineers from having to manually package or fix unexpected errors, reducing the frequency of development interruptions. Context-switching costs about 30 minutes, and frequent interruptions lower productivity. After implementing CI/CD, the team can focus on continuous development, reducing communication and correction time, thereby improving overall efficiency and product quality.

404bd5c70040.name: Which directory should the GitHub Actions Workflow YAML files be placed in?

acceptedAnswer.text: GitHub Actions Workflow YAML files must be placed under the `.github/workflows/` directory in the GitHub repository. This is the default file structure for GitHub Actions, and all automation workflows are read from here. In the examples, all Workflow files are located in this path.

404bd5c70040.name: How to use Secrets in GitHub Actions to store sensitive information?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can add Secrets in the GitHub repository under Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → Secrets, such as Slack Bot Token or Apple Store Connect API Key. The content of Secrets is automatically hidden and cannot be viewed in the Action Log; they can only be overwritten. Currently, only plain text is supported; binary files need to be Base64 encoded first. This allows sensitive information to be securely used within Workflows.

404bd5c70040.name: How to specify using a Self-hosted Runner for a Job in GitHub Actions?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the Workflow Job, use the `runs-on` attribute to specify the label of the Self-hosted Runner, for example `runs-on: [self-hosted-zhgchgli]`. This will dispatch the Job to a self-hosted Runner with the matching label. Labels are combined with AND logic, so the Runner must have all specified labels to be selected.

404bd5c70040.name: Is it worth using Self-hosted Runner compared to GitHub Hosted Runner?

acceptedAnswer.text: A Self-hosted Runner is a machine you set up yourself, free to use without limits except for the one-time hardware cost. For example, a 32GB RAM M4 Mini costs about 40,000 TWD. Using GitHub Hosted Runner costs about 500 USD per month, so it pays off in about three months. It is suitable for teams running large volumes of CI/CD tasks long-term. GitHub Hosted Runner is better for small-scale, short-term, or testing purposes.

404bd5c70040.name: How do Job, Step, and Runner work together in a GitHub Actions Workflow?

acceptedAnswer.text: A Workflow defines multiple Jobs, which run concurrently. Each Job contains multiple Steps executed sequentially. Runners listen for and execute Jobs, dispatching tasks based on the Runner Label specified by the Job. Multiple Runners can handle multiple Jobs simultaneously. Steps run shell scripts or Actions in order; if a Step fails, the Job is interrupted.

404bd5c70040.name: How to implement automatic labeling of Pull Request file change size in GitHub Actions?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can use a shell script combined with the `gh` CLI to get the number of changed files in a PR when the Workflow triggers on PR events. Based on the count range, label the PR with XS, S, M, L, XL, XXL, etc. The example first removes old Size Labels, then adds the corresponding label. Alternatively, you can use an existing JavaScript Action like pascalgn/size-label-action@v0.5.5 to quickly achieve this feature.

404bd5c70040.name: How to call a Reuse Workflow across repositories in GitHub Actions Workflow?

acceptedAnswer.text: In the main repository's Workflow, use `uses: owner/repo/.github/workflows/file.yml@branch` to specify a Workflow from another repository, and pass parameters with `with` and necessary tokens with `secrets`. The called repository must be configured to allow access from other repositories in the same organization. This allows shared CI/CD workflows to be split into separate repositories for cross-repo sharing.

404bd5c70040.name: What is the difference between `${{ }}` expressions and Shell environment variables in GitHub Actions?

acceptedAnswer.text: `${{ }}` is a GitHub Actions Context expression that is replaced by GitHub Actions with the corresponding value during YAML parsing, not a Shell variable. Shell environment variables are injected by the Runner at runtime, such as `$VAR` or `${VAR}`. Context expressions are typically used to inject dynamic values in configuration files, while Shell variables are used during script execution. They serve different purposes and operate at different times.

4b001d2e8440.name: How to set up a CI process for iOS App unit testing using GitHub Actions?

acceptedAnswer.text: The CI process for an iOS App can use GitHub Actions to listen for Pull Request events, running workflows on a macOS runner. It first checks out the project, sets the specified Xcode and Ruby versions, then uses Fastlane to run the designated test lane (e.g., run_unit_tests). The process caches dependencies to speed up installation and parses test reports to provide feedback. This way, tests automatically run on every PR to ensure quality.

4b001d2e8440.name: What settings should be noted when packaging an iOS App with GitHub Actions and deploying to Firebase App Distribution?

acceptedAnswer.text: The packaging and deployment workflow needs to include steps to install certificates, converting Apple development certificates and Provisioning Profiles to Base64 and storing them as Secrets, then decoding and installing them during execution. A Firebase CLI Token should also be prepared and set as a Secret. Use Fastlane's beta lane to package and upload. The workflow must specify Xcode and Ruby versions and cache dependencies to speed up the process. This ensures secure and automated packaging and deployment.

4b001d2e8440.name: How to solve the issue of multiple self-hosted runners competing for simulators when running iOS tests in GitHub Actions?

acceptedAnswer.text: When multiple runners run tests on the same machine sharing the same simulator, they interfere and cause failures. The solution is to create a separate simulator for each runner and specify the device name in the test script as the runner name plus the iOS version, for example, "RunnerA (18.4)". This allows each runner to use its own simulator independently, avoiding resource contention.

4b001d2e8440.name: How to use a Makefile in GitHub Actions to unify environment setup and dependency installation for an iOS project?

acceptedAnswer.text: A Makefile encapsulates common commands such as installing Ruby Gems (bundle), Mint tools, generating the project with XcodeGen, and installing dependencies with CocoaPods. Both CI and developers only need to run make setup to install dependencies and make install to generate the project, ensuring a consistent environment. This avoids manual installation errors, unifies versions, and supports restricting dependency installation paths within the project directory to reduce system pollution.

4b001d2e8440.name: What are the benefits of using Fastlane in the iOS CI/CD workflow? Why use it?

acceptedAnswer.text: Fastlane wraps complex xcodebuild commands and integrates with APIs like App Store Connect and Firebase, simplifying CI/CD script writing. You can run tests by calling scan, package by calling gym, and it automatically handles versioning, signing, and other details. This lets developers focus on designing workflows without writing tedious commands, improving maintainability. The demo project includes three lanes: run_all_tests, run_unit_tests, and beta.

4b001d2e8440.name: How to set up caching in GitHub Actions to speed up iOS project dependency installation?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can use actions/cache to cache Bundler (Ruby Gems), CocoaPods dependencies, and Mint tools by caching the ./vendor, ./Product/Pods, and ./mint directories respectively. Cache keys use the corresponding lockfile hashes such as Gemfile.lock, Podfile.lock, and Mintfile. This reduces repeated downloads and installations on each workflow run, improving execution speed. Even with self-hosted runners, GitHub Cloud Cache is still used.

4b001d2e8440.name: How to use SSH Agent in GitHub Actions to support Fastlane Match or private CocoaPods repositories?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, generate a passwordless SSH key, set the public key as a Deploy Key for the private repository, and store the private key as a Secret in the current repository. In the workflow, use the webfactory/ssh-agent action to start the SSH Agent and load the private key, adding github.com to known_hosts. This grants permission to git clone the private repo via SSH during workflow execution, supporting Fastlane Match for managing certificates or private CocoaPods repositories.

4273e57e7148.name: Why is using Google Apps Script Web App to integrate with GitHub Actions for building a packaging tool platform more suitable for teams without backend personnel?

acceptedAnswer.text: Google Apps Script Web App does not require setting up your own server, is free and stable, and can directly integrate with Google ecosystem services. Permission control can restrict usage to users within the organization, and the development and maintenance threshold is low. Compared to Slack integration which requires developing and maintaining backend services with higher costs, GAS is faster and lighter, making it more suitable for teams without backend personnel.

4273e57e7148.name: What are the unfriendly aspects of the GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch manual trigger form for non-engineering users?

acceptedAnswer.text: The workflow_dispatch form requires users to understand branches and whether fields are mandatory, and it cannot directly notify when packaging is complete or provide download links. This makes operation complex and progress hard to track for non-engineering users. Additionally, for permission control, users must be added to the Repo, which creates security risks.

4273e57e7148.name: How does the Google Apps Script Web App packaging tool platform achieve cross-team sharing while ensuring security control?

acceptedAnswer.text: The GAS Web App can be configured so that only users logged in with Google accounts within the same organization can access it, and scripts run under the user's identity to prevent unauthorized external access. The platform itself does not store a database but purely relays and exchanges API data, reducing security risks while facilitating cross-team use.

4273e57e7148.name: How to set up GitHub API Token in a Google Apps Script project and connect to real data?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, generate a Fine-grained personal access token on GitHub with read/write permissions for Actions, then insert the token into the githubToken variable in Credentials.gs of the GAS project. Next, replace GitHubStub with the GitHub class and refresh the Web App URL to connect to real GitHub data.

4273e57e7148.name: How does the Google Apps Script Web App packaging tool platform integrate with Firebase App Distribution to obtain download links?

acceptedAnswer.text: During the GitHub Actions packaging process, write the Job Run ID into the Release Note. The GAS Web App retrieves this ID via the GitHub API, then uses the Firebase API to query the version containing that ID and obtain download information. Firebase authorization is dynamically obtained using ScriptApp.getOAuthToken(), so no additional token setup is required.

4273e57e7148.name: What limitations of Google Apps Script Web App affect collaborative development by multiple people?

acceptedAnswer.text: GAS does not support simultaneous editing by multiple people. If multiple windows edit at the same time, the later save will overwrite the earlier changes. It is recommended that only one person edits in a single window at a time to avoid version conflicts. This limitation has little impact on Web App usage but needs special attention during development.

4273e57e7148.name: How to update the Google Apps Script Web App code to make changes take effect?

acceptedAnswer.text: After code changes, you need to click "Deploy" → "Manage deployments" → "Edit" → "Create new version" → "Deploy" → "Done" in the project.
